title: Token Standard
author: Fabian Vogelsteller <fabian@ethereum.org>, Vitalik Buterin <vitalik.buterin@ethereum.org>
one_line_summary: Standard interface for tokens with transfer and approval functions.
notes: Core foundational ERC; implements allowance-based approvals for delegated transfers.

title: Mixed-case checksum address encoding
author: Vitalik Buterin <vitalik.buterin@ethereum.org>, Alex Van de Sande <avsa@ethereum.org>
one_line_summary: Mixed-case checksum address encoding for error detection.
notes: Encoding/addressing standard; enables 15 check bits per address reducing typo errors.

title: URI Scheme with Metadata, Value and Bytecode
description: Format for encoding transactions into a URI
author: Alex Van de Sande (@alexvansande)
one_line_summary: URI scheme for encoding Ethereum transactions with metadata and bytecode.
notes: Withdrawn; superseded by EIP-681. Format for wallet transaction requests.

title: Ethereum Domain Name Service - Specification
author: Nick Johnson <arachnid@notdot.net>
one_line_summary: Ethereum Name Service registry, resolver, and registrar architecture.
notes: Foundational naming infrastructure; enables human-readable address resolution via hierarchical domain system.

title: Initial ENS Hash Registrar
author: Maurelian, Nick Johnson  <nick@ethereum.org>, Alex Van de Sande <avsa@ethereum.org>
one_line_summary: Initial ENS hash registrar implementing Vickrey auction for name allocation.
notes: Implements auction mechanism for .eth domain registration; deed-based collateral model.

title: Standard Interface Detection
author: Christian Reitwießner <chris@ethereum.org>, Nick Johnson <nick@ethereum.org>, Fabian Vogelsteller <fabian@lukso.network>, Jordi Baylina <jordi@baylina.cat>, Konrad Feldmeier <konrad.feldmeier@brainbot.com>, William Entriken <github.com@phor.net>
authors_normalized: Christian Reitwießner <chris@ethereum.org>;Nick Johnson <nick@ethereum.org>;Fabian Vogelsteller <fabian@lukso.network>;Jordi Baylina <jordi@baylina.cat>;Konrad Feldmeier <konrad.feldmeier@brainbot.com>;William Entriken <github.com@phor.net>
one_line_summary: Standard interface detection method for contracts using interface identifiers.
notes: Foundational meta-protocol; enables contracts to query/publish supported interfaces via XOR of function selectors.

title: Contract Ownership Standard
description: A standard interface for ownership of contracts
author: Nick Mudge (@mudgen), Dan Finlay <dan@danfinlay.com>
one_line_summary: Contract ownership standard with transfer mechanism and owner query.
notes: Generic access control for single-owner pattern; widely reused in contract governance.

title: ENS support for reverse resolution of Ethereum addresses
author: Nick Johnson <arachnid@notdot.net>
one_line_summary: ENS reverse resolution enabling human-readable names for Ethereum addresses.
notes: Extends ENS to support addr.reverse lookups; allows addresses to claim authoritative names.

title: Ethereum Smart Contract Packaging Standard
author: Piper Merriam (@pipermerriam), Tim Coulter (@tcoulter), Denis Erfurt (@mhhf), RJ Catalano (@VoR0220), Iuri Matias (@iurimatias)
authors_normalized: Piper Merriam (@pipermerriam);Tim Coulter (@tcoulter);Denis Erfurt (@mhhf);RJ Catalano (@VoR0220);Iuri Matias (@iurimatias)
one_line_summary: Ethereum smart contract packaging standard with release lockfile specification.
notes: Meta-standard for contract distribution; enables deterministic builds and bytecode verification.

title: Signed Data Standard
author: Martin Holst Swende (@holiman), Nick Johnson <arachnid@notdot.net>
one_line_summary: Standard for signed data format preventing replay attacks and signature ambiguity.
notes: Defines 0x19 prefix scheme with version bytes; supports personal_sign and structured data contexts.

title: ENS support for contract ABIs
author: Nick Johnson <nick@ethereum.org>
one_line_summary: ENS resolver profile for storing and retrieving contract ABIs onchain.
notes: Multiple encoding formats (JSON, zlib, CBOR, URI); enables interface discovery via ENS.

title: Token with transaction handling model
description: Token with transaction handling model designed to behave identical to native currency (ether)
author: Dexaran (@Dexaran) <dexaran@ethereumclassic.org>
one_line_summary: Token standard with transaction handler callback for safe contract deposits.
notes: Requires tokenReceived callback; eliminates stuck-token problem by making transfer atomic.

title: Ethereum purpose allocation for Deterministic Wallets
author: Nick Johnson (@arachnid), Micah Zoltu (@micahzoltu)
one_line_summary: BIP32/BIP43 purpose allocation scheme for deterministic Ethereum wallets.
notes: Defines m/43'/60'/EIP' derivation hierarchy; tailored to account-based model.

title: Ethereum hierarchy for deterministic wallets
author: Nick Johnson (@arachnid), Micah Zoltu (@micahzoltu)
one_line_summary: BIP32 hierarchical path for deterministic Ethereum wallets with multi-account support.
notes: Four-level path m/43'/60'/EIP'/wallet'; enables multiple identities per seed.

title: Storage of text records in ENS
description: Profiles for ENS resolvers to store arbitrary text key/value pairs.
author: Richard Moore (@ricmoo)
one_line_summary: ENS resolver profile for arbitrary key-value text record storage.
notes: Extends ENS with metadata; supports global keys (avatar, email) and service-specific namespaces.

title: URL Format for Transaction Requests
author: Daniel A. Nagy (@nagydani)
one_line_summary: URL format for payment requests and transactions using ethereum: scheme.
notes: Supersedes ERC-67. Uses scientific notation for amounts similar to Bitcoin payment URLs.

title: Non-Fungible Token Standard
author: William Entriken (@fulldecent), Dieter Shirley <dete@axiomzen.co>, Jacob Evans <jacob@dekz.net>, Nastassia Sachs <nastassia.sachs@protonmail.com>
one_line_summary: Standard interface for non-fungible tokens with transfer and ownership tracking.
notes: Core NFT standard. Optional metadata extension and enumeration extension. Inspired by ERC-20.

title: General data key/value store and execution
description: An interface for a smart contract based account with attachable data key/value store
author: Fabian Vogelsteller (@frozeman), Tyler Yasaka (@tyleryasaka)
one_line_summary: Generic data key/value store and execution interface for smart contract accounts.
notes: Two sub-standards: ERC725X (execution) and ERC725Y (data storage). Building blocks for smart contract accounts and upgradable metadata.

title: Token Standard
author: Jacques Dafflon <mail@0xjac.com>, Jordi Baylina <jordi@baylina.cat>, Thomas Shababi <tom@truelevel.io>
one_line_summary: Token standard with send/receive hooks, operators, and ERC-20 backward compatibility.
notes: Uses ERC-1820 registry for hooks. Operators allow delegated sending. Backward compatible with ERC-20.

title: Canary Standard
author: ligi <ligi@ligi.de>
one_line_summary: Standard interface for canary contracts to signal state (alive/dead).
notes: Stagnant status. Used for warrant canaries and status signaling. Minimal specification.

title: Pseudo-introspection Registry Contract
author: Jordi Baylina <jordi@baylina.cat>, Jacques Dafflon <jacques@dafflon.tech>
one_line_summary: Pseudo-introspection registry for interface implementation discovery.
notes: Superseded by ERC-1820. Allows any address to register interfaces. Compatible with ERC-165.

title: Token Exchange Standard
author: Kashish Khullar <kkhullar7@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Token exchange standard enabling cross-token swaps and payments.
notes: Extends ERC-20 with exchange services. Preserves exchanged tokens in contract rather than destroying them.

title: URI Format for Ethereum
description: A way of creating Ethereum URIs for various use-cases.
author: ligi (@ligi)
one_line_summary: URI format container for Ethereum use-cases with optional prefixes.
notes: Generalizes ERC-67 and ERC-681. Uses 'eth:' or 'ethereum:' scheme with extensible prefix system.

title: ABI specification for REVERT reason string
description: A proposal to extend the ABI specification to include typed errors in the REVERT reason string.
author: Federico Bond (@federicobond), Renan Rodrigues de Souza (@RenanSouza2)
one_line_summary: ABI specification for typed errors in REVERT reason strings.
notes: Draft status. Extends ABI to include error declarations with parameters. Enables error parsing by clients.

title: Simpler NFT standard with batching and native atomic swaps
author: Weiwu Zhang <a@colourful.land>, James Sangalli <j.l.sangalli@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Simplified NFT standard with batch transfers and atomic p2p swaps.
notes: Withdrawn status. Allows transferring multiple tokens in one transaction. Includes p2p atomic swap functionality.

title: DGCL Token
author: Dave Sag <davesag@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: ERC-20 compatible token for Delaware corporate shares with KYC/AML support.
notes: Complies with Delaware General Corporations Law. Requires identity verification. Tracks shareholders and transfer history.

title: DelegateProxy
author: Jorge Izquierdo <jorge@aragon.one>, Manuel Araoz <manuel@zeppelin.solutions>
one_line_summary: Standard interface for proxy contracts to signal implementation address.
notes: Stagnant status. Distinguishes forwarding vs upgradeable proxies. Enables source code verification on explorers.

title: Simple Staking Interface
author: Dean Eigenmann <dean@tokenate.io>, Jorge Izquierdo <jorge@aragon.one>
one_line_summary: Simple staking interface for token staking systems.
notes: Optional history functions for historical stake tracking. Common interface for staking protocols.

title: Token Validation
author: Brooklyn Zelenka (@expede), Tom Carchrae (@carchrae), Gleb Naumenko (@naumenkogs)
one_line_summary: Token validation protocol for authorization and compliance checks.
notes: Uses ERC-1066 status codes. Enables KYC/AML and transfer restrictions without per-token whitelists.

title: Mineable Token Standard
author: Jay Logelin <jlogelin@alumni.harvard.edu>, Infernal_toast <admin@0xbitcoin.org>, Michael Seiler <mgs33@cornell.edu>, Brandon Grill <bg2655@columbia.edu>
one_line_summary: Mineable token standard using Proof of Work for distribution.
notes: Includes delegated minting and merged mining extensions. Alternative to ICO distribution model.

title: Address metadata registry
author: Nick Johnson <nick@ethereum.org>
one_line_summary: Address metadata registry for identity and authorization data
notes: Uses ERC-165 for interface detection; enables flexible metadata provider architecture

title: Generalised authorisations
author: Nick Johnson <nick@ethereum.org>
one_line_summary: Generalized authorization/permission framework via metadata providers
notes: Built on ERC-926; replaces protocol-specific approvals and operator patterns

title: Composable Non-Fungible Token
description: Extends a ERC-721 to own other ERC-721 and ERC-20 tokens.
author: Matt Lockyer <mattdlockyer@gmail.com>, Nick Mudge <nick@perfectabstractions.com>, Jordan Schalm <jordan.schalm@gmail.com>, sebastian echeverry <sebastian.echeverry@robotouniverse.com>, Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
authors_normalized: Matt Lockyer <mattdlockyer@gmail.com>;Nick Mudge <nick@perfectabstractions.com>;Jordan Schalm <jordan.schalm@gmail.com>;sebastian echeverry <sebastian.echeverry@robotouniverse.com>;Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: Composable non-fungible tokens that own other ERC-721 and ERC-20 tokens
notes: Top-down and bottom-up composable patterns; enables hierarchical ownership structures

title: tokenURI Interoperability
description: Extends ERC-20 with an ERC-721-like tokenURI, and extends ERC-721 and ERC-1155 with interoperability
author: Tommy Nicholas (@tomasienrbc), Matt Russo (@mateosu), John Zettler (@JohnZettler), Matt Condon (@shrugs), Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
authors_normalized: Tommy Nicholas (@tomasienrbc);Matt Russo (@mateosu);John Zettler (@JohnZettler);Matt Condon (@shrugs);Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
one_line_summary: Adds tokenURI metadata to ERC-20; extends ERC-721 and ERC-1155 interoperability
notes: Interoperability metadata schema for differentiating token types via URI

title: Ethereum Lightweight Identity
author: Pelle Braendgaard <pelle.braendgaard@consensys.net>, Joel Torstensson <oed@consensys.net>
one_line_summary: Lightweight decentralized identity registry with delegates and attributes
notes: DID-compliant; free identity creation via Ethereum accounts; supports key rotation

title: Formalize IPFS hash into ENS(Ethereum Name Service) resolver
author: Phyrex Tsai <phyrex@portal.network>, Portal Network Team
one_line_summary: IPFS hash to ENS resolver mapping for decentralized content addressing
notes: Bridges IPFS content addressing with ENS human-readable names

title: Status Codes
author: Brooklyn Zelenka (@expede), Tom Carchrae (@carchrae), Gleb Naumenko (@naumenkogs)
one_line_summary: Standardized status codes for smart contracts analogous to HTTP codes
notes: Extends contract communication beyond binary success/failure; enables automatable state transitions

title: Gas relay for contract calls
author: Alex Van de Sande <avsa@ethereum.org>, Ricardo Guilherme Schmidt (@3esmit)
authors_normalized: Alex Van de Sande <avsa@ethereum.org>;Ricardo Guilherme Schmidt (@3esmit)
one_line_summary: Gas relay for contract calls via signed messages and token payments
notes: Enables gas abstraction via third-party relayers; supports arbitrary token for fee payment

title: Universal login / signup using ENS subdomains
author: Alex Van de Sande <avsa@ethereum.org>
one_line_summary: Universal login/signup using ENS and identity contracts with signed messages
notes: Account model for dapps; uses device-specific keys for signing; progressive key privilege levels

title: Recoverable Token
author: Bradley Leatherwood <bradleat@inkibra.com>
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension with token recovery, theft prevention, and chargeback support
notes: Dispute resolution for lost/stolen tokens; lost account and stolen account procedures

title: Standard Bounties
author: Mark Beylin <mark.beylin@consensys.net>, Kevin Owocki <kevin.owocki@consensys.net>, Ricardo Guilherme Schmidt (@3esmit)
one_line_summary: Standard interface for issuing bounties in any ERC-20 token or ETH
notes: Standardizes issuance, fulfillment, and acceptance workflows; tracks reputation across platforms

title: Revised Ethereum Smart Contract Packaging Standard
author: g. nicholas d’andrea (@gnidan), Piper Merriam (@pipermerriam), Nick Gheorghita (@njgheorghita), Danny Ryan (@djrtwo)
authors_normalized: g. nicholas d’andrea (@gnidan);Piper Merriam (@pipermerriam);Nick Gheorghita (@njgheorghita);Danny Ryan (@djrtwo)
one_line_summary: EthPM v2 smart contract packaging standard (withdrawn in favor of ERC-2678)
notes: Superseded by ERC-2678; defines package manifest for contract distribution

title: Standardised DAPP announcements
author: Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
one_line_summary: Standardized announcements framework for dApp service status updates
notes: Permissions-based posting; event tracking for service transitions and freezes

title: Extending ERC20 with token locking capability
author: nitika-goel <nitika@govblocks.io>
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension enabling time-locking of tokens within the contract
notes: Multiple reasons for locking; allows locked tokens to remain usable in ecosystem; avoids external escrow

title: Oracle Interface
author: Alan Lu (@cag)
one_line_summary: Standard interface for oracle data reporting (withdrawn)
notes: Push-based oracle consumer pattern; immutable results per ID; status: withdrawn

title: Multi Token Standard
author: Witek Radomski <witek@enjin.io>, Andrew Cooke <ac0dem0nk3y@gmail.com>, Philippe Castonguay (@phabc) <pc@horizongames.net>, James Therien <james@turing-complete.com>, Eric Binet <eric@enjin.io>, Ronan Sandford (@wighawag) <wighawag@gmail.com>
authors_normalized: Witek Radomski <witek@enjin.io>;Andrew Cooke <ac0dem0nk3y@gmail.com>;Philippe Castonguay (@phabc) <pc@horizongames.net>;James Therien <james@turing-complete.com>;Eric Binet <eric@enjin.io>;Ronan Sandford (@wighawag) <wighawag@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Multi-token standard supporting fungible, non-fungible, and semi-fungible tokens in a single contract.
notes: Supports both batch transfers and hybrid fungible/non-fungible token implementations in one contract.

title: Minimal Proxy Contract
author: Peter Murray (@yarrumretep), Nate Welch (@flygoing), Joe Messerman (@JAMesserman)
one_line_summary: Minimal proxy contract bytecode for cheap, immutable contract cloning.
notes: Uses delegatecall for low-gas proxy deployment; bytecode-level specification enables direct bytecode interrogation.

title: Wallet & shop standard for all tokens (erc20)
author: Jet Lim (@Nitro888)
one_line_summary: Wallet and shop standard for ERC-20 tokens enabling single-call payments.
notes: Proposes infrastructure for commerce using ERC-20 tokens; status is Stagnant.

title: Multi-class Token Standard
author: Albert Chon <achon@stanford.edu>
one_line_summary: Multi-class fungible token standard supporting multiple token classes within one contract.
notes: Allows tokens to be fungible within same class but non-fungible across classes; status is Stagnant.

title: Storage of DNS Records in ENS
description: A system to store and retrieve DNS records within the ENS contract.
author: Jim McDonald (@mcdee)
one_line_summary: Storage of DNS records within ENS resolver profile for domain name resolution.
notes: Extends ENS with DNS record storage; operates at record-set level rather than zone level.

title: Add chain id to mixed-case checksum address encoding
author: Juliano Rizzo (@juli)
one_line_summary: Chain-aware checksum encoding for Ethereum addresses to prevent cross-network fund loss.
notes: Extends EIP-55 with chain ID to distinguish addresses across different networks.

title: Voting Interface
description: A general interface for voting on-chain
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv), ERC-1202 Working Group <erc1202@googlegroups.com>
one_line_summary: Voting interface standard for on-chain governance with vote casting and result tracking.
notes: Defines voting API with support for single/multi-vote, weights, and delegation; leaves proposals out of scope.

title: ERC-1203 Multi-Class Token Standard (ERC-20 Extension)
author: Jeff Huang <jeffishjeff@gmail.com>, Min Zu <crawlregister@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Multi-class token standard extending ERC-20 with support for multiple fungible token classes.
notes: Hybrid of ERC-20 and ERC-721 with optional class conversion; maintains ERC-20 backwards compatibility.

title: DAuth Access Delegation Standard
author: Xiaoyu Wang (@wxygeek), Bicong Wang (@Wangbicong)
one_line_summary: DAuth access delegation standard for authorizing contracts to access user data and assets.
notes: Distributed authorization without access tokens; inspired by OAuth but decentralized on-chain.

title: Membership Verification Token (MVT)
author: Chaitanya Potti (@chaitanyapotti), Partha Bhattacharya (@pb25193)
one_line_summary: Membership Verification Token standard for non-transferable, revocable membership proof.
notes: Non-transferable tokens with attributes; enables permissioned governance and compliance applications.

title: Standard Signature Validation Method for Contracts
description: Standard way to verify a signature when the account is a smart contract
author: Francisco Giordano (@frangio), Matt Condon (@shrugs), Philippe Castonguay (@PhABC), Amir Bandeali (@abandeali1), Jorge Izquierdo (@izqui), Bertrand Masius (@catageek)
authors_normalized: Francisco Giordano (@frangio);Matt Condon (@shrugs);Philippe Castonguay (@PhABC);Amir Bandeali (@abandeali1);Jorge Izquierdo (@izqui);Bertrand Masius (@catageek)
one_line_summary: Standard signature validation method for smart contracts to verify signed messages.
notes: Enables contract-based signature verification via isValidSignature() function; context-dependent validation.

title: Smart Contract Package Registry Interface
author: Piper Merriam <piper@ethereum.org>, Christopher Gewecke <christophergewecke@gmail.com>, g. nicholas d'andrea <nick.dandrea@consensys.net>, Nick Gheorghita (@njgheorghita)
authors_normalized: Piper Merriam <piper@ethereum.org>;Christopher Gewecke <christophergewecke@gmail.com>;g. nicholas d'andrea <nick.dandrea@consensys.net>;Nick Gheorghita (@njgheorghita)
one_line_summary: Smart contract package registry interface for publishing and retrieving versioned contract packages.
notes: Read/write API for package registries with semver versioning support; companion to EIP-1123.

title: WalletConnect URI Format
description: Define URI format for initiating connections between applications and wallets
author: ligi (@ligi), Pedro Gomes (@pedrouid)
one_line_summary: WalletConnect URI format specification for QR code-based wallet connection initiation.
notes: Defines URI syntax for v1.0 and v2.0 protocols; enables app-to-wallet communication via QR/link.

title: Subscriptions on the blockchain
author: Kevin Owocki <kevin@gitcoin.co>, Andrew Redden <andrew@blockcrushr.com>, Scott Burke <scott@blockcrushr.com>, Kevin Seagraves <k.s.seagraves@gmail.com>, Luka Kacil <luka.kacil@gmail.com>, Štefan Šimec <stefan.simec@gmail.com>, Piotr Kosiński (@kosecki123), ankit raj <tradeninja7@gmail.com>, John Griffin <john@atchai.com>, Nathan Creswell <nathantr@gmail.com>
authors_normalized: Kevin Owocki <kevin@gitcoin.co>;Andrew Redden <andrew@blockcrushr.com>;Scott Burke <scott@blockcrushr.com>;Kevin Seagraves <k.s.seagraves@gmail.com>;Luka Kacil <luka.kacil@gmail.com>;Štefan Šimec <stefan.simec@gmail.com>;Piotr Kosiński (@kosecki123);ankit raj <tradeninja7@gmail.com>;John Griffin <john@atchai.com>;Nathan Creswell <nathantr@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Subscription payment standard enabling recurring blockchain-based subscription transactions.
notes: Uses signed hashes for replay-able transactions; enables SaaS business models on-chain; status is Stagnant.

title: Payable Token
author: Vittorio Minacori (@vittominacori)
one_line_summary: Payable token standard enabling callbacks after transfer or approval in single transaction.
notes: Inspired by ERC-721 receiver pattern; maintains ERC-20 backwards compatibility with added callback support.

title: Attestation management contract
author: Weiwu Zhang <a@colourful.land>, James Sangalli <j.l.sangalli@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Attestation management contract for issuing, validating, and revoking cryptographic attestations.
notes: Part of attestation ecosystem (1386, 1387, 1388). Enables off-chain signed attestations to be verified on-chain with revocation via bloom filters.

title: Merkle Tree Attestations with Privacy enabled
author: Weiwu Zhang <a@colourful.land>, James Sangalli <j.l.sangalli@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Merkle tree attestations with privacy support for verifying claims without revealing full attestation.
notes: Privacy-preserving attestation standard allowing partial revelation via Merkle trees; complements ERC-1386.

title: Attestation Issuers Management List
author: Weiwu Zhang <a@colourful.land>, James Sangalli <j.l.sangalli@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Registry for managing lists of trusted attestation issuers with specific capabilities and expiry.
notes: Curated registry allowing smart contracts to trust attestation issuers; part of attestation ecosystem.

title: Poll Standard
author: Chaitanya Potti (@chaitanyapotti), Partha Bhattacharya (@pb25193)
one_line_summary: Modular poll standard supporting flexible voting schemes with membership qualification.
notes: Modularized governance enabling diverse voting types (token-proportional, quadratic, delegated) via permissioning.

title: dApp Components (avatar) & Universal Wallet
topic_other: dApp component registry (avatar/badge store) and universal wallet
author: Jet Lim (@Nitro888)
one_line_summary: Avatar store and universal wallet for reusable dApp components across Ethereum ecosystem.
notes: Proposes component-based architecture for avatars/badges and unified ERC-20 wallet; falls outside standard taxonomies.

title: Localized Messaging with Signal-to-Text
author: Brooklyn Zelenka (@expede), Jennifer Cooper (@jenncoop)
one_line_summary: Decentralized localization system converting machine codes to human-readable text in any language.
notes: Registry and proxy pattern for multilingual message translation; integrates with ERC-1066 status codes.

title: RTA-Controlled Security Token
description: Security tokens where the Registered Transfer Agent has exclusive control over transfers for regulatory compliance
author: Howard Marks (@howardmarks) <howard@startengine.com>, Devender Gollapally (@devender-startengine) <devender@startengine.com>, Joe Mathews (@se-joe) <joe@startengine.com>, Jordan Jahja (@jordan-jahja) <jordan.jahja@startengine.com>, John Shiple (@johnshiple), David Zhang (@david-colab) <david@startengine.com>
authors_normalized: Howard Marks (@howardmarks) <howard@startengine.com>;Devender Gollapally (@devender-startengine) <devender@startengine.com>;Joe Mathews (@se-joe) <joe@startengine.com>;Jordan Jahja (@jordan-jahja) <jordan.jahja@startengine.com>;John Shiple (@johnshiple);David Zhang (@david-colab) <david@startengine.com>
one_line_summary: RTA-controlled security token with exclusive transfer authority and KYC/AML compliance mechanisms.
notes: Regulated securities with Registered Transfer Agent exclusive control; disables standard ERC-20 transfer/approve; supports recovery and court orders.

title: Base Security Token
author: Maxim Kupriianov <mk@atlant.io>, Julian Svirsky <js@atlant.io>
one_line_summary: Base security token with compliance checking functions and legal document attachment.
notes: Minimal security token extension adding transfer/mint/burn checks and document references; foundational rather than prescriptive.

title: Digital Identity Aggregator
author: Anurag Angara <anurag.angara@gmail.com>, Andy Chorlian <andychorlian@gmail.com>, Shane Hampton <shanehampton1@gmail.com>, Noah Zinsmeister <noahwz@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Digital identity aggregator protocol supporting multiple associated addresses and resolver contracts.
notes: Un-opinionated identity registry enabling complex identity applications via associated addresses and resolvers; DID-compatible.

title: Human Cost Accounting Standard (Like Gas but for humans)
topic_other: human capital accounting tokens based on BDI model
author: Iamnot Chris (@cohabo)
one_line_summary: Human capital accounting standard tracking experience, reputation, and talents via BDI agent model.
notes: Speculative proposal applying belief-desire-intention framework to token-based human capital; incomplete implementation status.

title: Upgradable Smart Contract
author: Kaidong Wu <wukd94@pku.edu.cn>, Chuqiao Ren <cr025@bucknell.edu>, Ruthia He <rujiahe@gmail.com>, Yun Ma <mayun@pku.edu.cn>, Xuanzhe Liu <liuxuanzhe@pku.edu.cn>
authors_normalized: Kaidong Wu <wukd94@pku.edu.cn>;Chuqiao Ren <cr025@bucknell.edu>;Ruthia He <rujiahe@gmail.com>;Yun Ma <mayun@pku.edu.cn>;Xuanzhe Liu <liuxuanzhe@pku.edu.cn>
one_line_summary: Upgradable smart contract pattern with separate handler/data contracts and optional voting mechanism.
notes: Contract architecture for bug fixes and improvements via handler swapping while preserving data; includes governance component.

title: Standard for Insurance Policies as ERC-721 Non Fungible Tokens
author: Christoph Mussenbrock (@christoph2806)
one_line_summary: Insurance policy standard based on ERC-721 with policy-specific metadata structure.
notes: Specialized NFT for insurance contracts with carrier, risk, and premium metadata; applies 721 to regulated assets.

title: Transparent Contract Standard
author: Nick Mudge <nick@perfectabstractions.com>
one_line_summary: Transparent contract standard enabling atomic function upgrades with event logging.
notes: Superseded by ERC-2535 (Diamond); pioneered modular upgradability via delegatecall with documented change history.

title: contenthash field for ENS
author: Dean Eigenmann <dean@ens.domains>, Nick Johnson <nick@ens.domains>
one_line_summary: ENS contenthash field for mapping names to distributed content via multicodec format.
notes: Deprecates ENS 'content' and 'multihash' fields; supports IPFS, Swarm, and other protocols via machine-readable multicodec.

title: Non-wallet usage of keys derived from BIP-32 trees
description: A derivation path structure for BIP32 trees to generate key pairs not meant to hold crypto assets.
author: Michele Balistreri (@bitgamma)
one_line_summary: BIP32 key derivation path for non-wallet applications (messaging, signing, etc.).
notes: Hierarchical key derivation using m/43'/60'/1581' prefix; enables separate identity keys independent from wallet keys.

title: Address and ERC20-compliant transfer rules

notes: Enables contracts to fetch external data transparently via standardized gateway interface and callback mechanism. Final status.

title: Poster
description: A ridiculously simple general purpose social media smart contract.
author: Auryn Macmillan (@auryn-macmillan)
one_line_summary: Minimal smart contract for decentralized social media enabling posts to IPFS.
notes: Ridiculously simple contract emitting content and tag as events; builds application state off-chain via indexer.

title: A Vanilla Non-Fungible Token Standard
description: NFTs for representing abstract ownership
author: Simon Tian (@simontianx)
one_line_summary: NFT standard for abstract ownership such as rights, memberships, and coupons.
notes: Simplifies ERC-721 by removing URI-related fields; designed for representing abstract rights and utilities.

title: Chain-specific addresses
description: Prepending chain-specific addresses with a human-readable chain identifier
author: Lukas Schor (@lukasschor), Richard Meissner (@rmeissner), Pedro Gomes (@pedrouid), ligi <ligi@ligi.de>
one_line_summary: Chain-specific address format using human-readable prefix to prevent fund loss in multichain.
notes: Addresses ambiguity in EVM multichain by prepending chain shortName to addresses (e.g. eth:0x...).

title: Compressed Integers
description: Using lossy compression on uint256 to improve gas costs, ideally by a factor up to 4x.
author: Soham Zemse (@zemse)
one_line_summary: Lossy compression of uint256 into smaller types for storage cost reduction up to 4x.
notes: Divides compressed value into significant bits and shift amount; includes two decompression strategies.

title: Account Abstraction Using Alt Mempool
description: Account abstraction without consensus-layer protocol changes, instead relying on higher-layer infrastructure.
author: Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin), Yoav Weiss (@yoavw), Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh), Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn), Alex Forshtat (@forshtat), Kristof Gazso (@kristofgazso), Tjaden Hess (@tjade273)
authors_normalized: Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin);Yoav Weiss (@yoavw);Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh);Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn);Alex Forshtat (@forshtat);Kristof Gazso (@kristofgazso);Tjaden Hess (@tjade273)
one_line_summary: Account abstraction via UserOperations and alt mempool without consensus-layer changes.
notes: Defines UserOperation, EntryPoint, bundlers, paymasters, and factories for smart contract-based account validation. Final status.

title: Ordered NFT Batch Standard
description: The ordering information of multiple NFTs is retained and managed
author: Simon Tian (@simontianx)
one_line_summary: NFT batch standard preserving ordering for logographic characters and emojis.
notes: Extends ERC-3754 to retain ordering of NFT sequences, enabling meaning transmission via token IDs.

title: Interface for Staked Tokens in NFTs
description: This interface enables access to publicly viewable staking data of an NFT.
author: Rex Creed (@aug2uag), Dane Scarborough <dane@nftapps.us>
one_line_summary: Interface for querying staked tokens bound to NFTs.
notes: Simple interface enabling wallets to display staked token amounts for NFTs, supporting escrow and reward use cases.

title: Sign-In with Ethereum
description: Off-chain authentication for Ethereum accounts to establish sessions.
author: Wayne Chang (@wyc), Gregory Rocco (@obstropolos), Brantly Millegan (@brantlymillegan), Nick Johnson (@Arachnid), Oliver Terbu (@awoie)
authors_normalized: Wayne Chang (@wyc);Gregory Rocco (@obstropolos);Brantly Millegan (@brantlymillegan);Nick Johnson (@Arachnid);Oliver Terbu (@awoie)
one_line_summary: Standard for Ethereum account authentication via signed messages for off-chain sessions.
notes: SIWE protocol enabling self-custodial login alternative to centralized IdPs, with EOA and contract account support. Final status.

title: Micropayments for NFTs and Multi Tokens
description: An interface for tip tokens that allows tipping to holders of NFTs and multi tokens
author: Jules Lai (@julesl23)
one_line_summary: Micropayment standard for tipping NFT and multi-token holders via tip tokens.
notes: Interface for tip tokens exchanged for ERC-20, enabling batched tipping to NFT holders with gas optimization.

title: EIP-721 Consumable Extension
description: Interface extension for EIP-721 consumer role
author: Daniel Ivanov (@Daniel-K-Ivanov), George Spasov (@Perseverance)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 consumer role extension separating utility rights from ownership.
notes: Adds consumer address field and changeConsumer method; consumer cannot own/approve/transfer. Final status.

title: Described Transactions
description: A technique for contracts to provide a human-readable description of a transaction's side-effects.
author: Richard Moore (@ricmoo), Nick Johnson (@arachnid)
one_line_summary: Virtual functions and human-readable descriptions for Ethereum transactions.
notes: Solves the UX problem of opaque transaction signing by allowing contracts to generate paired descriptions and bytecode.

title: Permit for ERC-721 NFTs
description: ERC-712-singed approvals for ERC-721 NFTs
author: Simon Fremaux (@dievardump), William Schwab (@wschwab)
one_line_summary: ERC-712-signed approvals for ERC-721 NFTs enabling gasless permit flows.
notes: Extends ERC-2612 permit pattern to NFTs; nonce increments per NFT transfer.

title: Non-Fungible Tokens Tied to Physical Assets
description: Interface for non-fungible tokens representing physical assets that can generate or recover their own accounts and obey users.
author: Javier Arcenegui (@Hardblock-IMSE-CNM), Rosario Arjona (@RosarioArjona), Roberto Román <roman@imse-cnm.csic.es>, Iluminada Baturone (@lumi2018)
one_line_summary: NFTs representing physical assets with mutual authentication and secure communication.
notes: Extends ERC-721 with asset address, user assignment, and cryptographic key agreement protocols.

title: 721/20-compatible transfer
description: Recommends a simple extension to make NFTs compatible with apps and contracts that handle fungibles.
author: Ross Campbell (@z0r0z)
one_line_summary: Adds ERC-20 compatible transfer() function to ERC-721 NFTs.
notes: Simple compatibility bridge; allows NFTs to work with ERC-20-aware contracts and wallets.

title: Safer ERC-20
description: Extending ERC-20 with ERC165 and adding safeTransfer (like ERC-721 and ERC-1155)
author: William Schwab (@wschwab)
one_line_summary: Extends ERC-20 with ERC-165 interface detection and safeTransfer callbacks.
notes: Brings NFT safety patterns (safeTransfer/safeTransferFrom) and EIP-165 to ERC-20 tokens.

title: QR Code transmission protocol for wallets
description: QR Code data transmission protocol between wallets and offline signers.
author: Aaron Chen (@aaronisme), Sora Lee (@soralit), ligi (@ligi), Dan Miller (@danjm), AndreasGassmann (@andreasgassmann), xardass (@xardass), Lixin Liu (@BitcoinLixin)
one_line_summary: QR code data transmission protocol between offline signers and watch-only wallets.
notes: Uses UR encoding and animated QR codes; supports key distribution, transactions, and EIP-712 typed data.

title: Wrapped Deposits
description: A singleton contract for managing asset deposits.
author: Justice Hudson (@jchancehud)
one_line_summary: Singleton contract for unified asset deposits across ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1155, and Ether.
notes: Primary purpose is token management/deposit UX; uses ERC-20/721/1155 as infrastructure.

title: Tokenized Vaults
description: Tokenized Vaults with a single underlying EIP-20 token.
author: Joey Santoro (@joeysantoro), t11s (@transmissions11), Jet Jadeja (@JetJadeja), Alberto Cuesta Cañada (@alcueca), Señor Doggo (@fubuloubu)
authors_normalized: Joey Santoro (@joeysantoro);t11s (@transmissions11);Jet Jadeja (@JetJadeja);Alberto Cuesta Cañada (@alcueca);Señor Doggo (@fubuloubu)
one_line_summary: Standard API for tokenized vaults representing shares of underlying ERC-20 tokens.
notes: Core DeFi primitive; defines deposit/withdraw/redeem semantics for yield-bearing tokens.

title: Non-Tradable Tokens Standard
description: A standard interface for non-tradable tokens, aka badges or souldbound NFTs.
author: Omar Aflak (@omaraflak), Pol-Malo Le Bris, Marvin Martin (@MarvinMartin24)
one_line_summary: Non-tradable tokens (soulbound badges) for diplomas, credentials, and personal achievements.
notes: Explicitly non-transferable; represents inherently personal possessions; includes enumerable, delegate, and consensus extensions.

title: Multi-Fractional Non-Fungible Tokens
description: Fractionalize multiple NFTs using a single contract
author: David Kim (@powerstream3604)
one_line_summary: Fractionalize multiple NFTs into fungible shares within a single contract.
notes: Each token ID represents a distinct fractionalized NFT; improves on separate ERC-20 deployment pattern.

title: Non-Fungible Token Ownership Designation Standard
description: A standardized interface for designating ownership of an NFT
author: David Buckman (@davidbuckman), Isaac Buckman (@isaacbuckman)
one_line_summary: Designate ownership of NFTs held in escrow while preserving original authenticity.
notes: Enables rental, collateral, fractional ownership without losing authenticity; creates directed acyclic graph of NFTs.

title: Web3 URL to EVM Call Message Translation
description: A translation of an HTTP-style Web3 URL to an EVM call message
author: Qi Zhou (@qizhou), Chao Pi (@pichaoqkc), Sam Wilson (@SamWilsn)
one_line_summary: Translate HTTP-style Web3 URLs to EVM call messages for read-only contract execution.
notes: Enables direct Web3 access and on-chain web content rendering; supports ENS resolution and method auto-detection.

title: Common Interfaces for DAOs
description: An API for decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs).
author: Joshua Tan (@thelastjosh), Isaac Patka (@ipatka), Ido Gershtein <ido@daostack.io>, Eyal Eithcowich <eyal@deepdao.io>, Michael Zargham (@mzargham), Sam Furter (@nivida)
authors_normalized: Joshua Tan (@thelastjosh);Isaac Patka (@ipatka);Ido Gershtein <ido@daostack.io>;Eyal Eithcowich <eyal@deepdao.io>;Michael Zargham (@mzargham);Sam Furter (@nivida)
one_line_summary: API standard for DAOs relating on-chain and off-chain membership and proposals.
notes: Defines daoURI and JSON-LD schemas for members, proposals, activity log, and governance; enables DAO discoverability.

title: Hierarchical Domains
description: Extremely generic name resolution
author: Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
one_line_summary: Generic hierarchical name resolution with arbitrary access control, similar to ENS.
notes: All domains (including TLDs and root) are contracts; simpler than ENS with standardized access control.

title: Composable SVG NFT
description: Compose an SVG NFT by concatenating the SVG with the rendered SVG of another NFT.
author: Andrew B Coathup (@abcoathup), Alex (@AlexPartyPanda), Damian Martinelli (@damianmarti), blockdev (@0xbok), Austin Griffith (@austintgriffith)
one_line_summary: Compose SVG NFTs by concatenating rendered SVG strings from other NFTs.
notes: Enables on-chain SVG composability; used by Loogies; concatenation order determines rendering (painters model).

title: Subscription NFTs and Multi Tokens
description: An interface for subscription tokens that gives holders subscriptions to NFTs and multi tokens
author: Jules Lai (@julesl23)
one_line_summary: Subscription tokens giving holders time-limited access to NFTs/semi-fungibles.
notes: Bridges subscription mechanics with NFT ownership; subscription balance decreases linearly over time.

title: Proxy Ownership Register
description: A proxy ownership register allowing trustless proof of ownership between Ethereum addresses, with delegated asset delivery
author: Omnus Sunmo (@omnus)
one_line_summary: Register for proxy ownership linking cold wallets to active proxy addresses.
notes: Enables proving asset ownership via proxy without direct interaction from cold wallet.

title: EIP-721 Metadata Update Extension
description: Add a MetadataUpdate event to EIP-721.
author: Anders (@0xanders), Lance (@LanceSnow), Shrug <shrug@emojidao.org>, Nathan <nathan.gang@gemini.com>
one_line_summary: Standard MetadataUpdate and BatchMetadataUpdate events for 721/1155 tokens.
notes: Lightweight extension enabling third-party platforms to timely update NFT metadata.

title: Rental NFT, an Extension of EIP-721
description: Add a time-limited role with restricted permissions to EIP-721 tokens.
author: Anders (@0xanders), Lance (@LanceSnow), Shrug <shrug@emojidao.org>
one_line_summary: Dual owner/user roles for 721 NFTs with time-limited rental periods.
notes: Enables NFT rentals with automatic expiry; user cannot transfer.

title: Royalty Bearing NFTs
description: Extension of ERC-721 to correctly define, process, and pay (hierarchical) onchain NFT royalties.
author: Andreas Freund (@Therecanbeonlyone1969)
one_line_summary: Hierarchical royalty distribution system for 721 NFTs via Royalty Accounts.
notes: Supports multi-level royalty trees; handles complex inheritance and payout logic.

title: Generic Token Upgrade Standard
description: Create a standard interface for upgrading ERC20 token contracts.
author: John Peterson (@John-peterson-coinbase), Roberto Bayardo (@roberto-bayardo), David Núñez (@cygnusv)
one_line_summary: Standard interface for upgrading ERC-20 tokens with configurable ratios.
notes: Supports both upgrade and optional downgrade; reusable across different token pairs.

title: Contract with Exactly One Non-fungible Token
description: An ERC-721 compatible single-token NFT
author: Víctor Muñoz (@victormunoz), Josep Lluis de la Rosa (@peplluis7), Andres El-Fakdi (@Bluezfish)
authors_normalized: Víctor Muñoz (@victormunoz);Josep Lluis de la Rosa (@peplluis7);Andres El-Fakdi (@Bluezfish)
one_line_summary: Single NFT per contract, uniquely identifying token by contract address.
notes: Restricts 721 to exactly one token; enables contract as autonomous agent with budget.

title: Entangled Tokens
description: ERC-721 extension with two tokens minted that are tied together
author: Víctor Muñoz (@victormunoz), Josep Lluis de la Rosa (@peplluis7), Easy Innova (@easyinnova)
authors_normalized: Víctor Muñoz (@victormunoz);Josep Lluis de la Rosa (@peplluis7);Easy Innova (@easyinnova)
one_line_summary: Two entangled NFTs per contract for authenticity, royalties, and shared wallet.
notes: Enables artist to prove creation; only one transfer possible per pair.

title: Vendor Metadata Extension for NFTs
description: Add a new field to NFT metadata to store vendor specific data
author: Ignacio Mazzara (@nachomazzara)
one_line_summary: Vendor-specific metadata namespaces extension for 721/1155 NFTs.
notes: Allows project-specific data (e.g. 3D models) in standard JSON metadata without breaking compatibility.

title: Name-Owned Account
description: Name-Owned Account for Social Identity
author: Shu Dong (@dongshu2013), Qi Zhou (@qizhou), Zihao Chen (@zihaoccc)
one_line_summary: Smart contract account linked to ENS name node, owned by name owner.
notes: Enables one-to-one mapping from human-readable name to programmable account.

title: Account-bound Tokens
description: An interface for non-transferrable NFTs binding to an Ethereum account like a legendary World of Warcraft item binds to a character.
author: Tim Daubenschütz (@TimDaub)
one_line_summary: Non-transferrable account-bound tokens (soulbound) with EIP-712 signature verification.
notes: Tokens bind to single account; no transfer interface; can unequip voluntarily.

title: Ratings
description: An interface for assigning and managing numerical ratings
author: Daniel Tedesco (@dtedesco1)
one_line_summary: Standardized interface for assigning and managing int8 numerical ratings.
notes: Operator-controlled ratings; supports voting weight, experience points, loyalty tracking.

title: Held token interface
description: Interface to query ownership and balance of held tokens
author: Devin Conley (@devinaconley)
one_line_summary: Interface to query ownership and balance of tokens held by contracts.
notes: Separates functional owner from technical owner; enables governance/membership verification despite token deposits.

title: Zodiac Modular Accounts
description: Composable interoperable programmable accounts
author: Auryn Macmillan (@auryn-macmillan), Kei Kreutler (@keikreutler)
one_line_summary: Composable interfaces for modular accounts: Avatars, Modules, Modifiers, Guards.
notes: Decouples authentication from execution; enables flexible multi-control and progressive decentralization.

title: Rental NFT, NFT User Extension
description: Add a user role with restricted permissions to ERC-1155 tokens
author: Lance (@LanceSnow), Anders (@0xanders), Shrug <shrug@emojidao.org>
one_line_summary: User role extension for ERC-1155 enabling time-bound rental/usage rights.
notes: Parallel to 4907 for semi-fungibles; user cannot transfer.

title: Time NFT, ERC-721 Time Extension
description: Add start time and end time to ERC-721 tokens.
author: Anders (@0xanders), Lance (@LanceSnow), Shrug <shrug@emojidao.org>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension adding start and end time properties to NFT tokens.
notes: Optional composable extension supports splitting/merging time-based NFTs.

title: ERC-721 Nonce Extension
description: Add a `nonce` function to ERC-721.
author: Anders (@0xanders), Lance (@LanceSnow), Shrug <shrug@emojidao.org>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension adding nonce to prevent marketplace order replay attacks.
notes: Nonce increments on token transfer to invalidate stale orders.

title: Filesystem-like Interface for Contracts
description: An interface to provide access to binary objects similar to filesystems.
author: Qi Zhou (@qizhou)
one_line_summary: Filesystem-like interface for reading and writing binary objects in contracts.
notes: Supports chunked I/O for large files; enables Web3 resource discovery via EIP-4804.

title: Shareable Non-Fungible Token
description: An interface for creating value-holding tokens shareable by multiple owners
author: Jarno Marttila (@yaruno), Martin Moravek (@mmartinmo)
one_line_summary: Non-fungible shareable tokens that can be copied and shared while retaining original.
notes: Shareable non-transferable variant enables reputation/contribution tracking via share events.

title: Interactive NFTs with Modular Environments
description: Action messaging and discovery protocol for interactions on and between NFTs
author: Alexi (@alexi)
one_line_summary: Action messaging protocol for interactive NFTs with optional modular state environments.
notes: Enables NFT-to-NFT interaction; state contracts broker execution; validation via action hash.

title: Lockable Non-Fungible Tokens
description: Lockable EIP-721 tokens
author: Tyler (@radiocaca), Alex (@gojazdev), John (@sfumato00)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension with locking mechanism to prevent transfer while maintaining custody.
notes: NFTs remain in owner wallet while locked; enables NFTFi without escrow risk.

title: URL Format for Ethereum Network Switching
description: A way of representing various network configurations as URLs.
author: Luc van Kampen (@lucemans), Jakob Helgesson (@svemat01), Joshua Hendrix (@thejoshuahendrix)
one_line_summary: URL format for representing Ethereum network configurations for wallet integration.
notes: Extension of EIP-831; allows deep linking and QR codes for network addition.

title: Principal Token
description: Principal tokens (zero-coupon tokens) are redeemable for a single underlying EIP-20 token at a future timestamp.
author: Julian Traversa (@JTraversa), Robert Robbins (@robrobbins), Alberto Cuesta Cañada (@alcueca)
one_line_summary: Principal tokens (zero-coupon) redeemable for underlying ERC-20 at maturity.
notes: Standardizes yield tokenization and fixed-rate money markets; extends ERC-20.

title: Soulbound Badge
description: A token that is attached to a "soul" at mint time and cannot be transferred after that.
author: Micah Zoltu (@MicahZoltu)
one_line_summary: Soulbound badge token bound immutably to another NFT at mint time.
notes: Non-transferable; requires content-addressable URIs for metadata; bound to NFT personas.

title: SY Token
description: Interface for wrapped yield-bearing tokens.
author: Vu Nguyen (@mrenoon), Long Vuong (@UncleGrandpa925), Anton Buenavista (@ayobuenavista)
one_line_summary: Standardized Yield token interface for composable wrapped yield-bearing assets.
notes: More flexible than ERC-4626; supports multiple input/output tokens and reward handling.

title: SAFE Authentication For ENS
description: Using ENS Text Records to facilitate safer and more convenient signing operations.
author: Wilkins Chung (@wwhchung) <wilkins@manifold.xyz>, Jalil Wahdatehagh (@jwahdatehagh), Cry (@crydoteth), Sillytuna (@sillytuna), Cyberpnk (@CyberpnkWin)
one_line_summary: ENS-based authentication linking secondary wallets to prove primary wallet control.
notes: Uses ENS text records for safer signing via secondary wallets; one-to-many auth relationship.

title: Remote Procedure Call Provider Lists
description: Format for lists of RPC providers for Ethereum-like chains.
author: Sam Wilson (@SamWilsn)
one_line_summary: JSON schema format for lists of RPC providers with chain compatibility information.
notes: Supports semantic versioning; publishable via ENS or HTTPS; enables provider switching.

title: Slippage Protection for Tokenized Vault
description: An extension of EIP-4626 supporting improved EOA interactions.
author: Hadrien Croubois (@amxx)
one_line_summary: ERC-4626 vault extension adding slippage protection for EOA interactions.
notes: Overloaded deposit/mint/withdraw/redeem functions with min/max output bounds.

title: Cross-Chain Execution
description: Defines an interface that supports execution across EVM networks.
author: Brendan Asselstine (@asselstine), Pierrick Turelier (@PierrickGT), Chris Whinfrey (@cwhinfrey)
one_line_summary: Cross-chain execution interface for dispatching and executing messages across EVM chains.
notes: MessageDispatcher (origin) and MessageExecutor (destination); unique messageIds prevent replay.

title: Client Script URI for Token Contracts
description: Add a scriptURI to point to an executable script associated with the functionality of the token.
author: James (@JamesSmartCell), Weiwu (@weiwu-zhang)
one_line_summary: Add scriptURI to token contracts pointing to associated client-side executable scripts.
notes: Supports multiple URIs for redundancy; compatible with existing token standards.

title: NFT Future Rewards (nFR)
description: A multigenerational reward mechanism that rewards‌ all ‌owners of non-fungible tokens (NFT).
author: Yale ReiSoleil (@longnshort), dRadiant (@dRadiant), D Wang, PhD <david@iob.fi>
one_line_summary: Future rewards framework for NFT owners, sharing profits across historical holders.
notes: Extends ERC-721 with a multigenerational profit-sharing mechanism via geometric sequences. Uses sliding window to distribute rewards to previous owners when profitable sales occur.

title: NFT Updatable Metadata Extension
description: An interface extension for ERC-721/ERC-1155 controlled metadata updates
author: Christophe Le Bars (@clbrge)
one_line_summary: Controlled NFT metadata updates via recipes and JSONata transformations.
notes: Extends ERC-721/ERC-1155 to allow deterministic, on-chain-traceable metadata updates. Uses JSON transformation engine (JSONata) for controlled evolution of NFT properties.

title: Extend EIP-1155 with rentable usage rights
description: Separate ownership and usage rights of EIP-1155 to allow users to use NFTs for an allotted time and return them to owners after expiration.
author: DerivStudio (@DerivStudio)
one_line_summary: Rental extension for ERC-1155 separating usage rights from ownership.
notes: Extends ERC-1155 to enable rentals with expirable usage rights. Supports subletting and renewal; ownership transfers independent of active leases.

title: Account Abstraction via Endorsed Operations
description: An account abstraction proposal that avoids protocol changes while maintaining compatibility with existing smart contract wallets.
author: Agustín Aguilar (@agusx1211), Philippe Castonguay (@phabc), Michael Standen (@ScreamingHawk)
one_line_summary: Account abstraction via endorsed operations, using off-chain endorser validation.
notes: Proposes AA without protocol changes using 'endorser' smart contracts to validate operations. Endorsers signal readiness and dependencies; bundlers evaluate and include transactions in mempool.

title: Minimal Soulbound NFTs
description: Minimal interface for soulbinding EIP-721 NFTs
author: Tim Daubenschütz (@TimDaub), Anders (@0xanders)
one_line_summary: Minimal soulbound NFT interface marking non-transferrable tokens.
notes: Minimal extension of ERC-721 using ERC-165 feature detection. Introduces `locked()` function and events to identify tokens as soulbound (non-transferrable).

title: Blueprint contract format
description: Define a bytecode container format for indexing and utilizing blueprint contracts
author: Charles Cooper (@charles-cooper), Edward Amor (@skellet0r)
one_line_summary: Blueprint contract format with on-chain bytecode container for initcode storage.
notes: Defines bytecode preamble (0xFE71...) to mark contracts as initcode blueprints. Enables safe on-chain storage of constructor code with version and data section encoding.

title: ERC-1155 Allowance Extension
description: Extension for ERC-1155 secure approvals
author: Iván Mañús (@ivanmmurciaua), Juan Carlos Cantó (@EscuelaCryptoES)
one_line_summary: Granular approval extension for ERC-1155 by token ID and amount.
notes: Extends ERC-1155 with approve/allowance pattern inspired by ERC-20. Enables per-ID, per-amount approvals instead of blanket setApprovalForAll.

title: NFT Rights Management
description: An interface for creating copyright licenses that transfer with an NFT.
author: James Grimmelmann (@grimmelm), Yan Ji (@iseriohn), Tyler Kell (@relyt29)
one_line_summary: NFT copyright license management with tree-structured sublicenses.
notes: Extends ERC-721 with license hierarchy. Root license transfers with NFT; holders can create sublicenses. All operations traceable via events.

title: Contract Resource Requests
description: Allows the requesting of resources from contracts
author: Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
one_line_summary: HTTP-like resource requests from smart contracts via standardized interface.
notes: Standardizes contract resource requests with HTTP-like response codes, headers, and body support. Enables contracts to request data from contracts and facilitates decentralized DApp frontends.

title: Smart Contract Executable Proposal Interface
description: An interface to create and execute proposals.
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: Executable proposal interface for DAOs and governance contracts.
notes: Defines interface to create and execute proposals on-chain. Decoupled from voting (ERC-1202). Enables function calls bundled with targets, values, gas limits.

title: Account-bound Finance
description: An ERC-5114 extension that aids in preventing arbitrary loss of funds
author: Hyungsuk Kang (@hskang9), Viktor Pernjek (@smuxx)
one_line_summary: Personal finance contracts with account-bound tokens for self-sovereign asset management.
notes: Extends ERC-5114 with pattern for individual finance contracts + account-bound credentials. Separates investor funds from operator fees; governs via contribution-based voting multipliers.

title: Retrieval of EIP-712 domain
description: A way to describe and retrieve an EIP-712 domain to securely integrate EIP-712 signatures.
author: Francisco Giordano (@frangio)
one_line_summary: Retrieval of EIP-712 domain fields for secure signature verification.
notes: Standardizes eip712Domain() function to publish domain separator fields. Enables wallets/apps to validate and display signing context; supports extensions via EIP numbers.

title: ERC Detection and Discovery
description: An interface to identify if major behavior or optional behavior specified in an ERC is supported for a given caller.
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: ERC detection and discovery by number with caller-aware support query.
notes: Alternative to ERC-165 using ERC numbers instead of selector hashes. Caller-aware, supports minor identifiers for sub-features. Emits OnSupportERC events for discovery.

title: Ethereum Notary Interface
description: Allows Smart Contracts to be Legally Binding Off-Chain
author: Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
one_line_summary: Notary interface enabling legally-binding smart contracts via IPFS documents.
notes: Allows contracts to link to legal documents and track signatures. Uses ERC-5568 signals to request wallet signatures. Documents must be permissive, not restrictive.

title: ENS Trust to hold NFTs under ENS name
description: An interface for a smart contract acting as a "trust" that holds tokens by ENS name.
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: ENS trust contract holding NFTs on behalf of ENS domain names.
notes: Enables tokens to be held by ENS domains rather than wallet addresses. Implements ERC-721/ERC-1155 receivers; claimTo verifies ENS ownership before transferring tokens.

title: Light Contract Ownership
description: An interface for identifying ownership of contracts
author: William Entriken (@fulldecent)
one_line_summary: Minimal interface for identifying accounts that control contracts.
notes: Slimmed-down alternative to ERC-173; does not implement ERC-165 for backward compatibility with existing markets.

title: EIP-721 User And Expires And Level Extension
description: Add a time-limited role with restricted permissions to EIP-721 tokens.
author: Yan (@yan253319066)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension adding time-limited user role with restricted permissions.
notes: Supports role-based access control for NFTs; user role separates usage from ownership.

title: NFT Author Information and Consent
description: An extension of EIP-721 for NFT authorship and author consent.
author: Samuele Marro (@samuelemarro), Luca Donno (@lucadonnoh)
one_line_summary: Standard for storing NFT authorship info and consent proofs in metadata.
notes: Uses ERC-712 signed messages to prove author consent without requiring on-chain transactions from authors.

title: ERC-721 Entitlement Extension
description: Allows token owners to grant the ability for others to use specific properties of those tokens
author: Gavin John (@Pandapip1), Tim Daubenschütz (@TimDaub)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension enabling token owners to grant limited usage rights to others.
notes: Allows granular entitlements separate from ownership, with optional enumerable and metadata extensions.

title: EIP-1155 Non-Fungible Token extension
description: Allow EIP-1155 to represent Non-Fungible Tokens (tokens who have a unique owner)
author: Ronan Sandford (@wighawag)
one_line_summary: ERC-1155 extension adding ownerOf to support non-fungible tokens within ERC-1155.
notes: Enables detecting unique tokens and retrieving owner on-chain without implementing ERC-721.

title: Security Contact Interface
description: An interface for security notice using asymmetric encryption
author: Zainan Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: Interface for expressing security contact via asymmetric encryption keys.
notes: Provides standardized channel for security researchers to report vulnerabilities; supports GPG and future schemes.

title: Endorsement - Permit for Any Functions
description: A general protocol for approving function calls in the same transaction relying on ERC-5750.
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: General permit protocol for approving any function call via signatures.
notes: Extends ERC-5750; supports multi-sig and threshold approvals in single transaction; supersedes ERC-2612 scope.

title: Consensual Soulbound Tokens
description: Interface for special NFTs with immutable ownership and pre-determined immutable burn authorization
author: Buzz Cai (@buzzcai)
one_line_summary: Soulbound NFT standard with immutable ownership and predetermined burn authorization.
notes: Non-transferable credentials; burn authority immutable after issuance; extends ERC-721.

title: Jurisdiction, Accreditation, and Enforcement
description: An interface for identifying the sovereignty status, observed jurisdiction, accreditation, and enforcement mechanisms.
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: Interface for declaring contract sovereignty, jurisdiction, accreditation, and enforcement pathways.
notes: Bridges real-world legal frameworks with on-chain systems; supports regulated and institutional use cases.

title: NFT Hyperlink Extension
description: NFT Hyperlink Extension embeds hyperlinks onto NFTs, allowing users to click any hNFT and be transported to any url set by the owner.
author: IronMan_CH (@coderfengyun)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension embedding hyperlinks in NFTs for rich content attachments.
notes: Allows NFT owners to authorize addressees to manage URL slots; enables metadata-like customization.

title: Multi-privilege Management NFT Extension
description: Create shareable multi-privilege NFTs for EIP-721
author: Jeremy Z (@wnft)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension for managing shareable, time-limited privileges attached to tokens.
notes: Privileges can be cloned and distributed; separate from ownership; supports on-chain and off-chain utilities.

title: Rental & Delegation NFT - EIP-721 Extension
description: Adds a conditional time-limited user role to EIP-721. This role can be delegated or borrowed.
author: Jan Smrža (@smrza), David Rábel (@rabeles11), Tomáš Janča <tomas.janca@jtbstorage.eu>, Jan Bureš (@JohnyX89), DOBBYLABS (@DOBBYLABS)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension adding time-limited user role for rental and delegation scenarios.
notes: Borrowed flag prevents owner override during rental; owner retains NFT for airdrops; optional balance and terminable extensions.

title: EIP-1155 asset backed NFT extension
description: Extends EIP-1155 to support crucial operations for asset-backed NFTs
author: liszechung (@liszechung)
one_line_summary: ERC-1155 extension enabling forced acquisition of fractionalized asset-backed NFTs.
notes: Addresses fractionalization deadlock; enables majority holders to acquire remaining shares at TWAP price.

title: Refundable Tokens
description: Adds refund functionality to ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 tokens
author: elie222 (@elie222), Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
one_line_summary: Refund interface for ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 tokens during initial offerings.
notes: Funds escrowed until deadline; supports compliance with EU distance selling regulations; includes batch refund optional extensions.

title: Soulbound Multi-owner Tokens
description: An interface for non-transferable, Multi-owner NFTs binding to Ethereum accounts
author: Lucas Martín Grasso Ramos (@LucasGrasso), Matias Arazi (@MatiArazi)
one_line_summary: Non-transferable multi-owner soulbound tokens for credentials and certificates.
notes: Supports multi-cohort issuance via deterministic tokenId; irreversible renunciation; enables account recovery via multi-owner binding.

title: Referable NFT
description: An ERC-721 extension to construct reference relationships among NFTs
author: Saber Yu (@OniReimu), Qin Wang <qin.wang@data61.csiro.au>, Shange Fu <shange.fu@monash.edu>, Yilin Sai <yilin.sai@data61.csiro.au>, Shiping Chen <shiping.chen@data61.csiro.au>, Sherry Xu <xiwei.xu@data61.csiro.au>, Jiangshan Yu <jiangshan.yu@monash.edu>
authors_normalized: Saber Yu (@OniReimu);Qin Wang <qin.wang@data61.csiro.au>;Shange Fu <shange.fu@monash.edu>;Yilin Sai <yilin.sai@data61.csiro.au>;Shiping Chen <shiping.chen@data61.csiro.au>;Sherry Xu <xiwei.xu@data61.csiro.au>;Jiangshan Yu <jiangshan.yu@monash.edu>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension enabling reference relationships between NFTs forming a DAG.
notes: Introduces referable NFTs (rNFTs) with referring/referred relationships and timestamps for tracking citation networks.

title: Refundable Fungible Token
description: Allows refunds for EIP-20 tokens by escrow smart contract
author: StartfundInc (@StartfundInc)
one_line_summary: Escrow service extension for ERC-20 with refund capability.
notes: Dual contract model: Payable and Escrow contracts for reversible token exchanges.

title: Revocation List Registry
description: Registry of revocation lists for revoking arbitrary data.
author: Philipp Bolte (@strumswell), Lauritz Leifermann (@lleifermann), Dennis von der Bey (@DennisVonDerBey)
authors_normalized: Philipp Bolte (@strumswell);Lauritz Leifermann (@lleifermann);Dennis von der Bey (@DennisVonDerBey)
one_line_summary: Decentralized registry for managing revocation lists via namespaces.
notes: Role-based revocation management with delegation support; addresses PKI centralization issues.

title: Representing IP and its Royalty Structure
description: A way of representing intellectual property and its respective royalty structure on chain
author: Roy Osherove (@royosherove)
one_line_summary: Intellectual property representation on-chain with royalty structure tokens.
notes: Separates IP registration from licensing; royalty portion tokens for multiple payment types.

title: NFT Legal Use, Repurposing, and Remixing
description: An interface for describing and enforcing the legal use and remix of an NFT. On-chain registry of rights, attribution and derivative links.
author: Isaac Patka (@ipatka), COALA Licensing Taskforce <info@coala.org>
one_line_summary: NFT legal usage rights, remixing, and on-chain derivative/reproduction tracking.
notes: Extends ERC-5218; tracks derivative works and reproductions; copyright owner field; commercial exploitation logging.

title: Cross Chain Write Deferral Protocol
description: The cross chain write deferral protocol provides a mechanism to defer the storage & resolution of mutations to off-chain handlers
author: Paul Gauvreau (@0xpaulio), Nick Johnson (@arachnid)
one_line_summary: Cross-chain write deferral protocol for off-chain mutation storage.
notes: Handles L2 and off-chain database handlers; EIP-712 signed mutations; reduces L1 gas costs.

title: Redeemable NFTs
description: Makes an NFT redeemable for a physical object
author: Olivier Fernandez (@fernandezOli), Frédéric Le Coidic (@FredLC29), Julien Béranger (@julienbrg)
one_line_summary: Redeemable NFT extension signaling physical asset redemption.
notes: Simple extension with redeem() function and isRedeemable() status check.

title: Stealth Addresses
description: Private, non-interactive transactions
author: Toni Wahrstätter (@nerolation), Matt Solomon (@mds1), Ben DiFrancesco (@apbendi), Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin)
one_line_summary: Standardized stealth addresses for private, non-interactive token transfers.
notes: SECP256k1 with view tags; singleton ERC5564Announcer at 0x55649E01...; enables recipient privacy.

title: Well-Known Format for Required Actions
description: Signal to wallets that an action is needed through a well-known function and revert reason
author: Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
one_line_summary: Machine-readable format for wallet-signaled required actions via revert reasons.
notes: walletSignal24() and WalletSignal24 error; minimal 24-bit instruction IDs; extensible for future ERCs.

title: Digital Receipt Non-Fungible Tokens
description: Non-Fungible Tokens as digital receipts for physical purchases, where the metadata represents a JSON receipt
author: Sean Darcy (@darcys22)
one_line_summary: NFT-based digital receipts for physical purchases with JSON schema.
notes: Receipt structure with vendor, items, taxes; backward compatible with ERC-721 metadata.

title: Sign-In with Ethereum Capabilities, ReCaps
description: Mechanism on top of Sign-In with Ethereum for informed consent to delegate capabilities with an extensible scope mechanism
author: Oliver Terbu (@awoie), Jacob Ward (@cobward), Charles Lehner (@clehner), Sam Gbafa (@skgbafa), Wayne Chang (@wyc), Charles Cunningham (@chunningham)
authors_normalized: Oliver Terbu (@awoie);Jacob Ward (@cobward);Charles Lehner (@clehner);Sam Gbafa (@skgbafa);Wayne Chang (@wyc);Charles Cunningham (@chunningham)
one_line_summary: Sign-In with Ethereum extension (ReCaps) for capability delegation.
notes: Adds informed consent and scoped capabilities on top of SIWE; ReCap URIs in resources array.

title: ERC-721 NFT Authorization
description: Allows NFT owners to authorize other users to use their NFTs.
author: Veega Labs (@VeegaLabsOfficial), Sean NG (@ngveega), Tiger (@tiger0x), Fred (@apan826), Fov Cao (@fovcao)
one_line_summary: Separates NFT ownership from commercial usage rights authorization.
notes: Allows multiple authorized users with specific rights and expiration; supports duration extension and right updates.

title: NFT Lien
description: Extend ERC-721 to support putting liens on NFTs
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv), Allen Zhou <allen@ubiloan.io>, Alex Qin <alex@ubiloan.io>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension for placing and removing security liens on NFTs.
notes: addLienHolder() and removeLienHolder(); supports multiple lien holders; used for collateral/debt scenarios.

title: Multiverse NFTs
description: A universal representation of multiple related NFTs as a single digital asset across various platforms
author: Gaurang Torvekar (@gaurangtorvekar), Khemraj Adhawade (@akhemraj), Nikhil Asrani (@nikhilasrani)
one_line_summary: Multiverse NFT standard indexing related NFTs across multiple platforms.
notes: Bundle/unbundle delegate NFTs; enables cross-platform asset interoperability; partial bundling support.

title: ERC-1155 Supply Extension
description: A simple mechanism to fetch token supply data from ERC-1155 tokens
author: Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
one_line_summary: ERC-1155 supply extension with totalSupply() and exists() functions.
notes: Minimal interface; does not implement ERC-165; compatible with OpenZeppelin ERC1155Supply.

title: NFT Metadata JSON Schema dStorage Extension
description: Add a dStorage property to non-fungible tokens (NFTs) metadata JSON schema to provide decentralized storage information of NFT assets
author: Gavin Fu (@gavfu), Leo Wang (@wanglie1986), Bova Chen (@appoipp), Guang Han (@pangwa), Brian Wu (@wuhaixian1984)
authors_normalized: Gavin Fu (@gavfu);Leo Wang (@wanglie1986);Bova Chen (@appoipp);Guang Han (@pangwa);Brian Wu (@wuhaixian1984)
one_line_summary: Extension adding decentralized storage metadata to ERC-721/1155 NFT tokens.
notes: Provides optional dStorage JSON schema property for NFT metadata, compatible with existing standards without interface changes.

title: New approach for encryption / decryption
description: defines a specification for encryption and decryption using Ethereum wallets.
author: Firn Protocol (@firnprotocol), Fried L. Trout, Weiji Guo (@weijiguo)
one_line_summary: Encryption/decryption interface using Ethereum keys and secp256k1 curve via RPC methods.
notes: Proposes eth_getEncryptionPublicKey and eth_performECDH RPC methods for direct-to-address encryption; includes contract encryption support via encryptTo method similar to EIP-1271.

title: Composable Soulbound NFT, EIP-1155 Extension
description: Add composable soulbound property to EIP-1155 tokens
author: HonorLabs (@honorworldio)
one_line_summary: ERC-1155 extension for composable soulbound tokens preventing transfer.
notes: Adds isSoulbound per-token-ID property to ERC-1155; tokens marked soulbound cannot transfer but can mint/burn.

title: NFT Licensing Agreements
description: An oracle for retrieving NFT licensing agreements
author: Timi (@0xTimi), 0xTriple7 (@ysqi)
one_line_summary: Oracle for registering and retrieving NFT licensing agreements for derivative works.
notes: Defines dNFT (derivative NFT) licensing registry supporting exclusive/non-exclusive agreements with royalty tracking; compatible with ERC-2981.

title: Delegation Registry
description: Delegation of permissions for safer and more convenient signing operations.
author: foobar (@0xfoobar), Wilkins Chung (@wwhchung) <wilkins@manifold.xyz>, ryley-o (@ryley-o), Jake Rockland (@jakerockland), andy8052 (@andy8052)
one_line_summary: Delegation registry allowing vault wallets to grant asset control to delegate wallets.
notes: Enables wallet/contract/token-level delegation for safer authentication without sharing signing keys; supports read-only access patterns.

title: Subscription NFTs
description: Add subscription-based functionality to EIP-721 tokens
author: cygaar (@cygaar)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension for subscription NFTs with expiration and renewal functionality.
notes: Adds subscription lifecycle methods (renew, cancel, expiresAt, isRenewable) for time-limited NFT memberships/passes.

title: Token State Fingerprint
description: Unambiguous token state identifier
author: Naim Ashhab (@ashhanai)
one_line_summary: Interface for unambiguous identification of mutable token state via fingerprint.
notes: Provides deterministic state fingerprint without requiring knowledge of implementation details; enables protocol support for mutable tokens.

title: Token Minting and Burning
description: An extension for minting and burning EIP-20, EIP-721, and EIP-1155 tokens
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: Consistent minting and burning interface extension for ERC-20/721/1155 tokens.
notes: Standardizes mint/burn methods across token types with consistent event emission and supply tracking.

title: Bindable Token Interface
description: Interface for binding fungible and non-fungible tokens to assets.
author: Leeren (@leeren)
one_line_summary: Interface for binding fungible and non-fungible tokens to NFTs for bundled transfers.
notes: Enables N:1 token-to-NFT bundling; tokens transfer with bound NFTs until owner unbinds them; supports ERC-721 and ERC-1155.

title: Signature replacement interface
description: Non-interactive replacing of smart contract wallet signatures that became stale due to configuration changes.
author: Agustin Aguilar (@Agusx1211)
one_line_summary: Non-interactive replacement of stale smart contract wallet signatures via off-chain URI.
notes: Allows wallets to expose getAlternativeSignature URI for clients to refresh invalid signatures due to configuration changes.

title: Transferable Vesting NFT
description: An interface for transferable vesting NFTs which release underlying tokens over time.
author: Apeguru (@Apegurus), Marco De Vries <marco@paladinsec.co>, Mario <mario@paladinsec.co>, DeFiFoFum (@DeFiFoFum), Elliott Green (@elliott-green)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 standard for vesting NFTs releasing underlying tokens over time.
notes: Enables transferable liquid vesting contracts with configurable vesting curves; replaces traditional SAFT with on-chain semi-liquid assets.

title: Semi-Fungible Soulbound Token
description: An interface for soulbound tokens, also known as badges or account-bound tokens, that can be both fungible and non-fungible.
author: Austin Zhu (@AustinZhu), Terry Chen <terry.chen@phaneroz.io>
one_line_summary: Semi-fungible soulbound token interface for non-transferable identity/credential tokens.
notes: Combines fungible/non-fungible token storage in slots; includes governance, delegation, expiration, and recovery extensions.

title: Commit Interface
description: A simple but general commit interface to support commit-reveal scheme.
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv), Matt Stam (@mattstam)
one_line_summary: General commit interface for commit-reveal schemes without standardized reveal.
notes: Provides IERC_COMMIT_CORE and IERC_COMMIT_GENERAL for privacy-preserving interactions; compatible with EIP-1271 signatures.

title: Latent Fungible Token
description: An interface for tokens that become fungible after a period of time.
author: Cozy Finance (@cozyfinance), Tony Sheng (@tonysheng), Matt Solomon (@mds1), David Laprade (@davidlaprade), Payom Dousti (@payomdousti), Chad Fleming (@chad-js), Franz Chen (@Dendrimer)
authors_normalized: Cozy Finance (@cozyfinance);Tony Sheng (@tonysheng);Matt Solomon (@mds1);David Laprade (@davidlaprade);Payom Dousti (@payomdousti);Chad Fleming (@chad-js);Franz Chen (@Dendrimer)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension enabling tokens to become fungible after maturity period.
notes: Tokens remain non-fungible until maturity; adds balanceOfMatured and getMints methods for tracking vesting/deposit-locked receipt tokens.

title: General Extensibility for Method Behaviors
description: Designating last param of dynamically sized bytes to be used for behavior extensions of methods.
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: Standardizes using final bytes parameter for extensible method behavior across ERCs.
notes: Enables backward-compatible extensibility for existing methods via unstructured data parameter; multiple existing ERCs already partially compliant.

title: Lockable Extension for EIP-721
description: Interface for disabling token transfers (locking) and re-enabling them (unlocking).
author: Filipp Makarov (@filmakarov)
one_line_summary: Extension enabling NFTs to be locked and unlocked, preventing transfers while preserving utility.
notes: Novel locking mechanism for NFTs allowing collateralized loans, rentals, and installment purchases without transferring custody.

title: Context-Dependent Multi-Asset Tokens
description: An interface for Multi-Asset tokens with context dependent asset type output controlled by owner's preference.
author: Bruno Škvorc (@Swader), Cicada (@CicadaNCR), Steven Pineda (@steven2308), Stevan Bogosavljevic (@stevyhacker), Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
one_line_summary: Multi-Asset NFT standard enabling context-dependent output of different asset formats per token.
notes: Enables cross-metaverse compatibility by storing multiple assets with priority ordering; propose-accept pattern for asset changes.

title: Physical Backed Tokens
description: Minimal interface for linking ownership of ERC-721 NFTs to a physical chip
author: 2pmflow (@2pmflow), locationtba (@locationtba), Cameron Robertson (@ccamrobertson), cygaar (@cygaar), Brian Weick (@bweick), vectorized (@vectorized), djdabs (@djdabs)
one_line_summary: Minimal interface linking ERC-721 NFTs to physical chips via signature verification.
notes: Bridges on-chain and off-chain ownership of physical items; uses chip signatures to authorize transfers.

title: Voting with delegation
description: An interface for voting weight tracking, with delegation support
author: Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx), Francisco Giordano (@frangio)
one_line_summary: Standardizes voting power tracking and delegation with checkpoint support and ERC-712 signing.
notes: Supports multiple time-tracking modes (block number, timestamp); essential for DAO voting mechanisms.
