title: Auto-renewable allowance extension
description: Extension to enable automatic renewals on allowance approvals
author: zlace (@zlace0x), zhongfu (@zhongfu), edison0xyz (@edison0xyz)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension enabling auto-renewable allowances that recover over time at specified rates.
notes: Enables subscription and recurring payment use cases with automatic allowance recovery instead of unlimited approvals.

title: Complex Numbers stored in `bytes32` types
description: Store real and imaginary parts of complex numbers in the least significant and most significant 16 bytes respectively of a `bytes32` type.
author: Paul Edge (@genkifs)
one_line_summary: Standardizes storing complex numbers in bytes32 with real part in LSBs and imaginary in MSBs.
notes: Non-domain-specific encoding standard for mathematical computations; supports cartesian, polar, exponential formats.

title: On-Chain Verifiable Credentials
description: Interface for contracts that manage verifiable claims and identifiers as Soulbound tokens.
author: Yu Liu (@yuliu-debond), Junyi Zhong (@Jooeys)
one_line_summary: Soulbound Token standard for on-chain verifiable credentials with claim-based identity verification.
notes: Defines metadata structures for claims and logic operators; supports issuer/verifier separation for KYC/compliance.

title: Token Transfer by Social Recovery
description: On-Chain Social Recovery taking users' reputation into account & using a nearest-neighbour approach.
author: Erhard Dinhobl (@mrqc), Kevin Riedl (@wsdt)
one_line_summary: Social recovery mechanism for token transfers using neighbor approval with reputation scoring.
notes: Uses tanh-based reputation formula weighing neighbor rewards and time; addresses lost private key recovery problem.

title: Smart Contract Event Hooks
description: Format that allows contracts to semi-autonoumously respond to events emitted by other contracts
author: Simon Brown (@orbmis)
one_line_summary: Event hook standard enabling contracts to respond to events via relayer network without off-chain processes.
notes: Pub/sub pattern with registry, publishers, subscribers, and relayers; supports cross-chain messaging.

title: Role-based Access Control
description: An interface for role-based access control for smart contracts.
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: Role-based access control interface with bytes32 roles mapping to callable methods.
notes: Follows ERC-5750 extensibility pattern; separates core, general, and metadata interfaces.

title: ERC-721 Balance indexing via Transfer event
description: Mandates emitting the Transfer event for ERC-721 NFTs during contract creation
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: Mandates Transfer event emission during ERC-721 contract creation for indexability.
notes: Removes exception in ERC-721 to enable complete transfer tracking from minting; aligns with ERC-1155 behavior.

title: Parent-Governed Nestable Non-Fungible Tokens
description: An interface for Nestable Non-Fungible Tokens with emphasis on parent token's control over the relationship.
author: Bruno Škvorc (@Swader), Cicada (@CicadaNCR), Steven Pineda (@steven2308), Stevan Bogosavljevic (@stevyhacker), Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
one_line_summary: Nestable NFTs allowing tokens to be owned by other tokens with parent governance control.
notes: Parent-child relationships with propose-accept pattern; enables bundling, collecting, membership, delegation use cases.

title: Real Estate Token
description: An interface for real estate NFTs that extends ERC-721
author: Alex (@Alex-Klasma), Ben Fusek (@bfusek), Daniel Fallon-Cyr (@dfalloncyr)
one_line_summary: Real estate tokenization standard extending ERC-721 with legal agreements, debt tracking, and foreclosure status.
notes: Addresses on/off-chain reconciliation for physical assets; requires administrator entity for legal enforcement.

title: Signature Validation Method for NFTs
description: A way to verify signatures when the signing entity is an ERC-721 or ERC-1155 NFT
author: Jack Boyuan Xu (@boyuanx)
one_line_summary: Signature validation standard enabling NFTs to sign and verify messages via isValidSignature(tokenId, hash, data).
notes: Extends ERC-1271 for NFT use; enables office/role representation as transferrable NFTs in DAOs.

title: Custom errors for commonly-used tokens
description: Lists custom errors for common token implementations
author: Ernesto García (@ernestognw), Francisco Giordano (@frangio), Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx)
one_line_summary: Standardized custom errors for ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 tokens for gas-efficient error reporting.
notes: Replaces revert strings with structured errors; provides consistent error decoding across applications.

title: No Intermediary NFT Trading Protocol
description: Adds a marketplace functionality with more diverse royalty schemes to ERC-721
author: 5660-eth (@5660-eth), Silvere Heraudeau (@lambdalf-dev), Martin McConnell (@offgridgecko), Abu <team10kuni@gmail.com>, Wizard Wang
one_line_summary: Marketplace functionality for NFT trading with diverse royalty schemes.
notes: Enables non-intermediary NFT trading with support for value-added royalties and flexible payment tokens.

title: Universal Token Router
description: A singleton router contract allows tokens to be spent in the transfer-and-call pattern instead of approve-then-call.
author: Derion (@derion-io), Zergity (@Zergity), Ngo Quang Anh (@anhnq82), BerlinP (@BerlinP), Khanh Pham (@blackskin18), Hal Blackburn (@h4l)
one_line_summary: Universal router for token spending in transfer-and-call pattern.
notes: Replaces approve-then-call with secure transfer-and-call, supports ERC-20/721/1155 via singleton factory pattern.

title: Smart Derivative Contract
description: A deterministic protocol for frictionless trade processing of financial contracts
author: Christian Fries (@cfries), Peter Kohl-Landgraf (@pekola), Alexandros Korpis (@kourouta)
one_line_summary: Deterministic protocol for automated settlement of financial derivatives.
notes: Removes counterparty risk via on-chain valuation and settlement; supports collateralized and defaultable variants.

title: Guard of NFT/SBT, an Extension of ERC-721
description: A new management role with an expiration date of NFT/SBT is defined, achieving the separation of transfer right and holding right.
author: 5660-eth (@5660-eth), Wizard Wang
one_line_summary: NFT/SBT guard role separating holding and transfer rights with expiration.
notes: Enables anti-theft, lending, leasing, and SBT management by separating transfer authority from ownership.

title: Hierarchical NFTs
description: Hierarchical NFTs, an extension to EIP-721.
author: Keegan Lee (@keeganlee), msfew <msfew@hyperoracle.io>, Kartin <kartin@hyperoracle.io>, qizhou (@qizhou)
one_line_summary: Filesystem-like hierarchical NFTs with parent-child relationships.
notes: Enables nested NFT structures for forums, e-commerce, and org hierarchies with optional enumerable and burnable extensions.

title: Cross-Chain Messaging Interface
description: A common smart contract interface for interacting with messaging protocols.
author: Sujith Somraaj (@sujithsomraaj)
one_line_summary: Common interface for cross-chain arbitrary message bridges.
notes: Standardizes send/receive pattern across competing AMBs; draft status indicates ongoing refinement.

title: Composable NFTs utilizing Equippable Parts
description: An interface for Composable non-fungible tokens through fixed and slot parts equipping.
author: Bruno Škvorc (@Swader), Cicada (@CicadaNCR), Steven Pineda (@steven2308), Stevan Bogosavljevic (@stevyhacker), Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
one_line_summary: Composable NFTs with equippable fixed and slot parts via Catalog.
notes: Enables NFT composition with cherry-picked parts and equipped child NFTs; supports digital scarcity via exclusive equipping.

title: Contracts Dependencies Registry
description: A registry for managing smart contracts with their dependencies.
author: Artem Chystiakov (@arvolear)
one_line_summary: Registry system for managing protocol smart contracts with dependency injection.
notes: Enables contract upgrades, runtime substitution, and dependency management via ContractsRegistry and Dependant pattern.

title: Tokenized Vaults with Lock-in Period
description: ERC-4626 Tokenized Vaults with Lock-in Period.
author: Anderson Chen (@Ankarrr), Martinet Lee <martinetlee@gmail.com>, Anton Cheng <antonassocareer@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: ERC-4626 vaults extended with lock-in periods and scheduled deposits/redemptions.
notes: Allows vaults to lock assets during strategy execution; handles queued operations across vault rounds.

title: Semantic Soulbound Tokens
description: Adding RDF triples to ERC-5192 token metadata to capture social meaning
author: Jessica Chang (@JessicaChg)
one_line_summary: Soulbound tokens with RDF triple metadata for semantic social identity.
notes: Extends ERC-5192 with W3C RDF standards; enables linked data layer for interconnected social metadata.

title: Untransferability Indicator for EIP-1155
description: An extension of EIP-1155 for indicating the transferability of the token.
author: Yuki Aoki (@yuki-js)
one_line_summary: Untransferability indicator for ERC-1155 semi-fungible and fungible soulbound tokens.
notes: Standardizes locked/unlocked status for SBTs across fungibility types via ERC-165 feature detection.

title: ERC-2771 Namespaced Account Abstraction
description: Introducing per-forwarder namespaced addresses to facilitate meta-transactions under a namespacing framework
author: Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
one_line_summary: ERC-2771 extension with per-forwarder namespaced addresses for trustless account abstraction.
notes: Enables namespaced transactions and Namespaced function variants; allows trustless meta-transactions without per-app trust.

title: Elastic Signature
description: Use password to sign data as private key
author: George (@JXRow)
one_line_summary: Password-based signing via zero-knowledge proofs for on-chain verification.
notes: Provides password-based alternative to private key signing; integrable with AA wallets as plugin.

title: Charity token
description: Extension of EIP-20 token that can be partially donated to a charity project
author: Aubay <blockchain-team@aubay.com>, BOCA Jeabby (@bjeabby1507), EL MERSHATI Laith (@lth-elm), KEMP Elia (@eliakemp)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension enabling automatic percentage donations with whitelisted charity addresses.
notes: Supports flexible rates and per-holder charity preferences; donation deducted from sender during transfer.

title: Single-contract Multi-delegatecall
description: Allows an EOA to call multiple functions of a smart contract in a single transaction
author: Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
one_line_summary: Single-contract multicall via delegatecall for atomic multi-function execution.
notes: Reduces transaction count for bulk operations; optional payable variant with msg.value splitting.

title: Cross-Chain Token States Synchronization
description: A paradigm to synchronize token states over multiple existing public chains
author: Shawn Zheng (@xiyu1984), Jason Cheng <chengjingxx@gmail.com>, George Huang (@virgil2019), Kay Lin (@kay404)
one_line_summary: Cross-chain token state synchronization protocol enabling global tokens synchronized across multiple blockchains.
notes: Primary innovation is synchronization mechanism rather than token standard per se; defines consensus-agnostic cross-chain bridging at contract layer.

title: Permission Token
description: A token that holds the permission of an address in an ecosystem
author: Chiro (@chiro-hiro), Victor Dusart (@vdusart)
one_line_summary: Permission token system using bitwise operations to represent authorization in an ecosystem.
notes: Extends ERC-6617 to implement access control via uint256 permissions as alternative to ACLs; cross-ecosystem permission delegation.

title: Contract clock
description: An interface for exposing a contract's clock value and details
author: Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx), Francisco Giordano (@frangio)
one_line_summary: Standardized interface for contracts to expose their internal clock value and tracking mechanism.
notes: Improves composability by allowing external detection of block-number vs timestamp tracking; supports both native and cross-chain clocks.

title: Public Non-Fungible Token Emote Repository
description: React to any Non-Fungible Tokens using Unicode emojis.
author: Bruno Škvorc (@Swader), Steven Pineda (@steven2308), Stevan Bogosavljevic (@stevyhacker), Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
one_line_summary: Public emote repository enabling anyone to react to NFTs using standardized Unicode emojis.
notes: Implements feedback-based NFT evolution and valuation via on-chain reactions; deterministic singleton contract for cross-chain compatibility.

title: Human-readable offline signatures
description: A method for retrieving a human-readable description of EIP-712 typed and structured data.
author: Tal Be'ery <tal@zengo.com>, RoiV (@DeVaz1)
one_line_summary: Method to retrieve human-readable descriptions of EIP-712 typed data structures via contract.
notes: Addresses phishing risk by allowing wallets to query verifying contract for message meaning; contract-controlled description prevents misrepresentation.

title: Minimal Transferable NFT detection interface
description: A minimal extension to identify the transferability of Non-Fungible Tokens.
author: Bruno Škvorc (@Swader), Francesco Sullo (@sullof), Steven Pineda (@steven2308), Stevan Bogosavljevic (@stevyhacker), Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
authors_normalized: Bruno Škvorc (@Swader);Francesco Sullo (@sullof);Steven Pineda (@steven2308);Stevan Bogosavljevic (@stevyhacker);Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
one_line_summary: Minimal extension interface for ERC-721 to detect and enforce non-transferability of specific tokens.
notes: Enables verifiable attribution and immutable properties by binding tokens to owners; avoids specifying state management mechanism.

title: Multi-operator, per-token ERC-721 approvals.
description: Extends ERC-721 to allow token owners to approve multiple operators to control their assets on a per-token basis.
author: Cristian Espinoza (@crisgarner), Simon Fremaux (@dievardump), David Huber (@cxkoda), and Arran Schlosberg (@aschlosberg)
authors_normalized: Cristian Espinoza (@crisgarner);Simon Fremaux (@dievardump);David Huber (@cxkoda);and Arran Schlosberg (@aschlosberg)
one_line_summary: Per-token, multi-operator approval extension for ERC-721 enabling fine-grained operator authorization.
notes: Addresses setApprovalForAll security risk by allowing multiple specific operators per token with efficient bulk revocation via nonces.

title: Signature Validation for Predeploy Contracts
description: A way to verify a signature when the account is a smart contract that has not been deployed yet
author: Ivo Georgiev (@Ivshti), Agustin Aguilar (@Agusx1211)
one_line_summary: Signature validation standard for predeploy contracts using wrapper format to verify counterfactual accounts.
notes: Extends ERC-1271 to support signatures from contracts not yet deployed; wraps signature with deploy data and magic bytes for detection.

title: P2P Escrowed Governance Incentives
description: Interface for building contracts that escrow funds based on an account taking action in a DAO
author: Josh Weintraub (@jhweintraub)
one_line_summary: Escrow interface for peer-to-peer governance incentives enabling trustless vote bribing with verification.
notes: Supports both on-chain and off-chain voting with optional dispute resolution; enables treasury buyback incentive mechanisms.

title: Stealth Meta-Address Registry
description: A canonical contract for entities to register stealth meta-addresses directly or through a third party using signatures.
author: Matt Solomon (@mds1), Toni Wahrstätter (@nerolation), Ben DiFrancesco (@apbendi), Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin), Gary Ghayrat (@garyghayrat)
authors_normalized: Matt Solomon (@mds1);Toni Wahrstätter (@nerolation);Ben DiFrancesco (@apbendi);Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin);Gary Ghayrat (@garyghayrat)
one_line_summary: Canonical registry for stealth meta-addresses enabling private asset transfers via stealth addresses.
notes: Singleton contract supporting EIP-712 and ERC-1271 signatures for delegation; interoperable with ERC-5564 stealth address generation.

title: Non-fungible Token Bound Accounts
description: An interface and registry for smart contract accounts owned by non-fungible tokens
author: Jayden Windle (@jaydenwindle), Benny Giang <bg@futureprimitive.xyz>, Steve Jang, Druzy Downs (@druzydowns), Raymond Huynh (@huynhr), Alanah Lam <alanah@futureprimitive.xyz>, Wilkins Chung (@wwhchung) <wilkins@manifold.xyz>, Paul Sullivan (@sullivph) <paul.sullivan@manifold.xyz>, Auryn Macmillan (@auryn-macmillan), Jan-Felix Schwarz (@jfschwarz), Anton Bukov (@k06a), Mikhail Melnik (@ZumZoom), Josh Weintraub (@jhweintraub) <jhweintraub@gmail.com>, Rob Montgomery (@RobAnon) <rob@revest.finance>, vectorized (@vectorized), Víctor Martínez (@vnmrtz), Adrián Pajares (@0xadrii)
authors_normalized: Jayden Windle (@jaydenwindle);Benny Giang <bg@futureprimitive.xyz>;Steve Jang;Druzy Downs (@druzydowns);Raymond Huynh (@huynhr);Alanah Lam <alanah@futureprimitive.xyz>;Wilkins Chung (@wwhchung) <wilkins@manifold.xyz>;Paul Sullivan (@sullivph) <paul.sullivan@manifold.xyz>;Auryn Macmillan (@auryn-macmillan);Jan-Felix Schwarz (@jfschwarz);Anton Bukov (@k06a);Mikhail Melnik (@ZumZoom);Josh Weintraub (@jhweintraub) <jhweintraub@gmail.com>;Rob Montgomery (@RobAnon) <rob@revest.finance>;vectorized (@vectorized);Víctor Martínez (@vnmrtz);Adrián Pajares (@0xadrii)
one_line_summary: Token-bound accounts system enabling NFTs to own assets and execute operations as smart contract accounts.
notes: Deterministic registry using CREATE2 assigns smart accounts to NFTs; counterfactual accounts enable pre-deployment asset custody.

title: Cultural and Historical Asset Token
description: Metadata extension to enhance the discoverability, connectivity, and collectability of culturally and historically significant NFTs.
author: Phillip Pon <phillip@artifactlabs.com>, Gary Liu <gary@artifactlabs.com>, Henry Chan <henry@artifactlabs.com>, Joey Liu <joey@artifactlabs.com>, Lauren Ho <lauren@artifactlabs.com>, Jeff Leung <jeff@artifactlabs.com>, Brian Liang <brian@artifactlabs.com>, Joyce Li <joyce@artifactlabs.com>, Avir Mahtani <avir@artifactlabs.com>, Antoine Cote (@acote88), David Leung (@dhl)
authors_normalized: Phillip Pon <phillip@artifactlabs.com>;Gary Liu <gary@artifactlabs.com>;Henry Chan <henry@artifactlabs.com>;Joey Liu <joey@artifactlabs.com>;Lauren Ho <lauren@artifactlabs.com>;Jeff Leung <jeff@artifactlabs.com>;Brian Liang <brian@artifactlabs.com>;Joyce Li <joyce@artifactlabs.com>;Avir Mahtani <avir@artifactlabs.com>;Antoine Cote (@acote88);David Leung (@dhl)
one_line_summary: Comprehensive metadata standard for cultural and historical assets extending ERC-721/1155 with provenance and contextual fields.
notes: 48 additional JSON properties for museums/institutions; supports search/discovery interoperability with IIIF and OAI protocols.

title: Abstract Token
description: move tokens on- and off-chain as desired, enabling zero-cost minting while preserving on-chain composability
author: Chris Walker (@cr-walker) <chris@ckwalker.com>
one_line_summary: Abstract token standard enabling zero-cost off-chain minting with on-demand reification preserving on-chain composability.
notes: Supports merkle trees and signatures; tokens exist as messages until reified on-chain; optional dereification for cross-chain/contract movement.

title: Bit Based Permission
description: A permission and role system based on bits
author: Chiro (@chiro-hiro), Victor Dusart (@vdusart)
one_line_summary: Bit-based permission and role system using uint256 bitwise operations for efficient access control.
notes: Up to 256 permissions enabling 2^256 unique roles; supports importance ordering via bit position; optional metadata interface for descriptions.

title: AA Account Metadata For Authentication
description: An ERC-4337 extension to define a new authentication model
author: Shu Dong (@dongshu2013), Zihao Chen (@zihaoccc), Peter Chen (@pette1999)
one_line_summary: ERC-4337 extension defining on-chain account metadata for user-friendly authentication via relay services.
notes: Stores authenticator URIs and authentication schema on-chain; supports end-to-end encryption and signature aggregation for multisig.

title: Multi-redeemable NFTs
description: An extension of ERC-721 which enables an NFT to be redeemed in multiple scenarios for either a physical or digital object
author: RE:DREAMER Lab <dev@redreamer.io>, Archie Chang (@ArchieR7) <archie@redreamer.io>, Kai Yu (@chihkaiyu) <kai@redreamer.io>, Yonathan Randyanto (@Randyanto) <randy@redreamer.io>, Boyu Chu (@chuboyu) <boyu@redreamer.io>, Boxi Li (@boxi79) <boxi@redreamer.io>, Jason Cheng (@JasonCheng0729) <jason@redreamer.io>
authors_normalized: RE:DREAMER Lab <dev@redreamer.io>;Archie Chang (@ArchieR7) <archie@redreamer.io>;Kai Yu (@chihkaiyu) <kai@redreamer.io>;Yonathan Randyanto (@Randyanto) <randy@redreamer.io>;Boyu Chu (@chuboyu) <boyu@redreamer.io>;Boxi Li (@boxi79) <boxi@redreamer.io>;Jason Cheng (@JasonCheng0729) <jason@redreamer.io>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension enabling NFTs to be redeemed multiple times for physical or digital items.
notes: Adds redemption tracking and status management on-chain, with optional metadata schema extension.

title: NFT Flashloans
description: Minimal interface for ERC-721 NFT flashloans
author: out.eth (@outdoteth)
one_line_summary: Extends ERC-3156 flashloans to support NFT flashloans with configurable fee tokens.
notes: Primary purpose is enabling NFT flashloans; extends existing flashloan standard rather than creating token standard.

title: L2 Token List
description: Token List that ensures the correct identification of tokens from different Layer 1, Layer 2, or Sidechains.
author: Kelvin Fichter (@smartcontracts), Andreas Freund (@Therecanbeonlyone1969), Pavel Sinelnikov (@psinelnikov)
one_line_summary: JSON schema for canonical token lists across L1, L2, and sidechains with DIDs and URI identifiers.
notes: Uses JSON-LD and W3C standards for interoperability; defines tokens across multi-chain systems.

title: L2 Aliasing of EVM-based Addresses
description: Identify and translate EVM-based addresses from different Layer 1, Layer 2, or Sidechains
author: Kelvin Fichter (@smartcontracts), Andreas Freund (@Therecanbeonlyone1969)
one_line_summary: Deterministic EVM address aliasing for identifying same account across L1, L2, and sidechains.
notes: Enables cross-chain address translation using offset-based alias scheme; based on existing L2 patterns.

title: ERC-721 Utilities Information Extension
description: Provide easy access to information about the `utility` of an NFT via functions and the NFT's metadata
author: Otniel Nicola (@OT-kthd), Bogdan Popa (@BogdanKTHD)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension providing utility description and historical access information for NFTs.
notes: Adds utility metadata tracking with history; aims to prevent scams by capturing utility terms on-chain.

title: Registry for royalties payment for NFTs
description: A registry used for paying royalties for any NFT with information about the creator
author: Otniel Nicola (@OT-kthd), Bogdan Popa (@BogdanKTHD)
one_line_summary: Registry for paying and tracking royalties owed to NFT creators independent of marketplace.
notes: Extends ERC-2981 with cumulative payment tracking; enables anyone to pay royalties retroactively.

title: Order Book DEX with Two Phase Withdrawal
description: An order book-based DEX Interface that ensures the asset security of both users and the exchange
author: Jessica (@qizheng09), Roy (@royshang), Jun (@SniperUsopp)
one_line_summary: Order book DEX interface with two-phase withdrawal mechanism protecting users and exchange assets.
notes: Addresses security concerns specific to order book DEXs with forced withdrawal validation periods.

title: ERC-721 Holding Time Tracking
description: Add holding time information to ERC-721 tokens
author: Saitama (@saitama2009), Combo <combo@1combo.io>, Luigi <luigi@1combo.io>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension tracking duration and holder of NFT ownership for reward or access logic.
notes: Simple interface returning current holder and holding duration in seconds; supports whitelisting certain addresses.

title: Fungible Key Bound Token
description: An interface for Fungible Key Bound Tokens, also known as a FKBT.
author: Mihai Onila (@MihaiORO), Nick Zeman (@NickZCZ), Narcis Cotaie (@NarcisCRO)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension with optional security via two key wallets controlling transfers and approvals.
notes: Adds binding, approval, and transfer restrictions; includes safeFallback and resetBindings for recovery; fully backward compatible.

title: Non-Fungible Key Bound Token
description: An interface for Non-Fungible Key Bound Tokens, also known as a NFKBT.
author: Mihai Onila (@MihaiORO), Nick Zeman (@NickZCZ), Narcis Cotaie (@NarcisCRO)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension with optional security via two key wallets controlling token transfers and approvals.
notes: Parallel to ERC-6808 for NFTs; adds binding, transfer, approval conditions; includes recovery mechanisms.

title: Support ENS Name for Web3 URL
description: A mapping from an ENS name to the contract address in Web3 URL
author: Qi Zhou (@qizhou), Qiang Zhu (@qzhodl)
one_line_summary: Maps ENS names to contract addresses in Web3 URLs via contentcontract text record and chain-specific addressing.
notes: Extension to ERC-4804 enabling human-readable Web3 URL contracts via ENS; supports ERC-3770 chain-specific addresses.

title: Token Mapping Slot Retrieval Extension
description: Approach to enhance precision of off-chain transaction simulations by accessing mapping storage slot in ERC-20/721/1155 contracts.
author: qdqd (@qd-qd) <qdqdqdqdqd@protonmail.com>
one_line_summary: Storage slot retrieval function for ERC-20/721/1155 mapping enabling accurate off-chain transaction simulation.
notes: Adds getTokenLocationRoot() function to retrieve mapping storage slot; reduces simulation reliance on events.

title: Web3 URL to EVM Call Message Translation
description: A translation of an HTTP-style Web3 URL to an EVM call message
author: Qi Zhou (@qizhou), Chao Pi (@pichaoqkc), Sam Wilson (@SamWilsn), Nicolas Deschildre (@nand2)
one_line_summary: Translates HTTP-style Web3 URLs to EVM call messages supporting manual and auto resolve modes.
notes: Update to ERC-4804 with clarifications and format specifications; supports ENS names and cross-chain calls.

title: Upgradable Fungible Token
description: Upgradable fungible token, a simple extension to ERC-20
author: Jeff Huang (@jeffishjeff)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension enabling upgrade/downgrade of tokens while preserving balances across implementations.
notes: Allows non-proxy upgrade pattern on existing ERC-20 contracts; includes optional see-through functions for combined balances.

title: On-Chain EIP-712 Visualization
description: Visualize structured data highlighting the potential consequences for users' assets
author: Abderrahmen Hanafi (@a6-dou)
one_line_summary: On-chain EIP-712 visualization function returning structured asset impact data to prevent signing scams.
notes: Shifts EIP-712 visualization responsibility from wallets to protocols; returns assetsIn, assetsOut, and liveness information.

title: Modular Smart Contract Accounts
description: Interfaces for smart contract accounts and modules, optionally supporting upgradability and introspection
author: Adam Egyed (@adamegyed), Fangting Liu (@trinity-0111), Jay Paik (@jaypaik), Yoav Weiss (@yoavw), Huawei Gu (@huaweigu), Daniel Lim (@dlim-circle), Ruben Koch (@0xrubes), David Philipson (@dphilipson), Howy Ho (@howydev), Nikita Belenkov (@nikita-quantstamp), zer0dot (@zer0dot), David Kim (@PowerStream3604)
authors_normalized: Adam Egyed (@adamegyed);Fangting Liu (@trinity-0111);Jay Paik (@jaypaik);Yoav Weiss (@yoavw);Huawei Gu (@huaweigu);Daniel Lim (@dlim-circle);Ruben Koch (@0xrubes);David Philipson (@dphilipson);Howy Ho (@howydev);Nikita Belenkov (@nikita-quantstamp);zer0dot (@zer0dot);David Kim (@PowerStream3604)
one_line_summary: Modular interfaces for smart contract accounts and modules with upgradable execution logic.
notes: Extends ERC-4337 with modular architecture separating validation, execution, and hooks into composable modules.

title: Minimal Multi-Token Interface
description: A minimal specification for managing multiple tokens by their id in a single contract.
author: JT Riley (@jtriley2p), Dillon (@d1ll0n), Sara (@snreynolds), Vectorized (@Vectorized), Neodaoist (@neodaoist)
one_line_summary: Minimal multi-token standard combining ERC-20 allowance and ERC-1155 operator models without callbacks.
notes: Removes batch operations and mandatory callbacks from ERC-1155; hybrid operator-approval permission system.

title: Subscription-Based Token
description: ERC-20 extension providing access to a service or product that requires recurring payments.
author: 360 Core <hello@360coreinc.com>, Robin Rajput (@0xRobinR)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension enabling subscription-based access requiring periodic automatic payments.
notes: Adds subscribe/unsubscribe mechanics for recurring payments without manual intervention.

title: ERC-5219 Resolve Mode
description: Adds an ERC-4804 resolveMode to support ERC-5219 requests
author: Gavin John (@Pandapip1), Qi Zhou (@qizhou)
one_line_summary: ERC-4804 resolveMode extension for ERC-5219 contract resource requests.
notes: Minimal extension defining resolveMode return value for ERC-5219 resolution compatibility.

title: Asset-bound Non-Fungible Tokens
description: Asset-bound NFTs anchor a token 1-1 to an asset and operations are authorized through oracle-attestation of control over the asset
author: Thomas Bergmueller (@tbergmueller), Lukas Meyer (@ibex-technology)
one_line_summary: NFT standard anchoring tokens 1:1 to physical/digital assets with oracle-attested ownership transfers.
notes: Permissionless transfers via attestations; designed for phygitals and off-chain asset tokenization.

title: Dual Layer Token
description: Token with a two-level classification system using mainId and subId
author: Adam Boudjemaa (@aboudjem), Mohamad Hammoud (@mohamadhammoud), Nawar Hisso (@nawar-hisso), Khawla Hassan (@khawlahssn), Mohammad Zakeri Rad (@zakrad), Ashish Sood <soodgen@gmail.com>
authors_normalized: Adam Boudjemaa (@aboudjem);Mohamad Hammoud (@mohamadhammoud);Nawar Hisso (@nawar-hisso);Khawla Hassan (@khawlahssn);Mohammad Zakeri Rad (@zakrad);Ashish Sood <soodgen@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Dual-layer token combining ERC-20/721/1155 with mainId/subId classification for RWAs.
notes: Hierarchical token classification enabling fractional ownership of complex assets.

title: Reserved Ownership Accounts
description: A registry for generating future-deployed smart contract accounts owned by users on external services
author: Paul Sullivan (@sullivph) <paul.sullivan@manifold.xyz>, Wilkins Chung (@wwchung) <wilkins@manifold.xyz>, Kartik Patel (@Slokh) <kartik@manifold.xyz>
one_line_summary: Registry generating deterministic smart contract addresses for future-owned user accounts.
notes: Enables on-chain asset reception before account control assumption via signature verification.

title: Efficient Default Lockable Tokens
description: A gas-efficient approach to lockable ERC-721 tokens
author: Francesco Sullo (@sullof), Alexe Spataru (@urataps)
one_line_summary: Gas-efficient ERC-721 locking mechanism with DefaultLocked event on deployment.
notes: Emits status changes only for specific tokens; useful for non-transferable/soulbound NFTs.

title: ERC-721 with transaction validation step.
description: A new validation step for transfer and approve calls, achieving a security step in case of stolen wallet.
author: Eduard López i Fina (@eduardfina)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 with validation step for transfers and approvals to prevent wallet draining.
notes: Pending validations require authorization before execution; enables externalized validation.

title: Verifiable AI-Generated Content Token
description: An ERC-721 extension for verifiable AI-generated content tokens using Zero-Knowledge and Optimistic Machine Learning techniques
author: Cathie So (@socathie), Xiaohang Yu (@xhyumiracle), Conway (@0x1cc), Lee Ting Ting (@tina1998612), Kartin <kartin@hyperoracle.io>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension for verifiable AI-generated content using zkML and opML proofs.
notes: Indexes tokens by prompt; supports optional Enumerable and Updatable extensions for opML scenarios.

title: NFT Creator Attribution
description: Extending NFTs with cryptographically secured creator attribution.
author: indreams (@strollinghome)
one_line_summary: NFT creator attribution via EIP-712 signed CreatorAttribution event at deployment.
notes: Allows correct creator attribution even when non-creator wallet deploys contract.

title: Interoperable Digital Media Indexing
description: A universal indexing method to record, discover and retrieve the history of digital media on EVM-compatible blockchains.
author: Bofu Chen (@bafu), Tammy Yang (@tammyyang)
one_line_summary: Interoperable digital media indexing using CIDs and Commit events across EVM chains.
notes: Universal indexing method for traceability and discovery of digital media assets.

title: Lockable Extension for ERC-721
description: Interface for enabling locking of ERC-721 using locker and approved
author: Piyush Chittara (@piyush-chittara), StreamNFT (@streamnft-tech), Srinivas Joshi (@SrinivasJoshi)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 locking extension preventing transfers while preserving utility during collateralization.
notes: Enables NFT-collateralized loans, rentals, and BNPL without asset escrow; locker controls unlock.

title: NFT Relationship Enhancement
description: Establish relationships between NFTs and setting quantifiable attributes for those relationships.
author: Guang (@xg1990)
one_line_summary: Establishes relationships and quantifiable attributes between ERC-721/1155 tokens.
notes: Enables NFT graphs with derivative relationships and attribute metadata.

title: MIME type for Web3 URL in Auto Mode
description: Add ability to indicate or determine the MIME type of the data returned by a Web3 URL in auto mode
author: Qi Zhou (@qizhou), Nicolas Deschildre (@nand2)
one_line_summary: Query parameters for specifying MIME type of web3:// URL responses in auto mode.
notes: Extends ERC-6860 with mime.content, mime.type, and mime.dataurl parameters.

title: Financial Bonds
description: Represents debt issued by entities to investors.
author: Samuel Gwlanold Edoumou (@Edoumou)
one_line_summary: Fixed-income bonds with coupon rates, maturity dates, and cross-chain support.
notes: Introduces financial bond standard with named variables (coupon, principal, maturity) to improve upon ERC-3475; includes optional ESG parameters and cross-chain interface.

title: Social Recovery Interface
description: Interfaces for social recovery account supporting various guardian types and customizable recovery policies.
author: John Zhang (@johnz1019), Davis Xiang (@xcshuan), Kyle Xu (@kylexyxu), George Zhang (@odysseus0)
one_line_summary: Social recovery interface supporting various guardian types and customizable recovery policies.
notes: Separates identity verification (PermissionVerifier) from recovery policy logic (RecoveryPolicyVerifier) to enable flexible guardian types including off-chain identities; draft status.

title: ERC-20 with transaction validation step.
description: A new validation step for transfer and approve calls, achieving a security step in case of stolen wallet.
author: Eduard López i Fina (@eduardfina)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension adding validation step for transfers and approvals.
notes: Extends ERC-20 with pending validations to prevent wallet draining; transfer/approve create pending validations instead of executing immediately.

title: ERC-721 Multi-Metadata Extension
description: Multiple metadata URIs per token, with the option to pin a primary URI.
author: 0xG (@0xGh), Marco Peyfuss (@mpeyfuss)
one_line_summary: Multiple metadata URIs per ERC-721 token with pinning capability.
notes: Enables multi-metadata support for NFTs with ability to pin preferred URI; backward compatible with existing ERC-721.

title: Simple token, Simplified ERC-20
description: Designed for smart contract wallets, this removes the transferFrom, approve, and allowance functions from ERC-20 tokens.
author: Xiang (@wenzhenxiang), Ben77 (@ben2077), Mingshi S. (@newnewsms)
one_line_summary: Simplified token standard for smart contract wallets, removing approve/allowance/transferFrom.
notes: Designed for AA wallets; removes ERC-20 approval mechanism in favor of wallet-level permission management; forward compatible with ERC-20.

title: Namespaced Storage Layout
description: Conventions for the storage location of structs in the namespaced storage pattern.
author: Francisco Giordano (@frangio), Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx), Ernesto García (@ernestognw), Eric Lau (@ericglau)
one_line_summary: Namespaced storage layout convention using NatSpec annotation and collision-safe hashing.
notes: Standardizes storage location for modular contracts using @custom:storage-location; uses erc7201 formula: keccak256(keccak256(id)-1) & ~0xff.

title: Contract wallet management token
description: Focuses on fungible token management within smart contract wallets, offering enhanced transaction flexibility and security
author: Xiang (@wenzhenxiang), Ben77 (@ben2077), Mingshi S. (@newnewsms)
one_line_summary: Smart contract wallet token management with wallet-level approve and transfer hooks.
notes: Enables token asset management within AA wallets via tokenApprove, tokenAllowance, tokenTransfer functions; supports batch operations and transfer hooks.

title: On-Chain Data Containers
description: Interoperability by abstracting logic away from storage
author: Rachid Ajaja (@abrajaja), Matthijs de Vries (@sudomati), Alexandros Athanasopulos (@Xaleee), Pavel Rubin (@pash7ka), Sebastian Galimberti Romano (@galimba), Daniel Berbesi (@berbex), Apostolos Mavropoulos (@ApostolosMavro), Barbara Marcano (@Barbara-Marcano), Daniel Ortega (@xdaniortega)
authors_normalized: Rachid Ajaja (@abrajaja);Matthijs de Vries (@sudomati);Alexandros Athanasopulos (@Xaleee);Pavel Rubin (@pash7ka);Sebastian Galimberti Romano (@galimba);Daniel Berbesi (@berbex);Apostolos Mavropoulos (@ApostolosMavro);Barbara Marcano (@Barbara-Marcano);Daniel Ortega (@xdaniortega)
one_line_summary: On-chain data containers abstracting storage from logic via Data Objects and Data Managers.
notes: Separates data storage (Data Objects) from logic (Data Managers) using Data Points for indexing; enables data portability between Data Index implementations.

title: Identity-aggregated NFT
description: The aggregation of web2 & web3 identities to NFTs, authorized by individuals, gives attributes of ownerships, relationships, experiences.
author: Chloe Gu <chloe@carv.io>, Navid X. (@xuxinlai2002), Victor Yu <victor@carv.io>, Archer H.
one_line_summary: Identity-aggregated NFT binding Web2 and Web3 identities to tokens with verification.
notes: Extends ERC-721 to aggregate multiple identities (Web2/Web3) with signature-based verification; includes custom schema in metadata.

title: Encumber - Splitting Ownership & Guarantees
description: A token interface to allow pledging tokens without transferring ownership.
author: Coburn Berry (@coburncoburn), Mykel Pereira (@mykelp), Scott Silver (@scott-silver)
one_line_summary: Token encumber mechanism allowing pledge without transfer of ownership.
notes: Stronger guarantee than ERC-20 allowance; encumbered tokens reserved but not transferred; useful for collateral and escrow scenarios.

title: Token Revenue Sharing
description: Revenue token is a token that shares rewards for holders.
author: Quy Phan (@quyphandang), Quy Phan <quy.phan@cryptoviet.info>
one_line_summary: Revenue-sharing token distributing profits to holders without locking/staking.
notes: Extends ERC-20 with reward mechanisms; tracks inReward/outReward per account and allows periodic reward withdrawals.

title: Ethereum Access Token
description: A protocol for authorizing function calls from an off-chain service
author: Chris Chung (@0xpApaSmURf), Raphael Roullet (@ra-phael)
one_line_summary: Ethereum Access Token for off-chain service authorization of on-chain function calls.
notes: EIP-712 conformant signed messages granting short-lived access to specific functions; includes expiry and replay protection.

title: NFT Metadata Extension like JSON-LD
description: Let NFT metadata have a feature equivalent to JSON-LD to be semantic.
author: Yohei Nishikubo (@yoheinishikubo)
one_line_summary: NFT metadata extension with JSON-LD linked data support.
notes: Adds linked_data array to metadata enabling schema-based semantic data; backward compatible with existing NFT metadata.

title: Purpose bound money
description: An extension to ERC-1155 implementing a hybrid form of programmable payment and money.
author: Orchid-Dev (@proj-orchid-straitsx), Victor Liew (@alcedo), Wong Tse Jian (@wongtsejian), Jacob Shan (@Jacobshan429), Chin Sin Ong (@chinsinong), Praveen Kumar (@veenkumarr)
authors_normalized: Orchid-Dev (@proj-orchid-straitsx);Victor Liew (@alcedo);Wong Tse Jian (@wongtsejian);Jacob Shan (@Jacobshan429);Chin Sin Ong (@chinsinong);Praveen Kumar (@veenkumarr)
one_line_summary: Purpose-bound money wrapping digital currency with conditional spending constraints.
notes: ERC-1155 wrapper around ERC-20 sovToken implementing programmable money with whitelist/blacklist compliance checks; used for vouchers and escrow.

title: Token-Controlled Token Circulation
description: Access control scheme based on token ownership.
author: Ko Fujimura (@kofujimura)
one_line_summary: Token-controlled access via ERC-721/1155 control tokens representing privileges.
notes: Uses token ownership as access control mechanism; roles granted/revoked via mint/burn of control tokens; visible in wallets.

title: Vanilla Options for ERC-20 Tokens
description: An interface for creating, managing, and executing simple time-limited call/put (vanilla) options.
author: Ewan Humbert (@Xeway) <xeway@protonmail.com>, Lassi Maksimainen (@mlalma) <lassi.maksimainen@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Standard interface for vanilla options on ERC-20 tokens with time-limited exercise windows.
notes: Requires ERC-1155 for fractional option ownership. Supports both call and put options with writer collateralization.

title: ⚡ Flash Loans ⚡
description: Interfaces and processes for flash loans
author: Alberto Cuesta Cañada (@alcueca), Michael Amadi (@AmadiMichaels), Devtooligan (@devtooligan), Ultrasecr.eth (@ultrasecreth), Sam Bacha (@sambacha)
one_line_summary: Standardized interface for flash loans with flexible lender and borrower architecture.
notes: Successor to ERC-3156. Supports multiple assets and callback functions with fee collection.

title: Parent-Governed Non-Fungible Tokens Nesting
description: An interface for Non-Fungible Tokens Nesting with emphasis on parent token's control over the relationship.
author: Bruno Škvorc (@Swader), Cicada (@CicadaNCR), Steven Pineda (@steven2308), Stevan Bogosavljevic (@stevyhacker), Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
one_line_summary: Parent-controlled NFT nesting allowing tokens to own other tokens with propose-accept pattern.
notes: Supersedes ERC-6059. Fixes interface ID mismatch. Enables bundling, collecting, membership, and delegation use cases.

title: Portable Smart Contract Accounts
description: Migrating smart contract accounts at the Proxy (ERC-1967) layer.
author: Aaron Yee (@aaronyee-eth)
one_line_summary: Portable smart contract accounts enabling migration between wallets via ERC-1967 proxy layer.
notes: Uses migration keys and time locks for security. Requires shared registry for tracking migrations.

title: Multi-Namespace Onchain Registry
description: An universally accepted multi-namespace registry with mapping structures on the Ethereum
author: Mengshi Zhang (@MengshiZhang), Zihao Chen (@zihaoccc)
one_line_summary: Multi-namespace onchain registry with key-value mapping structure supporting versioning and versioning.
notes: Enables flexible data management. Supports namespace-based data organization and history tracking.

title: Public Non-Fungible Tokens Emote Repository
description: React to any Non-Fungible Tokens using Unicode emojis.
author: Bruno Škvorc (@Swader), Steven Pineda (@steven2308), Stevan Bogosavljevic (@stevyhacker), Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
one_line_summary: Public emote repository allowing reactions to NFTs using Unicode emoji at deterministic address.
notes: Supersedes ERC-6381. Uses string for emojis instead of bytes4. Supports presigned emotes and bulk operations.

title: ERC-20 Update Allowance By Spender
description: Extension to enable revoking and decreasing allowance approval by spender for ERC-20
author: Mohammad Zakeri Rad (@zakrad), Adam Boudjemaa (@aboudjem), Mohamad Hammoud (@mohamadhammoud)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension enabling spenders to decrease allowance autonomously without owner approval.
notes: Addresses security by allowing spenders to revoke excessive allowances. Nullifies infinite approvals.

title: On-Demand Off-Chain Data Retrieval
description: A method to construct multicalls with prepended verifiable off-chain data
author: Noah Litvin (@noahlitvin), db (@dbeal-eth)
one_line_summary: On-demand off-chain data retrieval via oracle contracts with multicall prepending pattern.
notes: Alternative to ERC-3668. Enables cross-chain reads without callback complexity. Supports fee collection.

title: Token Converter
description: Smart-contract service that converts token of one ERC version to another
author: Dexaran (@Dexaran) <dexaran@ethereumclassic.org>
one_line_summary: Token converter enabling 1:1 conversion between ERC-20 and ERC-223 tokens with wrapper contracts.
notes: Solves token standard interoperability. One ERC-223 wrapper per ERC-20 token. Supports bidirectional conversion.

title: Tokenized Reserve
description: Transparent reserve fund on-chain with stakeholder participation.
author: Jimmy Debe (@jimstir)
one_line_summary: Tokenized reserve fund extension of ERC-4626 with stakeholder governance and transparent asset tracking.
notes: Enables proposal-based reserve withdrawals. Stakeholders participate through share minting.

title: Non-Fungible Token Roles
description: Role Management for NFTs. Enables accounts to share the utility of NFTs via expirable role assignments.
author: Ernani São Thiago (@ernanirst), Daniel Lima (@karacurt)
one_line_summary: Role management for NFTs with automatic expiration and custom data supporting permissioned utility sharing.
notes: Roles expire automatically. Supports revocable and non-revocable roles. Includes metadata extension for role descriptions.

title: Prevent ticket touting
description: An interface for customers to resell their tickets via authorized ticket resellers.
author: LeadBest Consulting Group <service@getoken.io>, Sandy Sung (@sandy-sung-lb), Mars Peng <mars.peng@getoken.io>, Taien Wang <taien.wang@getoken.io>
authors_normalized: LeadBest Consulting Group <service@getoken.io>;Sandy Sung (@sandy-sung-lb);Mars Peng <mars.peng@getoken.io>;Taien Wang <taien.wang@getoken.io>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension preventing ticket scalping through status tracking and authorized reseller transfers.
notes: Restricts transfers to authorized platforms only. Defines token statuses: Sold, Resell, Void, Redeemed.

title: Time Locks Maturity
description: Interface for conveying the date upon which a time-locked system becomes unlocked
author: Thanh Trinh (@thanhtrinh2003) <thanh@revest.finance>, Joshua Weintraub (@jhweintraub) <josh@revest.finance>, Rob Montgomery (@RobAnon) <rob@revest.finance>
one_line_summary: Standard interface for querying maturity timestamp of time-locked assets for valuation purposes.
notes: Enables Black-Scholes valuation of locked assets. Works with ERC-721 and ERC-1155 time-locked tokens.

title: Registry Extension for ERC-7579
description: Adapters that allow modular smart accounts to verify the security of modules using a Module Registry
author: Konrad Kopp (@kopy-kat), zeroknots (@zeroknots)
one_line_summary: Registry and adapter interface for modular smart accounts to verify module security via attestations.
notes: Includes singleton registry reference implementation. Supports attester thresholds and module type validation.

title: NFT Dynamic Traits
description: Extension to ERC-721 and ERC-1155 for dynamic onchain traits
author: Adam Montgomery (@montasaurus), Ryan Ghods (@ryanio), 0age (@0age), James Wenzel (@emo-eth), Stephan Min (@stephankmin)
one_line_summary: Extension for ERC-721 and ERC-1155 enabling dynamic onchain traits with marketplace sale validation.
notes: Supports multiple data types: string, decimal, boolean, epochSeconds. Includes validateOnSale property.

title: NFT Redeemables
description: Extension to ERC-721 and ERC-1155 for onchain and offchain redeemables
author: Ryan Ghods (@ryanio), 0age (@0age), Adam Montgomery (@montasaurus), Stephan Min (@stephankmin)
one_line_summary: NFT redeemable campaigns with onchain/offchain redemptions and dynamic traits.
notes: Extends ERC-721/1155 for redeemable entitlements. Integrates ERC-7496 dynamic traits. Uses EIP-712 signatures for optional redemption approval.

title: Trusted Hint Registry
description: A system for managing on-chain metadata, enabling verification of ecosystem claims.
author: Philipp Bolte (@strumswell), Dennis von der Bey (@DennisVonDerBey), Lauritz Leifermann (@lleifermann)
authors_normalized: Philipp Bolte (@strumswell);Dennis von der Bey (@DennisVonDerBey);Lauritz Leifermann (@lleifermann)
one_line_summary: On-chain metadata registry for managing ecosystem claims with delegation and EIP-712 signatures.
notes: Standardizes hint management for trustworthiness verification. Supports delegated access, EIP-712 meta-transactions. Optional ENS integration for discovery.

title: Multi-User NFT Extension
description: An extension of ERC-721 to allow multiple users to a token with restricted permissions.
author: Ming Jiang (@minkyn), Zheng Han (@hanbsd), Fan Yang (@fayang)
one_line_summary: Multi-user NFT extension allowing multiple concurrent subscriptions with per-user expiration times.
notes: Complements ERC-4907 (single rental) with support for concurrent users. Useful for IP licensing scenarios with time-limited subscriptions.

title: Dynamic On-Chain Token Attributes Repository
description: Dynamic on-chain storage of token attributes in a public-good repository.
author: Steven Pineda (@steven2308), Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
one_line_summary: Public on-chain NFT attributes repository with access control and presigned updates.
notes: Common-good repository for ERC-721/1155 attributes. Supports multiple attribute types, presigned modifications. Designed for cross-collection interactivity and perpetual on-chain storage.

title: Entity Component System
description: Represent entities comprising components of data, with systems which operate on entities' components.
author: Rickey (@HelloRickey)
one_line_summary: Minimal Entity Component System for smart contracts enabling decoupled data and behavior.
notes: Standardizes ECS pattern for smart contracts. World contracts manage entities, components, systems. Facilitates modular architecture and cross-contract applications.

title: Cross-Contract Hierarchical NFT
description: An extension of ERC-721 to maintain hierarchical relationship between tokens from different contracts.
author: Ming Jiang (@minkyn), Zheng Han (@hanbsd), Fan Yang (@fayang)
one_line_summary: Cross-contract hierarchical NFT relationships enabling multi-parent derivative tokens.
notes: Extends ERC-721 with cross-contract parent tracking. Unlike ERC-6150 (same-contract), enables derivative NFTs in new collections from multiple parents.

title: Minimal Proxy Contract with PUSH0
description: Optimizes the previous Minimal Proxy Contract with the PUSH0 opcode
author: 0xAA (@AmazingAng), vectorized (@Vectorized), 0age (@0age)
one_line_summary: Minimal proxy contract optimized with PUSH0 opcode reducing deployment and runtime gas.
notes: Improves ERC-1167 with PUSH0; 200 gas deployment savings, 5 gas runtime per call. Requires Shanghai+ EVM. Same functionality as prior minimal proxy.

title: Onchain Representation for Audits
description: Proposal to define a contract parseable representation of Audit reports.
author: Richard Meissner - Safe (@rmeissner), Robert Chen - OtterSec (@chen-robert), Matthias Egli - ChainSecurity (@MatthiasEgli), Jan Kalivoda - Ackee Blockchain (@jaczkal), Michael Lewellen - OpenZeppelin (@cylon56), Shay Zluf - Hats Finance (@shayzluf), Alex Papageorgiou - Omniscia (@alex-ppg)
authors_normalized: Richard Meissner - Safe (@rmeissner);Robert Chen - OtterSec (@chen-robert);Matthias Egli - ChainSecurity (@MatthiasEgli);Jan Kalivoda - Ackee Blockchain (@jaczkal);Michael Lewellen - OpenZeppelin (@cylon56);Shay Zluf - Hats Finance (@shayzluf);Alex Papageorgiou - Omniscia (@alex-ppg)
one_line_summary: On-chain audit report representation with EIP-712 signatures and ERC compliance verification.
notes: Standardizes audit metadata for on-chain verification. Tracks auditor, contract instance, ERC compliance. Supports SECP256K1, BLS, ERC1271, SECP256R1 signatures.

title: Smart NFT - A Component for Intent-Centric
description: This proposal defines a new type of NFT that combines smart contract execution logic, granting NFTs executable capabilities.
author: MJ Tseng (@TsengMJ) <tsngmj@gmail.com>, Clay (@Clay2018) <clay.uw@outlook.com>, Jeffery.c <jeffery.c@a3sprotocol.xyz>, Johnny.c <johnny.c@a3sprotocol.xyz>
one_line_summary: NFTs with embedded executable smart contract logic via intent-centric modules.
notes: Fuses NFT with smart contract execution. Smart-Manager tracks verification status. Intent-Proxy enables batch NFT usage. Based on ERC-1155 for reusability.

title: Content Consent for AI/ML Data Mining
description: A proposal adding "dataMiningPreference" in the metadata to preserve the digital content's original intent and respect creator's rights.
author: Bofu Chen (@bafu), Tammy Yang (@tammyyang)
one_line_summary: AI/ML data mining preference metadata for digital assets respecting creator rights.
notes: Extends ERC-721/7053 with dataMiningPreference field. Supports granular consent for data mining, AI training, inference. Aligns with C2PA standards.

title: Dynamic Compliant Interop Security Token
description: Security token framework with semi-fungible partitions for dynamic regulatory compliance management and cross-chain interoperability
author: Abhinav (@abhinav-d3v) <abhinav@zoniqx.com>, Prithvish Baidya (@d4mr) <pbaidya@zoniqx.com>, Rajat Kumar (@rajatwasan) <rwasan@zoniqx.com>, Prasanth Kalangi <pkalangi@zoniqx.com>
one_line_summary: Security token framework with partitions, dynamic compliance, and voucher-based transfer controls.
notes: Extends ERC-1155 with partitions for granular compliance management. Includes token locking, forced transfers, address freezing, payouts. Supports interoperability via wrapping.

title: General Intents for Smart Contract Wallets
description: A generalized intent specification for smart contract wallets, allowing authorization of current and future intent structures at sign time
author: Stephen Monn (@pixelcircuits), Bikem Bengisu (@supiket)
one_line_summary: Generalized intent framework for smart wallets enabling future-proof flexible intent standards.
notes: Entry-point contract for intent execution. Decouples verification from intent handling. MEV searchers (solvers) build IntentSolutions. Supports embedded standard optimizations.

title: OIDC ZK Verifier for AA Account
description: A ERC-4337 compatible OIDC ZK Verifier
author: Shu Dong (@dongshu2013) <shu@hexlink.io>, Yudao Yan <dean@dauth.network>, Song Z <s@misfit.id>, Kai Chen <kai@dauth.network>
one_line_summary: ERC-4337 compatible OIDC ZK verifier enabling social login for smart accounts.
notes: Links OIDC identity to ERC-4337 accounts via ZK proofs. Avoids MPC centralization. Supports aggregated proofs for multiple operations.

title: PLUME Signature in Wallets
description: A new signature scheme for Ethereum keypairs that allows for "nullifiers" to enable unique anonymity and zk voting.
author: Yush G (@Divide-By-0) <aayushg@mit.edu>, Kobi Gurkan (@kobigurk), Richard Liu (@rrrliu), Vivek Bhupatiraju (@vb7401), Barry Whitehat (@barryWhiteHat)
authors_normalized: Yush G (@Divide-By-0) <aayushg@mit.edu>;Kobi Gurkan (@kobigurk);Richard Liu (@rrrliu);Vivek Bhupatiraju (@vb7401);Barry Whitehat (@barryWhiteHat)
one_line_summary: PLUME deterministic signature scheme producing verifiable nullifiers for anonymous uniqueness.
notes: Verifiably deterministic ECDSA-based signatures enabling nullifiers without exporting private keys. Supports ZK voting, sybil resistance, pseudonymous airdrop uniqueness.

title: Token Bound Function Oracle AMM
description: Interfaces that wrap FT to NFT and unwrap NFT to FT based on an embedded Function Oracle AMM
author: Elaine Zhang (@lanyinzly) <lz8aj@virginia.edu>, Jerry <jerrymindflow@gmail.com>, Amandafanny <amandafanny200@gmail.com>, Shouhao Wong (@wangshouh) <wongshouhao@outlook.com>, 0xPoet <0xpoets@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Wrap/unwrap ERC-20 or ETH to/from ERC-721 with embedded Function Oracle AMM pricing.
notes: Agency/App architecture with FOAMM pricing. Supports custom wrap/unwrap fees. Enables liquidity via internal mint/burn rather than external LPs.

title: ETH (Native Asset) Address Convention
description: An address placeholder for ETH when used in the same context as an ERC-20 token.
author: Joey Santoro (@joeysantoro)
one_line_summary: Address convention for representing ETH in ERC-20 token contexts using 0xeeee...eeee.
notes: Complements ERC-4626 and ERC-20 by standardizing ETH representation; used extensively by liquid staking protocols.

title: Contract Discovery and eTLD+1 Association
description: Leveraging TXT Records to discover, verify and associate a smart contract with the owner of a DNS domain.
author: Todd Chapman (@tthebc01), Charlie Sibbach <charlie@cwsoftware.com>, Sean Sing (@seansing)
one_line_summary: Discover and verify smart contracts via DNS TXT records with eTLD+1 domain association.
notes: Enables off-chain verification of smart contract ownership using DoH; useful for anti-spoofing and contract discoverability.

title: Staked ERC-721 Ownership Recognition
description: Recognizing NFT ownership when staked into other contracts.
author: Francesco Sullo (@sullof)
one_line_summary: Recognize NFT ownership when staked in pools via RightsHolderChange events.
notes: Solves legacy NFT staking challenges by tracking actual owners separately from technical holders; supports flexible rights types.

title: Public Cross Port
description: Help bridges to connect all EVM chains
author: George (@JXRow), Zisu (@lazy1523)
one_line_summary: Public Cross Port for connecting EVM chains via pull-based cross-chain messaging with MerkleTree packing.
notes: Reduces cross-chain bridges from O(n^2) to O(n); enables redundant bridge verification for enhanced security.

title: Native Asset ERC-4626 Tokenized Vault
description: ERC-4626 Tokenized Vaults with Ether (Native Asset) as the underlying asset
author: Joey Santoro (@joeysantoro)
one_line_summary: ERC-4626 tokenized vaults with native ETH as underlying asset instead of ERC-20.
notes: Extends ERC-4626 specifically for ETH and native assets; primary use case is liquid staking tokens.

title: Multiplicative Tokens
description: Incorporates a multiplier field to ERC-20 and ERC-1155 for fractional token values
author: Gavin John (@Pandapip1)
one_line_summary: Add multiplier field to ERC-20 and ERC-1155 metadata for fractional token display.
notes: Enables fractional/deflation mechanisms without standard decimals; uses ERC-1046 tokenURI for metadata.

title: Asynchronous ERC-4626 Tokenized Vaults
description: Extension of ERC-4626 with asynchronous deposit and redemption support
author: Jeroen Offerijns (@hieronx), Alina Sinelnikova (@ilinzweilin), Vikram Arun (@vikramarun), Joey Santoro (@joeysantoro), Farhaan Ali (@0xfarhaan), João Martins (@0xTimepunk)
authors_normalized: Jeroen Offerijns (@hieronx);Alina Sinelnikova (@ilinzweilin);Vikram Arun (@vikramarun);Joey Santoro (@joeysantoro);Farhaan Ali (@0xfarhaan);João Martins (@0xTimepunk)
one_line_summary: Async deposit/redemption requests for ERC-4626 vaults with pending/claimable/claimed states.
notes: Extends ERC-4626 for async vaults (LSTs, RWA protocols); operators can manage requests on behalf of controllers.

title: Upgradeable Clone for Scalable Contracts
description: An upgradeable, cloneable, horizontally extensible proxy pattern.
author: Shogo Ochiai (@shogochiai) <shogo.ochiai@pm.me>, Kai Hiroi (@KaiHiroi) <kai.hiroi@pm.me>
one_line_summary: Upgradeable Clone pattern combining function-level upgradeability with factory cloneability.
notes: Separates Dictionary from Proxy for factory-friendly simultaneous upgrades; addresses ERC-2535 limitations.

title: Open IP Protocol built on NFTs
description: A protocol that enables users to remix NFTs and generate new NFT derivative works, while their relationships can be traced on chain.
author: Combo <combo@1combo.io>, Saitama (@saitama2009), CT29 <CT29@1combo.io>, Luigi <luigi@1combo.io>
one_line_summary: Protocol for remixing NFTs to create derivative works with traceable relationships on-chain.
notes: Extends ERC-721 with remix, license, and network modules; tracks IP lineage through singleton network module.

title: Single Sign-On for Account Discovery
description: Discover accounts using a signing key that do not use the secp256k1 curve.
author: Alexander Müller (@alexmmueller), Gregory Markou (@GregTheGreek), Willem Olding (@Wollum), Belma Gutlic (@morrigan), Marin Petrunić (@mpetrunic), Pedro Gomes (@pedrouid)
authors_normalized: Alexander Müller (@alexmmueller);Gregory Markou (@GregTheGreek);Willem Olding (@Wollum);Belma Gutlic (@morrigan);Marin Petrunić (@mpetrunic);Pedro Gomes (@pedrouid)
one_line_summary: Single sign-on for discovering accounts using non-secp256k1 signing keys (passkeys, etc).
notes: Solves account fragmentation for ERC-4337 with alternative signers; redirect-based SSO pattern for key discovery.

title: Simple NFT, Simplified ERC-721
description: Designed for contract wallet, removes safeTransferFrom, approve, setApprovalForAll, getApproved, isApprovedForAll functions from ERC-721
author: Xiang (@wenzhenxiang), Ben77 (@ben2077), Mingshi S. (@newnewsms)
one_line_summary: Simplified ERC-721 for smart contract wallets; removes approve/safeTransferFrom functions.
notes: Forward-compatible simplification designed for contract wallets; shifts approval to wallet level instead of NFT contract.

title: Account Abstraction Validation Scope Rules
description: A set of limitations on validation EVM code to protect Account Abstraction nodes from denial-of-service attacks through unpaid computation.
author: Yoav Weiss (@yoavw), Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh), Alex Forshtat (@forshtat), Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn)
one_line_summary: Validation scope rules for ERC-4337 account abstraction to prevent DoS via unpaid computation.
notes: Defines opcode restrictions, storage isolation, reputation system for bundlers; critical mempool security rules.

title: Contract wallet management NFT
description: Focuses on NFT management within smart contract wallets, offering enhanced transaction flexibility and security
author: Xiang (@wenzhenxiang), Ben77 (@ben2077), Mingshi S. (@newnewsms)
one_line_summary: Smart contract wallet interface for managing NFT approvals and transfers with granular controls.
notes: Shifts NFT approval control to wallet level with support for batch operations and custom hooks.

title: Perpetual Contract NFTs as Collateral
description: Lock financial assets as NFTs and use them as collateral for borrowing funds in DeFi, facilitating liquidity provision.
author: Hyoungsung Kim (@HyoungsungKim) <hyougnsung@keti.re.kr>, Yong-Suk Park <yspark@keti.re.kr>, Hyun-Sik Kim <hskim@keti.re.kr>
one_line_summary: Perpetual contract NFTs representing locked assets that can be collateralized for loans.
notes: Enables DeFi composability by allowing LP/staking NFTs to be used as collateral; integrates with ERC-4907.

title: Multiplayer Game Communication
description: A communication channel that facilitates interaction between players in on-chain games.
author: Rickey (@HelloRickey)
one_line_summary: Multiplayer game communication protocol using rooms for player grouping and messages for game actions.
notes: Room-based architecture with message relay; designed for turn-based/strategy on-chain games rather than real-time.

title: Contract-level metadata via `contractURI()`
description: Specifying and updating contract-level metadata
author: Devin Finzer (@dfinzer), Alex Atallah (@alexanderatallah), Ryan Ghods (@ryanio)
one_line_summary: Standard interface for contract-level metadata via contractURI() function.
notes: Provides contract-level metadata discovery mechanism for dapps and indexers; enables rich information display without manual per-dapp configuration.

title: Conditional-upon-Transfer-Decryption for DvP
description: A Protocol for Secure Delivery-versus-Payment across two Blockchains
author: Christian Fries (@cfries), Peter Kohl-Landgraf (@pekola)
one_line_summary: Protocol for secure delivery-versus-payment across two blockchains using cryptographic locking and decryption.
notes: Cross-chain DvP using hash-linked contracts and decryption oracles; enables atomic asset-payment exchanges between chains without intermediaries.

title: Authentication SBT using Credential
description: Verifying a user's decentralized identifier (DID)/credential and issuing soul-bound token (SBT)
author: Geunyoung Kim (@c1ick), JaeCheol Ryou <jcryou@home.cnu.ac.kr>
one_line_summary: Mechanism for issuing non-transferable SBTs as proof of credential verification using zero-knowledge proofs.
notes: Extends ERC-721 with credential attestation; SBTs non-transferable per ERC-5192; privacy-preserving on-chain authentication without exposing credential data.

title: Multi-Asset ERC-4626 Vaults
description: Extended ERC-4626 Interface enabling Multi-Asset Vaults
author: Jeroen Offerijns (@hieronx), Alina Sinelnikova (@ilinzweilin), Vikram Arun (@vikramarun), Joey Santoro (@joeysantoro), Farhaan Ali (@0xfarhaan)
authors_normalized: Jeroen Offerijns (@hieronx);Alina Sinelnikova (@ilinzweilin);Vikram Arun (@vikramarun);Joey Santoro (@joeysantoro);Farhaan Ali (@0xfarhaan)
one_line_summary: Multi-asset vault extension to ERC-4626 enabling multiple assets/entry points with single share token.
notes: Extends ERC-4626 for multi-asset vaults and pipes; externalizes ERC-20 dependency; optionally includes vault-to-share lookup via ERC-165.

title: Physical Asset Redemption
description: Provides the holder of physical asset backed NFTs readily available real-world information on the underlying physical asset.
author: Lee Vidor (@V1d0r), David Tan <david@emergentx.org>, Lee Smith <lee@emergentx.org>, Gabriel Stoica (@gabrielstoica)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension embedding physical asset metadata for redemption, authenticity, and legal framework transparency.
notes: Captures issuer, asset holder, storage location, terms, jurisdiction, and declared value; ensures NFT holder can assess redemption risks without legal review.

title: Minimal Modular Smart Accounts
description: Modular smart account interfaces and behavior for interoperability with minimal restrictions for accounts and modules
author: zeroknots (@zeroknots), Konrad Kopp (@kopy-kat), Taek Lee (@leekt), Fil Makarov (@filmakarov), Elim Poon (@yaonam), Lyu Min (@rockmin216)
authors_normalized: zeroknots (@zeroknots);Konrad Kopp (@kopy-kat);Taek Lee (@leekt);Fil Makarov (@filmakarov);Elim Poon (@yaonam);Lyu Min (@rockmin216)
one_line_summary: Minimal standard interfaces for modular smart accounts and modules ensuring interoperability across implementations.
notes: Defines execution, config, fallback, and module interfaces for ERC-4337 accounts; supports validators, executors, fallback handlers, and hooks with minimal constraints on account innovation.

title: Advertisement Tracking Interface
description: Interfaces for advertisement clients tracking user actions and checking proportional rewards.
author: wart (@wartstone)
one_line_summary: Standard interface for tracking user actions across dapps to enable advertisement protocols and reward distribution.
notes: Enables inter-dapp tracking via onTrackStart/End events; projects can fund users for specific actions while dapps receive proportional rewards.

title: Modular Accounts with Delegated Validation
description: Extends ERC-4337 interface with nonce-based plugins
author: Shivanshi Tyagi (@nerderlyne), Ross Campbell (@z0r0z)
one_line_summary: Minimalist extension to ERC-4337 enabling nonce-based plugin validation for modular smart accounts.
notes: Encodes validator identifier in high 192 bits of nonce; minimal footprint—no new functions required; leverages existing EntryPoint accounting and UserOperationEvent.

title: MixHash and Public Data Storage Proofs
description: A design for minimum value selection Storage Proofs on Merkle trees
author: Liu Zhicong (@waterflier), William Entriken (@fulldecent), Wei Qiushi (@weiqiushi), Si Changjun (@photosssa)
one_line_summary: MixHash algorithm and public data storage proofs for verifying possession of public data on Merkle trees.
notes: Combines hashing algorithm selection and file size in 256-bit MixHash; enables game-theoretic storage proof validation without on-chain encryption; defends against sourcing attacks.

title: Interest Rate Swaps
description: Interest rate swaps derivative contracts
author: Samuel Gwlanold Edoumou (@Edoumou)
one_line_summary: Standardized framework for on-chain interest rate swaps enabling fixed/floating rate cash flow exchange.
notes: ERC-20 compliant; supports configurable rates, payment frequencies, benchmarks, and oracles; tokenizes swap cash flows; includes termination mechanics.

title: Blob Transactions Metadata JSON Schema
description: Attaching metadata to blobs carried by blob transactions
author: Gavin Fu (@gavfu), Leo Wang (@wanglie1986), Bova Chen (@appoipp), Aiden X (@4ever9)
one_line_summary: Standard JSON schema for attaching metadata to blobs in EIP-4844 blob transactions.
notes: Metadata includes originator, description, content type, extras; optional per-blob metadata overlay; enhances auditability and enables novel blob-based applications.

title: Semi-Fungible Token Roles
description: Role Management for Semi-Fungible Tokens (SFTs). Enables accounts to share the utility of SFTs via expirable role assignments.
author: Ernani São Thiago (@ernanirst), Daniel Lima (@karacurt)
one_line_summary: Role management system for SFTs enabling expirable, revocable utility delegation via role assignments.
notes: Roles expressed as bytes32 with arbitrary _data; supports token commitment abstraction; optional single-tx and role-balance extensions; works with immutable SFTs via external contract.

title: ERC-20 Holder Extension for NFTs
description: Extension to allow NFTs to receive and transfer ERC-20 tokens.
author: Steven Pineda (@steven2308), Jan Turk (@ThunderDeliverer)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension enabling NFTs to hold and transfer ERC-20 tokens with nonce-based front-running protection.
notes: Pull mechanism avoids ERC-20 callback limitation; includes transfer-out nonce to prevent front-running; balanceOfERC20 and transfer methods per token.

title: Collateralized NFT
description: ERC-721 Extension to enable collateralization with ERC-20 based tokens.
author: 571nKY (@571nKY), Cosmos (@Cosmos4k), f4t50 (@f4t50), Harpocrates (@harpocrates555)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension enabling NFT collateralization with ERC-20 tokens for on-chain price floor and sustainable royalties.
notes: Supports multiple collateral tokens per NFT; includes harvest (commission exchange), disperse (collective distribution), and VRF preset; EnviousHouse abstraction layer for deployed collections.

title: Signature Validation Extension for Permit
description: An ERC to extend ERC-2612 Permit to support ERC-1271-based signature validation.
author: Yvonne Zhang (@yvonnezhangc), Aloysius Chan (@circle-aloychan)
one_line_summary: Extension to ERC-2612 Permit supporting ERC-1271 contract signature validation for smart contract wallets.
notes: Unifies signature validation for EOAs (secp256k1) and contracts (isValidSignature); enables gasless ERC-20 approvals from smart contract wallets; backward-compatible.

title: Use contract signature for signed transfer
description: An ERC to extend ERC-3009 Transfer with Authorization to support ERC-1271 signature validation.
author: Yvonne Zhang (@yvonnezhangc), Aloysius Chan (@circle-aloychan)
one_line_summary: Extends ERC-3009 transfers with ERC-1271 contract wallet signature validation.
notes: Enhancement allowing smart contract wallets to authorize transfers via ERC-1271 signatures alongside ECDSA.

title: ERC-1155 Multi-Asset extension
description: An interface compatible with ERC-1155 for Multi-Asset tokens with context-dependent asset type output control.
author: Haru (@haruu8)
one_line_summary: ERC-1155 extension enabling context-dependent multi-asset output per token.
notes: Allows tokens to expose multiple representations based on context (e.g., different media formats for different consumers).

title: ERC-1155 Permit Approvals
description: Permit approvals for ERC-1155 tokens
author: calvbore (@calvbore), emiliolanzalaco (@emiliolanzalaco)
one_line_summary: Permit approval flow for ERC-1155 tokens with EIP-712 signatures.
notes: Extends ERC-2612/ERC-4494 permit pattern to ERC-1155 multi-asset context. Requires ERC-5216 for amount-based approvals.

title: Puppet Proxy Contract
description: A proxy that, if called by its deployer, delegates to an implementation specified in calldata.
author: Igor Żuk (@CodeSandwich)
one_line_summary: A stateless proxy contract that delegates calls only from its deployer.
notes: Enables deterministic account addresses via CREATE2, alternative to clone/beacon proxies with minimal gas and bytecode.

title: Atomic Push-based Data Feed Among Contracts
description: An Atomic Mechanism to Allow Publisher Contract Push Data to Subcriber Contracts
author: Elaine Zhang (@lanyinzly) <lz8aj@virginia.edu>, Jerry <jerrymindflow@gmail.com>, Amandafanny <amandafanny200@gmail.com>, Shouhao Wong (@wangshouh) <wongshouhao@outlook.com>, Doris Che (@Cheyukj) <dorischeyy@gmail.com>, Henry Yuan (@onehumanbeing) <hy2878@nyu.edu>
one_line_summary: Push-based data feed mechanism for atomic publisher-to-subscriber contract updates.
notes: Allows publisher contracts to push data to subscriber contracts with conditional and unconditional subscription modes.

title: Chunk support for ERC-5219 mode in Web3 URL
description: In ERC-5219 resolve mode, add ability to fetch a Web3 URL resource made of several chunks
author: Qi Zhou (@qizhou), Nicolas Deschildre (@nand2)
one_line_summary: Chunk support for ERC-5219 Web3 URL resolve mode to fetch large resources.
notes: Extends Web3 URL standard with chunked fetching via web3-next-chunk header to work around gas limits.

title: Content encoding in ERC-5219 mode Web3 URL
description: In ERC-5219 resolve mode, add ability to decode a compressed resource before serving it to the client
author: Qi Zhou (@qizhou), Nicolas Deschildre (@nand2)
one_line_summary: Content encoding decompression for Web3 URL resolve mode.
notes: Supports gzip and brotli decompression for compressed resources in Web3 URL protocol.

title: Basket Token
description: A multi-asset basket token with weighted constituents and owner-managed rebalancing.
author: Dominic Ryder <dom@alvara.xyz>, Michael Ryder <mike@alvara.xyz>, Callum Mitchell-Clark (@AlvaraProtocol) <cal@alvara.xyz>
one_line_summary: Multi-asset basket token with weighted constituents and owner-managed rebalancing.
notes: Standardizes manager-rebalanced weighted baskets; basket contract is ERC-20 share token. Distinct from ERC-4626/ERC-7575.

title: Secure Messaging Protocol
description: End-to-end encryption for sending messages between users.
author: Chen Liaoyuan (@chenly) <cly@kip.pro>
one_line_summary: End-to-end encrypted messaging protocol for on-chain user communication.
notes: Enables registered public key encryption for private messages; supports multiple algorithms (ECDSA, ED25519, X25519).

title: ERC-721 Ownership Shares Extension
description: Introduces ownership shares to ERC-721 tokens, allowing for queryable, transferable, and approvable fractional ownership.
author: Chen Liaoyuan (@chenly) <cly@kip.pro>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension adding queryable, transferable ownership shares per token.
notes: Bridges ERC-721 uniqueness with ERC-1155-like share mechanics for dividend/rights distribution.

title: ERC-20/ERC-721 Unified Token Interface
description: introduces a single interface for ERC-20/ERC-721 tokens, enabling seamless interaction by defining common functions for both token types.
author: 0xZeus1111 (@0xZeus1111), Nvuwa (@Nvuwa)
one_line_summary: Unified interface for ERC-20 and ERC-721 tokens with mode conversion support.
notes: Proposes single interface and state transitions between fungible and non-fungible modes; addresses fragmentation.

title: Dual Nature Token Pair
description: A specification for a co-joined fungible and non-fungible token pair
author: vectorized (@vectorized), Thomas (@0xth0mas), Quit (@quitcrypto), Michael Amadi (@AmadiMichael), cygaar (@cygaar), Harrison (@pop-punk)
one_line_summary: Linked ERC-20 and ERC-721 contracts with optional per-account NFT skipping.
notes: Enables dual-nature token pairs; ERC-20 base syncs with ERC-721 mirror. Skip NFT feature prevents O(n) gas blowup.

title: Interfaces for Named Token
description: Enable tokens to have a string name and be able to convert between name and id.
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: Adds string name support and bidirectional token-name mapping for NFTs.
notes: Allows tokens to be identified by human-readable names; supports both custom and default keccak256-based mapping.

title: Limited Transfer Count NFT
description: An ERC-721 extension that caps how many times an individual token can be transferred
author: Qin Wang (@qinwang-git), Saber Yu (@OniReimu), Shiping Chen <shiping.chen@data61.csiro.au>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension capping the number of times a token can be transferred.
notes: Enforces per-token transfer limits; does not count mint/burn. Enables use cases like wear-out mechanics and value preservation.

title: Batch Calls Encoding in SCA
description: Encode multiple calls for smart contract account (SCA) with atomic and saving gas
author: George (@JXRow), Zisu (@lazy1523)
one_line_summary: Batch calls encoding for smart contract accounts enabling atomic multi-call execution.
notes: Compresses multiple calls (to, value, data) into bytes for gas efficiency and atomic bundler-paid transactions.

title: Intrinsic RevShare Token
