description: An ERC-20 extension that integrates a revenue-sharing mechanism, ensuring tokens intrinsically represent a share of a communal revenue pool
author: Conway (@0x1cc), Cathie So (@socathie), Xiaohang Yu (@xhyumiracle), Suning Yao (@fewwwww), Kartin <kartin@hyperoracle.io>
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension with integrated revenue-sharing and burning mechanism for token holders.
notes: Combines ERC-20 with snapshot-based revenue distribution and optional ERC-20 alt token support; burning mechanism provides intrinsic floor value.

title: ERC-721 Name Registry Extension
description: Extend a time-limited unique name to each token within ERC-721, utilizing the name registry for registration and management.
author: Chen Liaoyuan (@chenly)
one_line_summary: Extends ERC-721 with time-limited unique name registry mechanism per token.
notes: Adds naming system to NFTs with expiry dates ensuring uniqueness and preventing monopolization; compatible with ERC-721.

title: Bonding curve-embedded liquidity for NFTs
description: Embedding liquidity into Non-Fungible Tokens (NFTs) without modifications to ERC-721.
author: Arif Khan <arif@alethea.ai>, Ahmad Matyana <ahmad@alethea.ai>, Basil Gorin (@vgorin), Vijay Bhayani (@unblocktechie)
one_line_summary: Embeds bonding curve-based liquidity into NFTs via tradable shares without modifying ERC-721.
notes: Introduces TradeableShares tied to NFTs with customizable bonding curves and guaranteed creator fees; separate from NFT contract.

title: Fractionally Represented Non-Fungible Token
description: A specification for fractionally represented non-fungible tokens.
author: Acme (@0xacme), Calder (@caldereth)
one_line_summary: Standard for fractionally represented NFTs coexisting with fungible shares in unified contract.
notes: Hybrid standard combining fractional and whole token semantics; distinct events to avoid ERC-20/ERC-721 conflicts; includes optional metadata and banking interfaces.

title: ERC-721 Guarantee Extension
description: Allow NFT holders to exchange their NFTs for circulating tokens at any time
author: Liu.C.Dao (@CDao) <iunknow@163.com>, Sam <1047180870@qq.com>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension enabling guarantor role to guarantee NFT buyback at set prices.
notes: Introduces guarantee mechanism where DAOs/guarantors offer price guarantees for NFT liquidity; supports multi-guarantor scenarios and sequence establishment.

title: Request Method Types
description: Use a set of request methods to indicate the type of action to take on the contract.
author: Rickey (@HelloRickey)
one_line_summary: Standardizes request-response communication (GET, POST, PUT, OPTIONS) for contracts.
notes: Defines contract interaction protocol with self-describing parameter types; enables consistent client-contract communication across different implementations.

title: Generalized Contract-Linked Services
description: Define a factory for generic services linked to any contract type, including smart accounts and NFTs
author: Francesco Sullo (@sullof)
one_line_summary: Factory for deploying generic services linked to contracts (accounts, NFTs) with deterministic addressing.
notes: Extends token-bound accounts concept to any contract type; supports multiple linking modes via bytes12 parameter; uses ERC-1167 minimal proxy pattern.

title: AI Agent NFTs
description: A specification for NFTs that represent AI Agents.
author: Greg Marlin (@marleymarl)
one_line_summary: Standard for AI Agent NFTs with encrypted prompts accessible only to token holder.
notes: Extends ERC-721 with custom struct for AI agent metadata; recommends encrypted prompt storage with holder-specific decryption; supports dynamic variable injection in prompts.

title: Distinguishable base256emoji Addresses
description: Depict Account Addresses As A String of Emoji
author: William Morriss (@wjmelements)
one_line_summary: Base256emoji encoding for addresses to improve human-readability and reduce spoofing attacks.
notes: Replaces hexadecimal with 256-emoji mapping for addresses; squaring difficulty of spoofing addresses; addresses encoded as 20 emoji instead of 40 hex chars.

title: Temporary Approval Extension for ERC-20
description: Ephemeral ERC-20 approvals lasting a single transaction
author: Xenia Shape (@byshape), Mikhail Melnik (@ZumZoom), Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension enabling single-transaction temporary approvals via transient storage.
notes: Uses ERC-1153 TSTORE for ephemeral allowances; temporary approval consumed before persistent allowance; enables one-time approvals without leaving standing permissions.

title: Paymaster Web Service Capability
description: A way for apps to communicate with smart wallets about paymaster web services
author: Lukas Rosario (@lukasrosario), Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh), Wilson Cusack (@wilsoncusack), Kristof Gazso (@kristofgazso), Hazim Jumali (@hazim-j)
authors_normalized: Lukas Rosario (@lukasrosario);Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh);Wilson Cusack (@wilsoncusack);Kristof Gazso (@kristofgazso);Hazim Jumali (@hazim-j)
one_line_summary: Capability for ERC-4337 wallets to communicate with paymaster web services.
notes: Defines JSON-RPC paymaster API (pm_getPaymasterStubData, pm_getPaymasterData) and wallet capability; enables app-specified paymaster via wallet_sendCalls.

title: UserOperation Builder
description: Construct UserOperations without being coupled with account-specific logic.
author: Derek Chiang (@derekchiang), Garvit Khatri (@plusminushalf), Fil Makarov (@filmakarov), Kristof Gazso (@kristofgazso), Derek Rein (@arein), Tomas Rocchi (@tomiir), bumblefudge (@bumblefudge)
authors_normalized: Derek Chiang (@derekchiang);Garvit Khatri (@plusminushalf);Fil Makarov (@filmakarov);Kristof Gazso (@kristofgazso);Derek Rein (@arein);Tomas Rocchi (@tomiir);bumblefudge (@bumblefudge)
one_line_summary: Standard interface for smart accounts to expose nonce, calldata, signature encoding logic.
notes: Allows vendor-agnostic UserOp construction without account-specific SDKs; IUserOperationBuilder provides getNonce, getCallData, formatSignature functions.

title: Dual Nature Multi Token Protocol
description: Combining fungible token ERC-20 and multi-token ERC-1155
author: Sennett Lau (@sennett-lau)
one_line_summary: Dual-nature token combining ERC-20 fungible and ERC-1155 semi-fungible in single contract.
notes: Unified token sync between standards; optional skip mechanism for smart contracts to avoid ERC-1155 minting overhead; implements both interfaces with distinct events.

title: Auxiliary Funds Capability
description: A capability allowing wallets to indicate that they have access to additional funds.
author: Lukas Rosario (@lukasrosario), Wilson Cusack (@wilsoncusack), Alex Donesky (@adonesky1)
one_line_summary: EIP-5792 capability indicating wallet access to auxiliary funds beyond on-chain balance.
notes: Enables advanced wallets to signal access to off-chain funds or multi-account assets; supports optional requiredAssets metadata for cross-chain/bridge flows.

title: Cross Chain Intents
description: Programmable solvers for intent protocols.
author: Francisco Giordano (@frangio), Mark Toda (@marktoda), Matt Rice (@mrice32), Nick Pai (@nicholaspai), Alexander Lindgren (@reednaa), Mark Gretzke (@mgretzke), Chris Cashwell (@ccashwell)
authors_normalized: Francisco Giordano (@frangio);Mark Toda (@marktoda);Matt Rice (@mrice32);Nick Pai (@nicholaspai);Alexander Lindgren (@reednaa);Mark Gretzke (@mgretzke);Chris Cashwell (@ccashwell)
one_line_summary: Solver-facing interface for programmable intent protocols with resolver-based order decoding.
notes: Standardizes how protocols describe orders to solvers via IResolver; defines steps, variables, payments, assumptions; supports cross-chain via ERC-7930 interoperable addresses.

title: Solana Storage Router
description: Cross-chain storage router protocol incorporating storage router for Solana
author: Avneet Singh (@sshmatrix), 0xc0de4c0ffee (@0xc0de4c0ffee)
one_line_summary: Cross-chain storage router protocol extension for Solana blockchain storage operations.
notes: Extends EIP-7700 with Solana-specific storage routing; includes EVM-SVM type casting guidance.

title: Ownership Delegation and Context for ERC-721
description: Introduces contexts and ownership delegation for ERC-721 tokens, expanding dApps and financial use cases without transferring ownership
author: Duc Tho Tran (@ducthotran2010)
one_line_summary: Ownership delegation and context management for ERC-721 tokens without transfer.
notes: Enables delegation of temporary ownership and contextual use while preserving primary owner rights; supports rental, mortgage, BNPL use cases.

title: ERC-20 Transfer Reference Extension
description: Include a unique identifier (transfer or "payment" reference) for each ERC-20 transaction to associate transfers with orders/invoices.
author: Radek Svarz (@radeksvarz)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension adding transfer reference fields for payment reconciliation.
notes: Enables association of transactions with orders/invoices via optional reference parameter; compatible with existing ERC-20.

title: Cross-chain Storage Router Protocol
description: Provides a mechanism to replace L1 storage with L2 and databases through cross-chain routers
author: Avneet Singh (@sshmatrix), 0xc0de4c0ffee (@0xc0de4c0ffee), Nick Johnson (@arachnid), Makoto Inoue (@makoto)
one_line_summary: Cross-chain storage router protocol for L1/L2/database storage operations via revert events.
notes: CCIP-Store specification; defines StorageRoutedToL1/L2/Database routers with deterministic key generation for database storage.

title: Smart Contract Delegation
description: Interfaces for consistently delegating capabilities to other contracts or EOAs.
author: Ryan McPeck (@McOso), Dan Finlay (@DanFinlay), Rob Dawson (@rojotek), Derek Chiang (@derekchiang)
one_line_summary: Standard delegation interface for smart contracts to grant capabilities to other contracts or EOAs.
notes: Enables long-lived sessions and bounded permissions via single signature; compatible with ERC-4337 and modular smart accounts.

title: Request Permissions from Wallets
description: Adds JSON-RPC method for requesting permissions from a wallet
author: Luka Isailovic (@lukaisailovic), Derek Rein (@arein), Dan Finlay (@danfinlay), Derek Chiang (@derekchiang), Fil Makarov (@filmakarov), Pedro Gomes (@pedrouid), Conner Swenberg (@ilikesymmetry), Lukas Rosario (@lukasrosario), Idris Bowman (@V00D00-child), Jeff Smale (@jeffsmale90)
authors_normalized: Luka Isailovic (@lukaisailovic);Derek Rein (@arein);Dan Finlay (@danfinlay);Derek Chiang (@derekchiang);Fil Makarov (@filmakarov);Pedro Gomes (@pedrouid);Conner Swenberg (@ilikesymmetry);Lukas Rosario (@lukasrosario);Idris Bowman (@V00D00-child);Jeff Smale (@jeffsmale90)
one_line_summary: wallet_requestExecutionPermissions RPC method for DApps to request scoped transaction permissions.
notes: Enables permissions without repeated signatures and off-chain transaction execution; integrates with ERC-7710 delegation.

title: Deferred Token Transfer
description: Allows users to schedule ERC-20 token transfers for withdrawal at a specified future time, enabling deferred payments.
author: Chen Liaoyuan (@chenly) <cly@kip.pro>
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension for time-locked token transfers with deferred withdrawal capability.
notes: Enables vesting, escrow, and timed reward scenarios with unique deposit IDs tracking unlock times.

title: Lockable Extension for ERC-1155
description: Interface for enabling locking of ERC-1155 using locker and token id based approvals
author: Piyush Chittara (@piyush-chittara)
one_line_summary: Lockable extension for ERC-1155 enabling transfer restrictions while preserving utility.
notes: Supports NFT collateral lending, rental, BNPL without escrow transfer; lock/unlock mechanisms with locker designation.

title: Opaque Token
description: A token specification designed to enhance privacy by concealing balance information.
author: Ivica Aračić (@ivica7), Ante Bešlić (@ethSplit), Mirko Katanić (@mkatanic), SWIAT
one_line_summary: Privacy-preserving opaque token using hashed baskets to conceal on-chain balance information.
notes: Uses keccak256(salt, tokenId, value) baskets with oracle-verified reorganization; off-chain storage model for DeFi compliance.

title: Common Quote Oracle
description: Interface for data feeds providing the relative value of assets.
author: alcueca (@alcueca), ruvaag (@ruvaag), totomanov (@totomanov), r0ohafza (@r0ohafza)
one_line_summary: Standard oracle interface for asset value quotes using explicit token amounts.
notes: getQuote(baseAmount, base, quote) pattern preferred over price factors for security; addresses ETH/BTC/ISO4217 currencies.

title: Token with Metadata
description: An ERC-20 extension for tokens with metadata.
author: msfew (@fewwwww)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension adding metadata() URI function for token image and description.
notes: Enables memecoins and visual metadata display; backward compatible with existing ERC-20 tokens.

title: Structured Data Clear Signing Format
description: JSON format describing how to clear-sign smart contract calls and typed messages.
author: Laurent Castillo (@lcastillo-ledger), Derek Rein (@arein), Pierre Aoun (@paoun-ledger), Arik Galansky (@arikg), Bartosz Rozwarski (@llbartekll), Kaan Uzdogan (@kuzdogan), Fredrik (@Fredrik0x)
authors_normalized: Laurent Castillo (@lcastillo-ledger);Derek Rein (@arein);Pierre Aoun (@paoun-ledger);Arik Galansky (@arikg);Bartosz Rozwarski (@llbartekll);Kaan Uzdogan (@kuzdogan);Fredrik (@Fredrik0x)
one_line_summary: JSON schema for clear-signing structured data with human-readable intent and field formatting.
notes: Enables wallet clear-signing for contracts and EIP-712 messages; supports encryption schemes like FHE; addresses phishing risks.

title: Decentralized Identity Verification (DID)
description: A privacy-preserving method for decentralized identity verification, enabling secure integration of identity management in dApps.
author: Anushka Yadav (@64anushka) <64anushka@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Minimalist decentralized identity verification using cryptographic hashes for privacy.
notes: createIdentity/verifyIdentity/revokeIdentity pattern; off-chain identity details; events for transparency.

title: Permissionless Script Registry
description: Permissionless registry to fetch executable scripts for contracts
author: Victor Zhang (@zhangzhongnan928), James Brown (@JamesSmartCell)
one_line_summary: Permissionless registry for associating executable scripts with smart contracts.
notes: Allows scripts for contracts without ERC-5169 interface; multichain singleton pattern; owner scripts prioritized.

title: Readable Typed Signatures for Smart Accounts
description: A defensive rehashing scheme which prevents signature replays across smart accounts and preserves the readability of the signed contents
author: vectorized (@vectorized), Sihoon Lee (@push0ebp), Francisco Giordano (@frangio), Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx), Ernesto García (@ernestognw), Im Juno (@junomonster), howydev (@howydev), Atarpara (@Atarpara), 0xcuriousapple (@0xcuriousapple)
authors_normalized: vectorized (@vectorized);Sihoon Lee (@push0ebp);Francisco Giordano (@frangio);Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx);Ernesto García (@ernestognw);Im Juno (@junomonster);howydev (@howydev);Atarpara (@Atarpara);0xcuriousapple (@0xcuriousapple)
one_line_summary: Defensive rehashing scheme for ERC-1271 to prevent signature replay across smart accounts.
notes: TypedDataSign and PersonalSign workflows; preserves readable nested EIP-712 structures; prevents cross-account replay.

title: Authorize Operator
description: Set Operator via EIP-712 secp256k1 signatures
author: Jeroen Offerijns (@hieronx), João Martins (@0xTimepunk)
one_line_summary: EIP-712 based operator authorization with nonce management for meta-transactions.
notes: Follows EIP-2612 pattern closely but uses random bytes32 nonces instead of sequential; applies to any operator model contract including EIP-6909 and EIP-7540.

title: Multi-Owner Non-Fungible Tokens (MO-NFT)
description: Non-fungible token that supports multiple owners, allowing shared ownership and transferability among users.
author: Cheng Qian (@jamesavechives) <james.walstonn@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension enabling shared multi-owner NFTs with provider fees and ownership archiving.
notes: Deviates from ERC-721 single-owner model by disabling approvals and safe transfers to prevent unintended transfers in co-ownership scenarios; maintains ERC-721 compatibility via supportsInterface.

title: Code Index
description: Global repository of bytecode, enabling developers, auditors, and researchers to find, analyze, and reuse bytecode efficiently.
author: Tim Pechersky (@peersky) <t@peersky.xyz>
one_line_summary: Global bytecode registry enabling trustless contract discovery and verification by bytecode hash.
notes: Prevents re-indexing of same bytecode hash to mitigate collision attacks; excludes EIP-7702 delegated accounts; allows bytecode reindexing if original indexed contract self-destructed.

title: Composable Security Middleware Hooks
description: An interface for composable, runtime security checks in smart contracts.
author: Tim Pechersky (@peersky)
one_line_summary: Composable security middleware interface for before/after function call validation.
notes: Enables modular security layers independent of contract logic; layers can maintain state for complex validations like rate limiting; reverts on validation failure ensure fail-safe.

title: Decentralized Employment System
description: An employment system that records employment history.
author: James Savechives (@jamesavechives) <james.walstonn@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: On-chain employment system with Soulbound Tokens, labor contracts, escrow payments, and reputation.
notes: Uses SBTs for employment history; includes contract execution, salary escrow, dispute resolution via moderators, and review system for reputation; primarily employment/labor focused rather than token or governance.

title: Wrapping of bubbled up reverts
description: Handling bubbled up reverts using custom errors with additional context
author: Daniel Gretzke (@gretzke), Sara Reynolds (@snreynolds), Alice Henshaw (@hensha256), Marko Veniger <marko.veniger@tenderly.co>, Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx)
authors_normalized: Daniel Gretzke (@gretzke);Sara Reynolds (@snreynolds);Alice Henshaw (@hensha256);Marko Veniger <marko.veniger@tenderly.co>;Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx)
one_line_summary: Standard error wrapping using WrappedError custom error for better revert message handling.
notes: Standardizes nested bubbled-up reverts across multiple contracts; enables tools like Etherscan and Foundry to display detailed error traces; allows additional context via details bytes parameter.

title: Tamperproof Extension Wallets API (TWIST)
description: Provides a mechanism for dapps to use the extension wallets API in a tamperproof way
author: Erik Marks (@remarks), Guillaume Grosbois (@uni-guillaume)
one_line_summary: Tamperproof wallet RPC method for dapp-to-wallet communication via signed requests and DNS verification.
notes: Uses dapp DNS records and signed payloads to prevent MITM attacks on wallet interactions; requires <2 hour key caching; supports ES256 and EdDSA signature algorithms.

title: Transfer With Authorization
description: Transfer fungible assets via a signed authorization.
author: Peter Jihoon Kim (@petejkim), Kevin Britz (@kbrizzle), David Knott (@DavidLKnott), Dongri Jin (@dongri)
authors_normalized: Peter Jihoon Kim (@petejkim);Kevin Britz (@kbrizzle);David Knott (@DavidLKnott);Dongri Jin (@dongri)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 meta-transaction extension using EIP-712 signatures with random nonces for atomic transfers.
notes: Addresses EIP-2612 sequential nonce limitations by using random bytes32 nonces; enables gas delegation, batching, and recipient-triggered transfers; includes optional cancelAuthorization.

title: Minimal Upgradeable Proxies
description: Minimal upgradeable proxies with immutable arguments and support for onchain implementation queries
author: Atarpara (@Atarpara), JT Riley (@jtriley-eth), Thomas (@0xth0mas), xiaobaiskill (@xiaobaiskill), Vectorized (@Vectorized)
one_line_summary: Minimal ERC-1967 upgradeable proxy bytecode standards for transparent, UUPS, and beacon patterns.
notes: Provides minimal bytecode variants with optional immutable arguments and onchain implementation queries; deliberately omits PUSH0 for EVM compatibility; does not emit ERC-1967 events in bytecode.

title: Privileged Non-Fungible Tokens Tied To RWA
description: An interface extending ERC-721 representing real world assets that users can exercise privileges with NFTs.
author: frank (@frankmint2024) <frank@mintchain.io>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension for real-world asset NFTs enabling privilege exercise by token holders.
notes: Allows exercising real-world privileges via onchain transactions; includes optional metadata extension for privilege URIs; compatible with ERC-721 metadata and enumerable extensions.

title: Signature Aggregation for ERC-4337
description: An ERC-4337 improvement to aggregation of all UserOperation signatures in a bundle
withdrawal_reason: No production adoption of aggregateable signature schemes in contemporary AA context. Alternative will be created for Native AA aggregation in the near future.
author: Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin), Yoav Weiss (@yoavw), Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh), Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn), Alex Forshtat (@forshtat)
authors_normalized: Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin);Yoav Weiss (@yoavw);Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh);Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn);Alex Forshtat (@forshtat)
one_line_summary: ERC-4337 extension for signature aggregation enabling shared validation across bundled UserOperations.
notes: Withdrawn due to lack of production adoption of aggregatable signature schemes; defines IAggregator interface for aggregateSignatures and validateSignatures; requires staked aggregators per ERC-7562.

title: JSON-RPC API for ERC-4337
description: JSON-RPC API methods for communication between smart contract account wallets and ERC-4337 bundlers
author: Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin), Yoav Weiss (@yoavw), Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh), Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn), Alex Forshtat (@forshtat)
authors_normalized: Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin);Yoav Weiss (@yoavw);Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh);Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn);Alex Forshtat (@forshtat)
one_line_summary: JSON-RPC API specification for ERC-4337 bundlers and wallets including debug methods.
notes: Standardizes eth_sendUserOperation, eth_estimateUserOperationGas, eth_getUserOperationByHash, eth_getUserOperationReceipt, eth_supportedEntryPoints, eth_chainId methods; includes EIP-7702 authorization support.

title: Fractional Reserve Token
description: An interface for tokens that maintain a peg to an underlying token while being only partially redeemable to this token.
author: Yaron Velner (@yaronvel)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 standard for partially redeemable synthetic assets backed by collateral with fractional reserve.
notes: Tracks totalBorrowedSupply and requiredReserveRatio; supports segregated accounts excluded from reserve calculations; enables lending markets and risk curators to mint fractional reserve tokens.

title: Cache invalidation in ERC-5219 mode Web3 URL
description: Introduce a new HTTP cache control method using EVM events for invalidation
author: Nicolas Deschildre (@nand2), Sam Wilson (@SamWilsn)
one_line_summary: Cache invalidation mechanism for ERC-6944 web3:// resolve mode using EVM events and HTTP headers.
notes: Introduces evm-events Cache-Control directive and ClearPathCache event; supports wildcards in cache invalidation paths; maintains cache key-value mapping per chain/contract/pathQuery.

title: Transparent Financial Statements
description: Open Transparent Financial Data such as Revenue, Costs of Goods Sold, Operating Expenses, EBITDA and Earnings Per Share for all Investors.
author: Ignacio Ceaglio (@Nachoxt17) <ignacioceaglio@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Standard API for publishing on-chain financial statements (income statements and balance sheets).
notes: Returns financial data as structs with optional detail fields; all values denominated in specified stablecoin; supports balance sheet snapshots and income statements via timestamp parameters.

title: Governance for Human Robot Societies
description: Defines interfaces for managing the identities of humans and robots, and establishing rule sets for their interaction.
author: OpenMind, Jan Liphardt <jan@openmind.org>, Shaohong Zhong (@ShaohongZ), Boyuan Chen (@bchen-dev), Paige Xu <paige@openmind.org>, James Ball <james.ball@nethermind.io>, Thamer Dridi <thamer.dridi@nethermind.io>
authors_normalized: OpenMind;Jan Liphardt <jan@openmind.org>;Shaohong Zhong (@ShaohongZ);Boyuan Chen (@bchen-dev);Paige Xu <paige@openmind.org>;James Ball <james.ball@nethermind.io>;Thamer Dridi <thamer.dridi@nethermind.io>
one_line_summary: Governance interfaces for human-robot societies with identity and rule management.
notes: Defines IUniversalIdentity and IUniversalCharter for managing identities and rule sets for humans and robots.

title: Interoperable Delegated Accounts
description: Interface for delegated externally owned accounts to enable better redelegation between wallets.
author: David Kim (@PowerStream3604), Richard Meissner (@rmeissner), Akshay Patel (@akshay-ap), Joshua Kim (@LightningHun), Fangting (@trinity-0111), Yoav Weiss (@yoavw)
authors_normalized: David Kim (@PowerStream3604);Richard Meissner (@rmeissner);Akshay Patel (@akshay-ap);Joshua Kim (@LightningHun);Fangting (@trinity-0111);Yoav Weiss (@yoavw)
one_line_summary: Interoperable delegated accounts interface for redelegation between wallets post-EIP-7702.
notes: Addresses storage management for delegated EOAs, enabling safe migration between wallet implementations.

title: Validation Module Extension for ERC-7579
description: Introduces new smart account module types for signature validation and permissioning
author: zeroknots (@zeroknots), Konrad Kopp (@kopy-kat), Taek Lee (@leekt), Fil Makarov (@filmakarov)
one_line_summary: Validation module extensions for ERC-7579 with policy, signer, and validator types.
notes: Introduces policy, signer, stateless validator and pre-validation hook modules for composable account validation.

title: Onchain registration of chain identifiers
description: Derive chain identifiers by hashing their chain name and use ENS to map chain names to these identifiers
author: Marco Stronati (@paracetamolo), Jeff Lau (@jefflau)
one_line_summary: On-chain registration of chain identifiers using ENS and derived hashing.
notes: Proposes deriving chain identifiers by hashing chain names and using ENS for decentralized mapping.

title: Cross-Chain Messaging Gateway
description: An interface for contracts to send and receive cross-chain messages containing arbitrary data.
author: Francisco Giordano (@frangio), Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx), Ernesto García (@ernestognw), CJ Cobb (@cjcobb23), Sergey Gorbunov (@sergeynog), joxes (@Joxess)
authors_normalized: Francisco Giordano (@frangio);Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx);Ernesto García (@ernestognw);CJ Cobb (@cjcobb23);Sergey Gorbunov (@sergeynog);joxes (@Joxess)
one_line_summary: Cross-chain messaging gateway interface for standardized message sending and receiving.
notes: Unified interface for diverse cross-chain messaging protocols using Interoperable Addresses.

title: Soulbound Degradable Governance
description: DAO governance where voting power is non-transferable and decays over time without active contributions.
author: Guilherme Neves (@0xneves), Rafael Castaneda <rafaelcastaneda@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: DAO governance with soulbound, non-transferable voting power that decays over time.
notes: Implements merit-based governance by decoupling governance from economic power with decay mechanics.

title: Grant Registry
description: A cross-network registry contract to manage and track grants, enhancing transparency and  interoperability for grant programs.
author: Guilherme Neves (@0xneves)
one_line_summary: Cross-network grant registry for tracking and managing grants with lifecycle events.
notes: Standardized contract for grant registration, tracking disbursements, and milestone management.

title: Wallet Call Token Capabilities
description: Wallet Call API extension for transaction prerequisites for common token types
author: Agustín Aguilar (@agusx1211), Michael Standen (@ScreamingHawk), Peter Kieltyka (@pkieltyka), William Hua (@attente), Philippe Castonguay (@PhABC)
authors_normalized: Agustín Aguilar (@agusx1211);Michael Standen (@ScreamingHawk);Peter Kieltyka (@pkieltyka);William Hua (@attente);Philippe Castonguay (@PhABC)
one_line_summary: Wallet Call API extension for token prerequisites via wallet_sendCalls capabilities.
notes: Extends EIP-5792 with token balance, allowance and ownership capability specs for wallets.

title: Conditional send transaction RPC
description: JSON-RPC API for block builders allowing users to express preconditions for transaction inclusion
author: Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh), Yoav Weiss (@yoavw), Alex Forshtat (@forshtat), Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn)
one_line_summary: JSON-RPC method for block builders enabling preconditions for conditional transaction inclusion.
notes: Proposes eth_sendRawTransactionConditional for conditional transaction submission reducing simulation needs.

title: Token With Mint/Burn Access Across Chains
description: Allow authorized contracts to mint and burn token representations during crosschain transfers
author: skeletor (@skeletor-spaceman), parti (@0xParticle), joxes (@Joxess), ng (@0xng), agus duha (@agusduha), disco (@0xDiscotech), gotzen <gotzen@defi.sucks>, 0age <0age@uniswap.org>, Mark Tyneway <mark@oplabs.co>, Zain Bacchus <zain@oplabs.co>, Matt Solomon <msolomon@oplabs.co>, Maurelian <maurelian@protonmail.ch>, Blaine Malone (@blmalone)
authors_normalized: skeletor (@skeletor-spaceman);parti (@0xParticle);joxes (@Joxess);ng (@0xng);agus duha (@agusduha);disco (@0xDiscotech);gotzen <gotzen@defi.sucks>;0age <0age@uniswap.org>;Mark Tyneway <mark@oplabs.co>;Zain Bacchus <zain@oplabs.co>;Matt Solomon <msolomon@oplabs.co>;Maurelian <maurelian@protonmail.ch>;Blaine Malone (@blmalone)
one_line_summary: Standardized crosschain token interface with crosschainMint and crosschainBurn functions.
notes: Minimal extensible interface enabling bridges to mint/burn token representations across chains uniformly.

title: EIP-712 Extensions for Account Abstraction
description: Improvements for EIP-712 to support smart contract accounts.
author: Francisco Giordano (@frangio)
one_line_summary: EIP-712 extensions with signing domains and auth method specification for smart contracts.
notes: Improves EIP-712 signatures for SCAs by adding replay protection and authentication method coordination.

title: Minimal intent-centric EOA smart account
description: Extensible intent-centric EOA smart account interface design to support batch execution, gas sponsorship and more other functionalities.
author: hellohanchen (@hellohanchen)
one_line_summary: Intent-centric EOA smart account interface supporting batch execution and gas sponsorship.
notes: Enables EOAs to delegate code for intent-based execution via solvers with simplified architecture versus ERC-4337.

title: Wallet Asset Discovery
description: JSON-RPC method for wallets to share a user’s full asset list with Dapps, including assets not easily found through on-chain data alone
author: Luka Isailovic (@lukaisailovic), Konrad Kopp (@kopy-kat), Derek Rein (@arein), Chris Smith (@chris13524)
one_line_summary: JSON-RPC method wallet_getAssets for wallets to declare user asset lists to dapps.
notes: Enables wallets to share asset discovery with dapps including off-chain and multi-chain assets.

title: ZK Identity Registry
description: Singleton registry system for storing abstract private provable statements.
author: Artem Chystiakov (@arvolear) <artem@rarilabs.com>, Oleksandr Kurbatov <oleksandr@rarilabs.com>, Yaroslav Panasenko <yaroslav@rarilabs.com>, Michael Elliot (@michaelelliot) <mike@zkpassport.id>, Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin)
authors_normalized: Artem Chystiakov (@arvolear) <artem@rarilabs.com>;Oleksandr Kurbatov <oleksandr@rarilabs.com>;Yaroslav Panasenko <yaroslav@rarilabs.com>;Michael Elliot (@michaelelliot) <mike@zkpassport.id>;Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin)
one_line_summary: Singleton ZK registry for storing and proving private statements using sparse Merkle trees.
notes: Abstract registry system for provable statements enabling zero-knowledge proofs of private data authenticity.

title: Store, Table-Based Introspectable Storage
description: On-chain tables for automatic indexing and introspectable state
author: alvarius (@alvrs), dk1a (@dk1a), frolic (@frolic), ludens (@ludns), vdrg (@vdrg), yonada <yonada@proton.me>
one_line_summary: Table-based on-chain storage with automatic indexing via standardized events and schema.
notes: Flexible storage pattern with schema-aware tables enabling automatic off-chain indexing and introspection.

title: Expirable ERC-20
description: An ERC-20 extension for creating fungible tokens with expiration, supporting time-limited use cases.
author: sirawt (@MASDXI), ADISAKBOONMARK (@ADISAKBOONMARK)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension adding expiration mechanism for time-limited tokens.
notes: FIFO transfer order recommended. Uses epochs (block-based or time-based) for lazy expiration tracking. Consider L2 timestamp security concerns.

title: Access Control Registry
description: Registration, unregistration, role assignment, and role revocation for contracts, ensuring secure and transparent role management.
author: Shubham Khandelwal (@shubh-ta), Anushka Yadav (@anushka642000)
one_line_summary: Centralized registry for role-based access control across multiple contracts.
notes: Enables bulk role grants/revocations. Reduces redundancy vs. bespoke implementations. Admin-only state modifications.

title: Minimal Batch Executor Interface
description: A minimal batch executor interface for delegations
author: Vectorized (@Vectorized), Jake Moxey (@jxom), Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx)
one_line_summary: Minimal batch executor interface for EIP-7702 delegations and atomic call batching.
notes: Supports single batch and batch-of-batches modes with optional opData. Designed for maximal simplicity and interoperability.

title: JSON Contract with Value Version Control
description: Manage a JSON object onchain with per-key version history and a REST-like read interface.
author: lex-clinic (@lex-clinic)
one_line_summary: Smart contract interface for managing JSON objects with per-key version history.
notes: REST-like interface targeting web2 developers. Gas-heavy on-chain JSON encoding. Recommend client-side efficiency.

title: Interoperable Names
description: Defines a human readable chain-specific address format that allows for chain discovery using ENS.
author: Sam Kaufman (@SampkaML), Marco Stronati (@paracetamolo), Yuliya Alexiev (@yuliyaalexiev), Jeff Lau (@jefflau), Sam Wilson (@samwilsn), Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin), Teddy (@0xteddybear), Joxes (@Joxess), Racu (@0xRacoon), Skeletor Spaceman (@0xskeletor-spaceman), TiTi (@0xtiti), Gori (@0xGorilla), Ardy (@0xArdy), Onizuka (@onizuka-wl), Lumi (@oxlumi), Moebius (@0xmoebius), Thomas Clowes (@clowestab), Prem Makeig (@nxt3d), Mono (@0xMonoAx), Orca (@0xrcinus)
authors_normalized: Sam Kaufman (@SampkaML);Marco Stronati (@paracetamolo);Yuliya Alexiev (@yuliyaalexiev);Jeff Lau (@jefflau);Sam Wilson (@samwilsn);Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin);Teddy (@0xteddybear);Joxes (@Joxess);Racu (@0xRacoon);Skeletor Spaceman (@0xskeletor-spaceman);TiTi (@0xtiti);Gori (@0xGorilla);Ardy (@0xArdy);Onizuka (@onizuka-wl);Lumi (@oxlumi);Moebius (@0xmoebius);Thomas Clowes (@clowestab);Prem Makeig (@nxt3d);Mono (@0xMonoAx);Orca (@0xrcinus)
one_line_summary: Human-readable chain-specific addresses combining ENS with CAIP-350 identifiers.
notes: Maps chain labels via on.eth ENS namespace. Supports non-EVM chains. Optional checksums for integrity.

title: Data Asset NFT
description: Bring Data Assets—online digital products—to NFTs and ensure their integrity through a storage proof.
author: Allen Dong (@Allen2730), Lonika Zhang <lonika@memolabs.net>, Steven He <steven@memolabs.net>
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension for data asset NFTs with storage proof and multi-reader support.
notes: Uses modular data availability layer. Optimistic proof approach for gas efficiency. Supports KZG commitments.

title: Multi-Chain Addressing
description: Textual representation of an address and a chain.
author: Sam Wilson (@SamWilsn) <sam@binarycake.ca>
one_line_summary: Multi-chain addressing via chain-specific format resolved using ENS on any root chain.
notes: Modified ERC-55 checksum wrapping. Separate bridge contracts per chain. Supports arbitrary address lengths.

title: Sustainable collaborative NFT collections
description: Economically sustainable collaborative NFT collections with dynamic fees and donation-based engagement.
author: Gustavo Lobo (@gflobo)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension enabling economically sustainable collaborative collections with dynamic fees.
notes: Progressive discount cycles via logarithmic fee reduction. Donation-based engagement model. 720-hour update interval.

title: Wallet Call Preparation API
description: Adds JSON-RPC methods for requesting unsigned EIP-5792 calls based on the wallet's implementation, and then sending those calls once signed.
author: Lukas Rosario (@lukasrosario), Conner Swenberg (@ilikesymmetry), Adam Hodges (@ajhodges), Paaras Bhandari (@paarasbhandari), Jake Moxey (@jxom)
authors_normalized: Lukas Rosario (@lukasrosario);Conner Swenberg (@ilikesymmetry);Adam Hodges (@ajhodges);Paaras Bhandari (@paarasbhandari);Jake Moxey (@jxom)
one_line_summary: JSON-RPC methods for application-signed call preparation before wallet execution.
notes: Complements EIP-5792 wallet_sendCalls. Supports session keys and scoped permissions. Multiple key types (secp256k1, p256, WebAuthn).

title: Diffusive Tokens
description: A fungible token that mints new tokens on transfer, charges a per-token native fee, and enforces a capped supply.
author: Cheng Qian (@jamesavechives) <james.walstonn@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Fungible tokens that mint new tokens on transfer with per-token native currency fee.
notes: Interface-compatible with ERC-20 but behaviorally distinct. Sender balance never reduced. Max supply cap.

title: Cross-chain Message Format and Mailbox
description: A mailbox API and message format to provide a unified cross-chain developer experience
author: Ellie Davidson (@elliedavidson), Alex Xiong (@alxiong), Philippe Camacho (@philippecamacho), and Ben Fisch (@benafisch)
authors_normalized: Ellie Davidson (@elliedavidson);Alex Xiong (@alxiong);Philippe Camacho (@philippecamacho);and Ben Fisch (@benafisch)
one_line_summary: Standardized mailbox API and message format for cross-chain communication across L2s.
notes: Supports both synchronous and asynchronous messaging. VM-agnostic with bytes32 addresses. Modular settlement logic.

title: Universal Orchestrator RPC
description: A universal data model that an Orchestrator can understand to help fulfil a request for a solution from a wallet or any other system.
author: Kieran Goodary (@IAmKio), Pillar Wallet (@pillarwallet), Luke Wickens (@lbw33), Rana Khoury (@RanaBug)
one_line_summary: Universal data model for orchestrator request/response using JSON-RPC with chain abstraction.
notes: Prefers chain abstraction by default. Flexible action types (transfer, swap, call). Non-technical terminology for broader adoption.

title: Wallet Connection API
description: Adds JSON-RPC method for requesting wallet connection with modular capabilities.
author: Conner Swenberg (@ilikesymmetry), Jake Moxey (@jxom), Lukas Rosario (@lukasrosario)
one_line_summary: JSON-RPC method for wallet connection with modular capability support including SIWE.
notes: Complements EIP-5792. Enables combined connection and authentication in single step. Multiple account support.

title: Social Media NFTs
description: Create a social media post or publication in the form of an NFT.
author: Nick Juntilla (@nickjuntilla) <nick@ownerfy.com>
one_line_summary: Standardized NFT format for decentralized social media posts adapting Nostr protocol.
notes: Uses Schnorr signatures on secp256k1. Supports kind-based event types (0: metadata, 1: content, 6: repost). Compatible with ERC-721/1155.

title: Chain-Specific Payment Requests
description: A URI scheme for chain-specific payment requests.
author: Jack Chuma (@jackchuma)
one_line_summary: URI scheme for chain-specific payment requests using CAIP-10 identifiers.
notes: Format: cspr://recipient/amount/token-address. Supports native currency and ERC-20. Optional callback URLs.

title: AI Agents NFT with Private Metadata
description: AI agent NFTs, enabling secure management of private metadata with verifiable data transfer
author: Ming Wu (@sparkmiw), Jason Zeng (@zenghbo), Wei Wu (@Wilbert957), Michael Heinrich (@michaelomg)
one_line_summary: NFT standard for AI agents with private metadata and verifiable data transfer.
notes: Introduces privacy-preserving verification (TEE/ZKP) for agent metadata. Supports data cloning and authorization mechanisms. Complex cryptographic design for intellectual property protection.

title: Expirable NFTs and SBTs
description: Non-fungible (NFT) and soulbound (SBT) tokens with expiration, supporting time-limited use cases.
author: sirawt (@MASDXI), ADISAKBOONMARK (@ADISAKBOONMARK), parametprame (@parametprame), Nacharoen (@najaroen)
one_line_summary: Extends ERC-721 with expiration mechanism for time-limited NFTs and SBTs.
notes: Supports both block-based and timestamp-based expiration. Includes epoch-based extension for batch expiry management. Backward compatible with ERC-721.

title: ERC-721 Verifiable Credential Extension
description: An extension to ERC-721 that adds collection-wide verifiable credential properties, this allows native support for onchain credentials.
author: Valerio Massimo Camaiani (@vmc-crossmint)
one_line_summary: ERC-721 extension for verifiable credentials with encryption and revocation support.
notes: Enables on-chain credential management with W3C framework alignment. Supports encrypted storage and selective disclosure via ZK proofs. Credential revocation via burning.

title: Decentralised User Profiles
description: Decentralised Interoperable User Identity with segmented Avatars per DApp Profile
author: Kumar Anirudha (@anistark)
one_line_summary: Decentralized user profiles as SBTs with dApp-specific avatars and cross-chain support.
notes: Registry/resolver architecture inspired by ENS. Supports privacy controls for avatar visibility. Off-chain metadata storage (IPFS/Arweave). DIDs for global identification.

title: Wallet Signing API
description: Adds a JSON-RPC method for requesting a signature from a wallet
author: Lukas Rosario (@lukasrosario), Jake Moxey (@jxom), Cody Crozier (@wcrozier12), Conner Swenberg (@ilikesymmetry)
one_line_summary: Unified JSON-RPC method for wallet message signing supporting multiple EIP-191 versions.
notes: Consolidates personal_sign, typed data signing under single wallet_sign method. Supports EIP-5792 capabilities. Extensible for future EIP-191 versions.

title: Ethereum Network Configuration for DApps
description: EVM-compatible network configuration specification for DApps supporting any custom network features through extensions.
author: Bogdan Gusiev (@bogdan), Sergey Bomko (@aquiladev)
one_line_summary: Unified JSON configuration specification for EVM networks with extension support.
notes: Standardizes network configuration format for DApps. Includes RPC, contracts, explorers, native currency. Supports EIP-3085 alignment with extensibility via custom properties.

title: Bequeathable Contracts
description: Inheritance of tokens after the owner's death
author: Wamith Mockbill (@wamith)
one_line_summary: Inheritance mechanism for tokens via Wills and executors enabling post-mortem transfers.
notes: Mimics real-world Will execution with moratorium period safety buffer. Flexible executor model. Compatible with ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1155.

title: Operation Router
description: A protocol that enables smart contracts to redirect write operations to external systems.
author: Lucas Picollo (@pikonha), Alex Netto (@alextnetto), Nick Johnson (@arachnid)
one_line_summary: Protocol for redirecting write operations to external systems (L2, L1, databases).
notes: Enables off-chain/cross-chain operation routing. Uses view functions for determining handlers. Supports storage proofs for database authorization. EIP-712 typed signatures for security.

title: Cancelation for ERC-7540 Tokenized Vaults
description: Extension of ERC-7540 with cancelation support
author: Jeroen Offerijns (@hieronx), Vikram Arun (@vikramarun)
one_line_summary: Cancelation extension for ERC-7540 asynchronous tokenized vaults.
notes: Adds async cancelation flows with pending/claimable/claimed states. Blocks new requests during cancelation. Complements ERC-7540 vault design.

title: Crosschain Broadcaster
description: Trustless storage-based message broadcasting for rollups.
author: Henry Arneson (@godzillaba), Chris Buckland (@yahgwai)
one_line_summary: Storage-based cross-rollup messaging using state commitments and StateProver contracts.
notes: Trustless interop via storage proofs. Supports various state commitment types (block hashes, state roots). Upgradeable StateProver pattern. Receiver/Broadcaster architecture.

title: Splitting and Merging of NFTs
description: Interface for hierarchical NFTs, enabling splitting a single NFT and merging multiple NFTs
author: Nitin Bhagat (@nitin312) <bhagatnitin312@gmail.com>, JongWook Bae <bae@cwnu.ac.kr>, Su-Hyun Lee <sleepl@changwon.ac.kr>
one_line_summary: Hierarchical NFTs enabling splitting and merging with fractional ownership tracking.
notes: Extends ERC-721 and ERC-6150. Supports share redistribution and fractional ownership. Enables dynamic asset restructuring for real estate and gaming use cases.

title: DeFi Protocol Solvency Proof Mechanism
description: Verifiable solvency proofs and financial health monitoring for DeFi protocols
author: Sean Luis Guada Rodríguez (@SeanLuis) <seanluis47@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Standardized DeFi protocol solvency proof with verifiable health metrics and risk alerts.
notes: Oracle-based asset/liability tracking with solvency ratio calculation (105% min threshold). Multi-tier risk alerts (CRITICAL, HIGH_RISK, WARNING). Historical data for trend analysis. ERC-165 support.

title: API for Hierarchical Accounts
description: Adds JSON-RPC method for requesting that a universal wallet create or track another account that it owns
author: Wilson Cusack (@wilsoncusack), Jake Feldman (@jakefeldman), Montana Wong (@montycheese), Felix Zhang (@fan-zhang-sv), Jake Moxey (@jxom)
authors_normalized: Wilson Cusack (@wilsoncusack);Jake Feldman (@jakefeldman);Montana Wong (@montycheese);Felix Zhang (@fan-zhang-sv);Jake Moxey (@jxom)
one_line_summary: JSON-RPC method for wallets to create and track hierarchical smart accounts.
notes: Enables universal wallets to manage app-embedded accounts. Supports three use cases: deployed, create, undeployed. Complements EIP-5792 capabilities framework.

title: Wallet-Linked Services for Smart Accounts
description: Define a registry for modular services linked to ERC-4337 wallets.
author: Francesco Sullo (@sullof)
one_line_summary: Registry for modular services linked to ERC-4337 wallets using ERC-1167 proxies (WITHDRAWN).
notes: Creates deterministic CREATE2 addresses for wallet-linked services. ERC-1167 minimal proxies with immutable data. Note: Proposal withdrawn; functionality achievable via ERC-7656.

title: Wallet Capabilities for Account Abstraction
description: EIP-5792 Capabilities allowing dApps and wallets to exchange all AA specific UserOp fields
author: Yoav Weiss (@yoavw), Alex Forshtat (@forshtat), Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh), Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn)
one_line_summary: EIP-5792 capabilities for Account Abstraction including EIP-7702 auth, Paymaster, gas overrides.
notes: Enables dApp/wallet negotiation of AA features. Supports EIP-7702 authorization, static Paymaster config, validity time ranges, multidimensional nonce, gas param overrides.

title: HD wallet In Treasury Management
description: Hierarchical deterministic (HD) wallets for treasury systems, isolating entities via cryptographic key paths.
author: Xiaoyu Liu (@elizabethxiaoyu) <jiushi.lxy@antgroup.com>, Yuxiang Fu (@tmac4096) <kunfu.fyx@antgroup.com>, Yanyi Liang <eason.lyy@antgroup.com>, Hao Zou (@BruceZH0915) <situ.zh@antgroup.com>, Siyuan Zheng (@andrewcoder666) <zhengsiyuan.zsy@antgroup.com>, yuanshanhshan (@xunayuan) <yuanshanshan.yss@antgroup.com>
authors_normalized: Xiaoyu Liu (@elizabethxiaoyu) <jiushi.lxy@antgroup.com>;Yuxiang Fu (@tmac4096) <kunfu.fyx@antgroup.com>;Yanyi Liang <eason.lyy@antgroup.com>;Hao Zou (@BruceZH0915) <situ.zh@antgroup.com>;Siyuan Zheng (@andrewcoder666) <zhengsiyuan.zsy@antgroup.com>;yuanshanhshan (@xunayuan) <yuanshanshan.yss@antgroup.com>
one_line_summary: Hierarchical deterministic (HD) wallet derivation path standard for treasury management.
notes: Specifies BIP-44 compliance with entity/department isolation for enterprise treasury systems.

title: Signature Verifiers
description: Interface for the verification of signatures by address-less keys
author: Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx), Ernesto García (@ernestognw), Francisco Giordano (@frangio), Aryeh Greenberg (@arr00)
one_line_summary: Interface for verification of non-ethereum signatures via address-less keys.
notes: Extends ERC-1271 to support non-ethereum cryptographic curves and signature schemes (secp256r1, RSA, email ZK).

title: Composite EIP-712 Signatures
description: A scheme for signing multiple typed-data messages with a single signature
author: Sola Ogunsakin (@sola92)
one_line_summary: Single signature scheme for multiple EIP-712 typed-data messages via Merkle tree.
notes: Merkle tree approach enables batched signing with independent verifiability; addresses UX friction in multi-step approvals.

title: PermaLink Asset Bound Token
description: Asset-bound Tokens (ABT) permanently bound to another token, known as PermaLink-ABTs.
author: Mihai Onila (@MihaiORO), Nick Zeman (@NickZCZ), Narcis Cotaie (@NarcisCRO)
one_line_summary: Asset-bound tokens permanently linked to other tokens with reveal activation mechanism.
notes: Enables asset composability (digital identity, RWA, supply chain) with immutable binding; uses reveal instead of mint.

title: Interoperable Addresses
description: An extensible binary format to refer to an address specific to one chain.
author: Teddy (@0xteddybear), Joxes (@0xJoxess), Nick Johnson (@Arachnid), Francisco Giordano (@frangio), Skeletor Spaceman (@skeletor-spaceman), Racu (@0xRacoon), TiTi (@0xtiti), Gori (@0xGorilla), Ardy (@0xArdy), Onizuka (@onizuka-wl), Sam Kaufman (@SampkaML), Marco Stronati (@paracetamolo), Yuliya Alexiev (@yuliyaalexiev), Jeff Lau (@jefflau), Sam Wilson (@samwilsn), Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin), Thomas Clowes (@clowestab), Mono (@0xMonoAx), Prem Makeig (@nxt3d), Orca (@0xrcinus)
authors_normalized: Teddy (@0xteddybear);Joxes (@0xJoxess);Nick Johnson (@Arachnid);Francisco Giordano (@frangio);Skeletor Spaceman (@skeletor-spaceman);Racu (@0xRacoon);TiTi (@0xtiti);Gori (@0xGorilla);Ardy (@0xArdy);Onizuka (@onizuka-wl);Sam Kaufman (@SampkaML);Marco Stronati (@paracetamolo);Yuliya Alexiev (@yuliyaalexiev);Jeff Lau (@jefflau);Sam Wilson (@samwilsn);Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin);Thomas Clowes (@clowestab);Mono (@0xMonoAx);Prem Makeig (@nxt3d);Orca (@0xrcinus)
one_line_summary: Binary format for chain-specific addresses supporting arbitrary blockchains via CAIP-350 profiles.
notes: Compact, cross-chain address encoding; flexible for EVM and non-EVM chains; companion to human-readable ERC-7828.

title: Versioned Proxy Contract Interface
description: Multi-version proxy contracts that allow callers to select implementation
author: Raphina Liu (@Stamp9), Monica Jin (@mokita-j), Martin Monperrus (@monperrus)
one_line_summary: Versioned proxy contracts allowing callers to select implementation at call time.
notes: Maintains backward compatibility by exposing previous implementations; mitigates malicious upgrade risk.

title: uRWA - Universal Real World Asset Interface
description: Interfaces for common base tokens defining compliance checks, transfer controls, and enforcement actions for Real World Assets (RWAs).
author: Dario Lo Buglio (@xaler5), Tino Martinez Molina (@tinom9), Mihai Colceriu (@mihaic195)
authors_normalized: Dario Lo Buglio (@xaler5);Tino Martinez Molina (@tinom9);Mihai Colceriu (@mihaic195)
one_line_summary: Compliance interfaces for RWA tokens: forced transfer, freezing, and transfer authorization checks.
notes: Minimal, unopinionated standard supporting ERC-20/721/1155; separates account eligibility from transfer-level authorization.

title: Confidential Transactions Supported Token
description: Outlines a fungible token interface that hides per-account balances, boosting privacy in dApps
author: Siyuan Zheng (@andrewcoder666) <zhengsiyuan.zsy@antgroup.com>, Zhe Han (@iampkuhz) <hanzhe.hz@ant-intl.com>, Xiaoyu Liu (@elizabethxiaoyu) <jiushi.lxy@antgroup.com>, Wenwei Ma (@madyinglight) <huiwei.mww@antgroup.com>, Jun Meng Tan (@chadxeth) <junmeng.t@antgroup.com>, Yuxiang Fu (@tmac4096) <kunfu.fyx@antgroup.com>, Kecheng Gao (@thanks-v-me-50) <gaokecheng.gkc@antgroup.com>, Alwin Ng Jun Wei (@alwinngjw) <alwin.ng@antgroup.com>, Chenxin Wang (@3235773541) <wcx465603@antgroup.com>, Xiang Gao (@GaoYiRu) <gaoxiang.gao@antgroup.com>, yuanshanhshan (@xunayuan) <yuanshanshan.yss@antgroup.com>, Hao Zou (@BruceZH0915) <situ.zh@antgroup.com>, Yanyi Liang <eason.lyy@antgroup.com>, Yuehua Zhang (@astroyhzcc) <ruoying.zyh@antgroup.com>
authors_normalized: Siyuan Zheng (@andrewcoder666) <zhengsiyuan.zsy@antgroup.com>;Zhe Han (@iampkuhz) <hanzhe.hz@ant-intl.com>;Xiaoyu Liu (@elizabethxiaoyu) <jiushi.lxy@antgroup.com>;Wenwei Ma (@madyinglight) <huiwei.mww@antgroup.com>;Jun Meng Tan (@chadxeth) <junmeng.t@antgroup.com>;Yuxiang Fu (@tmac4096) <kunfu.fyx@antgroup.com>;Kecheng Gao (@thanks-v-me-50) <gaokecheng.gkc@antgroup.com>;Alwin Ng Jun Wei (@alwinngjw) <alwin.ng@antgroup.com>;Chenxin Wang (@3235773541) <wcx465603@antgroup.com>;Xiang Gao (@GaoYiRu) <gaoxiang.gao@antgroup.com>;yuanshanhshan (@xunayuan) <yuanshanshan.yss@antgroup.com>;Hao Zou (@BruceZH0915) <situ.zh@antgroup.com>;Yanyi Liang <eason.lyy@antgroup.com>;Yuehua Zhang (@astroyhzcc) <ruoying.zyh@antgroup.com>
one_line_summary: Fungible token interface hiding per-account balances for confidential transactions.
notes: Enables privacy-preserving token transfers without revealing amounts; supports custom proof systems (Zether, ZK).

title: Unidirectional Wallet Uplink aka UWULink
description: Unidirectional app to wallet communication for private construction of transactions
author: Moody Salem (@moodysalem), Tina Zheng (@tinaszheng)
one_line_summary: Unidirectional app-to-wallet protocol for batched transactions via QR/NFC without connection.
notes: Privacy-preserving one-way communication; supports static and programmable modes for deterministic call generation on-chain.

title: Account Abstraction Recovery Interface
description: Universal mechanism to recover a smart account's access via custom recovery providers.
author: Artem Chystiakov (@arvolear) <artem@rarilabs.com>
one_line_summary: Account abstraction recovery interface enabling access restoration via custom recovery providers.
notes: Flexible recovery provider aggregation; integrates ZK proofs/2FA without mandating specific recovery mechanism.

title: Encode chain id with transaction hash
description: Encode a chain ID and transaction hash into a unique string format
author: Lauri Peltonen (@microbecode)
one_line_summary: String format encoding chain ID and transaction hash for cross-chain transaction lookup.
notes: Simple, case-insensitive format: chainId:txHash:tx; enables direct blockchain explorer routing.

title: Permissionless CREATE2 Factory
description: A permissionless method and parameters for deploying a CREATE2 factory across EVM chains to a deterministic address using EIP-7702.
author: Nicholas Rodrigues Lordello (@nlordell), Richard Meissner (@rmeissner), Valentin Seehausen (@vseehausen)
one_line_summary: Permissionless, deterministic CREATE2 factory deployment across EVM chains via EIP-7702.
notes: Uses publicly-known deployer private key with EIP-7702 delegation; mitigates front-running via repeated attempts.

title: Key Hash Based Tokens
description: Extends privacy to ERC-721 and ERC-20 tokens.
author: Alex Tian (@dugubuyan), Zhixiong Pan (@nake13), Geoffrey (@stbrahms) <geoffrey@datadance.ai>, liyingxuan (@LiYingxuan) <liyingxuan@datadance.ai>
one_line_summary: Token ownership via key hashes instead of addresses for privacy and gas sponsorship.
notes: Separates ownership from transaction initiator; supports one-time key use and fresh key rotation per transfer.

title: Crosschain EIP-712 Signatures
description: Support EIP-712 signatures for crosschain account operations.
author: Ernesto García (@ernestognw)
one_line_summary: Standard EIP-712 signatures valid across multiple chains by omitting chainId from domain.
notes: Array-based encoding of chain-specific operations; enables crosschain intents, governance, and account management.

title: Proof-based Broadcast in ERC-7786 Gateways
description: Cross-chain messaging using cryptographic proofs to verify messages between blockchains.
author: Ernesto García (@ernestognw)
one_line_summary: Cryptographic proof-based cross-chain messaging attributes for ERC-7786 gateways.
notes: Trustless verification via storage/state proofs; supports multi-hop routing and granular broadcasting via ERC-7930.

title: Owner-Authorized Token Transfer Protocol
description: Enable third parties to transfer tokens authorized by a signed message.
author: Julius Lauterbach (@Julius278)
one_line_summary: Third-party authorized token transfers via signed owner approval.
notes: Two-phase proposal/approval model with expiry and fallback recovery function for owner control.

title: DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) Registry
description: A registry for DKIM public key hashes, enabling on-chain verification.
author: Mike Fu (@fumeng00mike), Matthew Yu (@0xknon), Ernesto García (@ernestognw)
one_line_summary: DKIM public key hash registry for on-chain email ownership verification.
notes: Enables email-based account abstraction via zkEmail; domain-specific key hashes stored on-chain.

title: Confidential Fungible Token
description: Confidential fungible tokens use an account-based accounting model while maintaining the confidentiality of transfer amounts and balances.
author: Aryeh Greenberg (@arr00), Ernesto García (@ernestognw), Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx), Ghazi Ben Amor (@GBAZama), Clement Danjou (@immortal-tofu), Joseph Andre Turk (@jatZama), Silas Davis (@silasdavis), Nicolas Pasquier (@npasquie)
authors_normalized: Aryeh Greenberg (@arr00);Ernesto García (@ernestognw);Hadrien Croubois (@Amxx);Ghazi Ben Amor (@GBAZama);Clement Danjou (@immortal-tofu);Joseph Andre Turk (@jatZama);Silas Davis (@silasdavis);Nicolas Pasquier (@npasquie)
one_line_summary: Privacy-preserving fungible tokens using pointer-based confidential amounts.
notes: Account-based model with pointer/commitment pattern for balance/transfer privacy; supports operator model and callbacks.

title: Gateway Attributes for Message Control
description: Gateway attributes for cancellation, timeout, retry, dependencies, and delivery control in cross-chain messaging.
author: Ernesto García (@ernestognw), Kalman Lajko (@LajkoKalman), Valera Grinenko (@0xValera)
one_line_summary: Standard attributes for cross-chain messaging gateways (cancellation, timeout, retry, dependencies).
notes: Extends ERC-7786 with message lifecycle control; pluggable attributes for delivery timing/failure handling.

title: Minimal Avatar Smart Wallet (MASW)
description: A smart‑wallet interface for EIP‑7702 account‑code delegation.
author: 0xMostafas (@MostafaS) <0xmostafas@proton.me>
one_line_summary: EIP-7702 delegate wallet with batched execution, policy modules, and fee sponsorship.
notes: Immutable bytecode for all EOAs using 7702; supports policy/recovery module hooks; gas sponsorship via ETH or ERC-20.

title: Verifiable ML Model Inference (ZKML)
description: Interfaces for registering ML model commitments and verifying zero-knowledge proofs of their inferences on-chain.
author: Aryaethn (@aryaethn)
one_line_summary: Registry and verifier interfaces for ZK-proofs of ML model inferences on-chain.
notes: Modular design: separate registry (model commitments) and verifier (proof validation); pluggable proof systems (Groth16, Plonk, STARKs).

title: Purpose-Bound ERC-20 with Conditional Unlock
description: Extends ERC-20 with programmable restrictions, enabling transfers based on multiple unlock conditions as defined in EIP-7291.
author: Anushka Yadav (@64anushka), Akash Kothawade (@akash3927), Atishek Singh (@atisheksingh)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension with multi-condition unlocking for controlled token disbursement.
notes: Generalizes ERC-7291 with composable unlock conditions (time, KYC, whitelist, etc.); enables scholarships, aid, payroll use cases.

title: Contract Feature Detection
description: Method to publish and detect contract features that lack an ERC-165 interface
author: raffy.eth (@adraffy)
one_line_summary: Feature detection method for contract capabilities lacking ERC-165 interface.
notes: Complements ERC-165 with reverse-domain-named features and keccak256-derived 4-byte selectors; used by ENS.

title: Operator contract for non delegated EOAs
description: Allowing operating batch executions on behalf of non delegated EOAs
author: Marcelo Morgado (@marcelomorgado), Manoj Patidar (@patidarmanoj10)
one_line_summary: Operator contract enabling batch calls for non-delegated EOAs via standard interface.
notes: Alternative to 7702; uses transient storage for msg.sender context; compatible with ERC-2771 _msgSender pattern.

title: Agent Coordination Framework
description: Minimal, single-chain, multi-party agent coordination using EIP-712 attestations
author: Kwame Bryan (@KBryan)
one_line_summary: Multi-party agent coordination primitive using EIP-712 attestations and intents.
notes: Single-chain intent + per-participant acceptances; canonical participant lists; lifecycle: Proposed → Ready → Executed; privacy/bonding/cross-chain as modules.

title: Simplified Payment Verification Gateway
description: Trustless singleton contract for on-chain verification of Bitcoin transactions through block headers
author: Artem Chystiakov (@arvolear), Oleh Komendant (@Hrom131)
one_line_summary: Trustless singleton SPV gateway for on-chain Bitcoin transaction verification via Merkle proofs.
notes: Permissionless block header submission; PoW-based mainchain tracking; enables Bitcoin collateral in DeFi.

title: Trustless Agents
description: Discover agents and establish trust through reputation and validation
author: Marco De Rossi (@MarcoMetaMask), Davide Crapis (@dcrapis) <davide@ethereum.org>, Jordan Ellis <jordanellis@google.com>, Erik Reppel <erik.reppel@coinbase.com>
authors_normalized: Marco De Rossi (@MarcoMetaMask);Davide Crapis (@dcrapis) <davide@ethereum.org>;Jordan Ellis <jordanellis@google.com>;Erik Reppel <erik.reppel@coinbase.com>
one_line_summary: Trustless agent discovery and trust via identity, reputation, and validation registries.
notes: ERC-721 agent identity registry with metadata; reputation scoring on-chain/off-chain; pluggable validation (stake, zkML, TEE).

title: Payout Race
description: Minimal ERC for a single-asset payout bucket that vends its entire balance for a fixed payment amount.
author: Kyle Thornton (@kyle) <kyle@cowrie.io>
one_line_summary: Single-asset payout bucket dispensing entire balance for fixed payment (Dutch auction primitive).
notes: Deterministic exchange: fills bucket of payout asset, winner pays fixed amount; analogous to Dutch auction; payment can be ETH or ERC-20.

title: Minimal Wallet-Managed Auto-Login for SIWE
description: Defines a wallet-local allowlist for automatic signing of ERC-4361 messages when simple match rules succeed.
author: Ivo Georgiev (@Ivshti), Vijay Krishnavanshi (@vijaykrishnavanshi)
one_line_summary: Wallet-managed auto-login policy for SIWE message signing with domain/chain/resource matching.
notes: Policy-based auto-signing for trusted SIWE domains; optional ERC-6492 support for hardware wallets via EIP-7702 session keys.

title: Multi-step Contract Ownership
description: Contract ownership management with multi-step ownership transfer mechanism for secure smart contract ownership
author: David Kim (@PowerStream3604)
one_line_summary: Multi-step contract ownership transfer with initiate, confirm, accept stages and time buffer.
notes: 3-step transfer (original owner initiates/confirms, new owner accepts); optional time-gated buffer for review/rollback; compatible with ERC-173.

title: Agent Council Oracles
description: Multi-agent councils to resolve decentralized information queries
author: Rohan Parikh (@phiraml), Jon Michael Ross (@jonmross)
one_line_summary: Multi-agent councils resolve queries using commit-reveal-judge flows with bonded incentives.
notes: Introduces role separation between Info Agents (submitters) and Judge Agents (aggregators). Dispute mechanism and reputation integration with ERC-8004 are optional. Core flow uses commit-reveal-judge pattern.

title: Referable NFT Royalties
description: A standalone royalty distribution for Referable NFTs, supporting multiple recipients, reference-based royalty distribution.
author: Ruiqiang Li (@richard-620) <richard.620.research@gmail.com>, Qin Wang <qin.wang@data61.csiro.au>, Shiping Chen <shiping.chen@data61.csiro.au>, Saber Yu (@OniReimu), Brian Yecies <byecies@uow.edu.au>, John Le <johnle@uow.edu.au>
authors_normalized: Ruiqiang Li (@richard-620) <richard.620.research@gmail.com>;Qin Wang <qin.wang@data61.csiro.au>;Shiping Chen <shiping.chen@data61.csiro.au>;Saber Yu (@OniReimu);Brian Yecies <byecies@uow.edu.au>;John Le <johnle@uow.edu.au>
one_line_summary: Royalty distribution for Referable NFTs supporting multiple recipients and DAG-based propagation.
notes: Standalone from ERC-2981. Includes EIP-712 signature-based configuration. Primary royalty cap of 10% (1000 bps). Reference depth limited to ≤3 to control gas costs.

title: ESG Tokenization Protocol
description: Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) asset tokenization with metadata, attestations, and lifecycle integrity.
author: Leandro Lemos (@agronetlabs) <leandro@agronet.io>
one_line_summary: Overlay interface for ESG asset tokenization with lifecycle states and attestation recording.
notes: Metadata bound via SHA3-512 digests. Post-quantum ready. Lifecycle: issued → audited → retired (irreversible). Works with ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1155.

title: Fixed-Supply Agent NFT Collections
description: Create fixed-supply collections of ERC-8004 Agent NFTs with mint number tracking
author: Prem Makeig (@nxt3d)
one_line_summary: Fixed-supply collections of Agent NFTs registered in ERC-8004 with mint number tracking.
notes: Supports multiple collections per agent via ERC-8119 parameterized keys. Collection metadata stored in agent's onchain metadata. Mint numbers 1-indexed and immutable.

title: Diamond Storage
description: Define storage locations for structs using human-readable, meaningful strings.
author: Nick Mudge (@mudgen)
one_line_summary: Standardizes diamond storage pattern using keccak256 hash of human-readable identifiers for storage locations.
notes: ASCII-restricted identifiers recommended. Simpler alternative to ERC-7201 for new projects. Formula: keccak256(identifier). Backward compatible with existing diamond storage deployments.

title: Forensic Token (Forest)
description: A DAG-based token enabling hierarchical traceability and efficient enforcement for compliant digital assets.
author: Sirawit Techavanitch (@MASDXI) <sirawit_tec@live4.utcc.ac.th>, Supache Innet <supachate_inn@utcc.ac.th>
one_line_summary: DAG-based token enabling hierarchical traceability with O(1) forensic enforcement for compliance.
notes: Inherits from ERC-1155 and ERC-5615. Soft-delete burning preserves lineage. Token Policy Enforcement (TPEn) uses bitmasks for efficient level-based freezing. Constant-time enforcement relative to DAG size.

title: Onchain Metadata for Token Registries
description: A key-value store interface that allows registries to store and retrieve arbitrary bytes as metadata directly onchain.
author: Prem Makeig (@nxt3d), Rafael Abuawad (@rafael-abuawad)
one_line_summary: Onchain key-value metadata store for ERC-721, ERC-1155, ERC-6909 tokens with optional Diamond Storage.
notes: Supports parameterized keys per ERC-8119. Optional metadata contract reference via ERC-7930 interoperable addresses. ERC-721T profile reserves keys for agent metadata.

title: Contract-Level Onchain Metadata
description: Onchain metadata for arbitrary contracts with optional Diamond Storage for predictable data storage locations.
author: Prem Makeig (@nxt3d), Rafael Abuawad (@rafael-abuawad)
one_line_summary: Contract-level onchain metadata with optional Diamond Storage for predictable storage locations.
notes: Key-value store (string→bytes). Optional metadata hooks via ERC-8121. Diamond Storage namespace: 'erc8049.contract.metadata.storage'.

title: Forkable ERC-20 Token
description: Checkpoint-based ERC-20 tokens that can be efficiently forked to distribute new tokens to existing holders.
author: Kevin (@kevzzsk), Fuxing (@fuxingloh)
one_line_summary: Checkpoint-based ERC-20 enabling efficient token forking to distribute new tokens to existing holders.
notes: Forked tokens inherit balances from checkpoint via lazy loading. Zero-gas distribution to holders without airdrops/claims. Checkpoint nonce prevents reorg/MEV vulnerabilities.

title: Scaled UI Amount Extension for ERC-20 Tokens
description: Equity Token support for Stock Splits
author: Chris Ridmann (@cridmann) <chris@superstate.co>, Daniel Gretzke (@gretzke), Gilbert Shih <chung.shih@robinhood.com>, Tino Martinez Molina (@tinom9)
authors_normalized: Chris Ridmann (@cridmann) <chris@superstate.co>;Daniel Gretzke (@gretzke);Gilbert Shih <chung.shih@robinhood.com>;Tino Martinez Molina (@tinom9)
one_line_summary: ERC-20 extension with updatable multiplier for UI amounts enabling stock splits without minting.
notes: Multiplier uses 18 decimals (1e18=1.0). Optional conversion and balances extensions. Separate UI functions preserve backward compatibility. Planned effective timestamp for delayed updates.

title: Groups - Membership Tokens
description: ERC-20 tokens representing group membership with threshold-based access control.
author: Cheng Qian (@jamesavechives) <contact@deakee.com>
one_line_summary: ERC-20 tokens representing group membership with threshold-based access control and tiered privileges.
notes: Token balance = membership level. Applications define thresholds. Optional isMember() introspection. Any ERC-20 can be a Group. No minting/burning mandate.

title: Zero Knowledge Token Wrapper
description: Enables any token to be wrapped, allowing secretly burnt tokens to be reminted — making privacy a native feature of every token.
author: Jiahui Cui (@doublespending), 0xZPL (@0xZPL)
one_line_summary: Permissionless zero-knowledge wrapper enabling privacy for existing tokens via burn-and-remint flows.
notes: Supports ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1155, ERC-6909. Relayer-enabled remint. Commitment schemes flexible (Merkle trees or accumulators). Dual workflows: ZWToken-aware and unaware.

title: Self-Describing Bytes via EIP-712 Selectors
description: Defines a convention for self-describing structured data in `bytes` parameters using 4-byte selectors derived from EIP-712 type strings.
author: Andrew Richardson (@awrichar)
one_line_summary: Convention for self-describing structured data in bytes using 4-byte EIP-712 selectors and canonical wrapper.
notes: Single-payload: selector + abi.encode(fields). Multi-payload: DataList(bytes[]) with selector 0xae74f986. Mirrors function selector pattern for compactness.

title: Zero-knowledge proof metadata
description: ZKmeta is a method to discover ZK circuit identifiers, semantic versions, verification keys, public input schemas.
author: cococay (@zwowo1997)
one_line_summary: Standardized interface for zero-knowledge proof metadata discovery (circuit ID, version, keys, schemas).
notes: Supports multiple proof systems (Groth16, Plonk, Halo2, zkSTARK, zkVM). Proof-system ID registry extensible. Event-based updates for indexers. Hash + URI pattern for integrity.

title: Dual-Mode Fungible Tokens
description: Fungible tokens supporting both transparent (ERC-20) and privacy-preserving (zk-SNARK) modes with seamless conversion
author: Rowan (@0xRowan)
one_line_summary: Dual-mode fungible tokens supporting transparent (ERC-20) and privacy (ZK-SNARK) modes with seamless conversion.
notes: For new token deployments. toPrivate/toPublic conversions bridge modes. Supply invariant: totalSupply = public + privacy supply. Permissionless deployment.

title: Privacy Token
description: An abstract privacy foundation layer that can be used in both dual-mode tokens and wrapped tokens
author: Rowan (@0xRowan)
one_line_summary: Privacy-preserving fungible token interface using zero-knowledge proofs for confidential transfers.
notes: Foundational privacy layer for wrapper protocols and dual-mode tokens; defines IZRC20 interface with commitment-based transfers.

title: Associated Accounts
description: A way to publicly associate two accounts with arbitrary contextual data
author: Steve Katzman (@stevieraykatz), Amie Corso (@amiecorso), Stephan Cilliers (@stephancill)
one_line_summary: Standard for establishing and verifying associations between blockchain accounts with signature support.
notes: Uses EIP-712 for signatures, supports EIP-1271 contract validation; enables sub-account identity inheritance and delegation.

title: Representable Contract State
description: Interface and XML binding schema for contracts that expose a canonical representation of their state.
author: Christian Fries (@cfries), Peter Kohl-Landgraf (@pekola), Raphael Prandtl (@RKP1101)
one_line_summary: XML template interface for contracts to expose canonical state representation without gas cost.
notes: Off-chain rendering via XML bindings; supports versioned and hashed state extensions; includes optional partial view interface.

title: RWA Event-based Compliance Framework
description: Event-driven compliance framework for Real World Asset tokens with entity classification and audit events
author: Andrew Wang (@wz14) <zhuowangy2k@outlook.com>, Jack Yin (@0xjackey) <0xjackey@gmail.com>
one_line_summary: Event-driven compliance framework for RWA tokens with entity classification and audit events.
notes: Distinguishes Compliance Entities from Decentralized Entities; soft policy flags via events without enforcement reverts.

title: ENS Trust Registry for Agent Coordination
description: Web of trust validation using ENS names for ERC-8001 multi-party coordination
author: Kwame Bryan (@KBryan)
one_line_summary: Web of trust validation registry using ENS names for multi-party agent coordination.
notes: GnuPG-style trust model; four trust levels; integrates with ERC-8001 coordination hooks; requires EIP-712 signatures.

title: Diamonds, Simplified
description: A simplified diamond architecture for modular smart contract systems.
author: Nick Mudge (@mudgen)
one_line_summary: Simplified diamond proxy architecture with standardized introspection functions and per-function events.
notes: Withdrawal reason: Superseded by EIP-8153; refinement of ERC-2535 with simpler terminology and events.

title: Domain Architecture for Diamonds
description: An architectural pattern that organizes Diamond storage by domain using ERC-8042 identifiers.
author: Hoang (@0x76agabond)
one_line_summary: Domain architecture pattern for Diamond storage organization using ERC-8042 identifiers.
notes: Separates storage ownership from facet logic; defines domain naming conventions and upgrade scenarios; fully backward-compatible.

title: Bound Signatures
description: Binding y-parity compresses ECDSA signatures
author: William Morriss (@wjmelements)
one_line_summary: Compressed ECDSA signatures binding y-parity to reduce size and eliminate malleability.
notes: Reduces 96-byte signatures to 64 bytes; restricts v to 27 for non-malleable signatures; valid with ecrecover.

title: Series Accounting for Incentivized Vaults
description: Series Accounting method for collecting performance fees in ERC-7540 type vaults.
author: Yash Saraswat (@0xpanicError)
one_line_summary: Series Accounting method for collecting performance fees in ERC-7540 async vaults.
notes: Solves free-rider problem in yield vaults; maintains independent series with unique highwater marks; consolidation mechanism.

title: Anti-Poisoning Compact EVM Address Format
description: Subscript (0x0₈) and ASCII (0x0(8)) notation for leading-zero EVM addresses to prevent address-poisoning by surfacing the unique suffix.
author: Zainan Victor Zhou (@xinbenlv)
one_line_summary: Display format for addresses with leading zeros using subscript or parenthesis notation to prevent poisoning.
notes: Subscript (0x0₈) for UI, parenthesis (0x0(8)) for ASCII; threshold n≥4; improves security of gas-optimized addresses.

title: Parameterized Storage Keys
description: A format for parameterized string keys used in EVM key-value storage.
author: Prem Makeig (@nxt3d)
one_line_summary: Standard formats for parameterized string keys in EVM key-value storage.
notes: Supports slash/colon form (single param) and bracket form (multiple params); ASCII label restrictions; UTF-8 parameters.

title: Cross-Chain Function Calls via Hooks
description: A specification for cross-chain function calls using hooks with ERC-7930 interoperable addresses.
author: Prem Makeig (@nxt3d)
one_line_summary: Hooks for cross-chain function calls with ERC-7930 addresses enabling ERC-3668 resolution.
notes: Fully specifies function calls across chains; includes optional selector for type safety; supports read and write operations.

title: Minimal Agent Registry

author: Prem Makeig (@nxt3d)

notes: Agents as tokens with single owners; onchain metadata via ERC-8048; supports custom deployments beyond singleton models.

title: AI Agent Verification

author: Leigh Cronian (@cybercentry) <leigh.cronian@cybercentry.co.uk>, Chris Johnson <chris@virtuals.io>

notes: Privacy-first via ZKPs; 0-100 risk score; integrates with ERC-8004; optional quantum cryptography support (QCV).

title: Human Readable Token Identifiers

author: Prem Makeig (@nxt3d)

notes: Format: [alias.]token-id@registry; enables usable naming for NFTs, RWAs, agents; portable across systems.

title: Content-Addressable Logic Modules (CALM)

author: Radek Svarz (@radeksvarz), Nick Mudge (@mudgen), William Morriss (@wjmelements)
one_line_summary: Content-addressable logic modules for deterministic proxy architecture deployment across chains.
notes: Focuses on deployment and bytecode identity, not token or account functionality. Infrastructure-meta for cross-chain logic reuse.

title: Facet-Based Diamonds
description: Simplifies diamond management, deployment and upgrades.
author: Nick Mudge (@mudgen)

description: Proxy-level function dispatch via delegatecall

notes: Generic proxy architecture beyond diamonds; cross-cutting infrastructure pattern.

author: Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin), Skeletor Spaceman (@skeletor-spaceman)

title: Blob Authenticated Messaging

author: Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin), Skeletor Spaceman (@skeletor-spaceman), Orca (@0xrcinus)
